At approximately 02:25, resident CP367 entered the washroom located next to the lobby. After about 15 minutes, when the resident had not exited, TL Peter instructed S/O Jehangil to check on CP367. S/O Jehangil knocked on the door, and CP367 responded that he was okay. S/O Jehangil then reported back that everything appeared to be fine. Approximately five minutes later, FC Iman was radioed and informed by TL Peter that the resident had been in the washroom for an extended period of time. At approximately 03:00, CP367 exited the washroom and left the building. While exiting the building, CP367 appeared to have a rectangular-shaped object concealed beneath his hoodie. TL Peter and FC Iman then conducted a check of the washroom. During the inspection, a clear plastic syringe-like object was observed inside the toilet bowl. A white substance was also noted around the toilet seat and on the floor. TL Peter and FC Iman took photographs for documentation purposes.

Security Officer Nibir started his shift at Metrobus for the period of 1445–2230 hrs. At approximately 1635 hrs, S/O Nibir dropped S/O Karamtej off at the Village Mall to catch her bus. S/O Nibir was waiting for a vehicle in front of him that was attempting to park in one of the spots. S/O Nibir was unable to move the vehicle as there were vehicles both in front and behind waiting for parking.  Suddenly, from the driver’s side, a white SUV (Honda CR-V), plate number JPX 977, began reversing into the ISS vehicle and struck the right side of the vehicle. The driver, identified as George Justin Powell, 34 years old, exited his vehicle, apologized, and informed S/O Nibir that he did not see the ISS vehicle behind him.  S/O Nibir then inspected the ISS vehicle and observed a large dent on the right passenger side of the car. The male driver provided his driver’s licence, insurance slip, and contact information. S/O Nibir then contacted Nora Murray and relayed all the relevant information.

He advised subject returned and requested an paramedics assistance as he have a severe pain in stomach. S/G Manav advised to call non-emergency paramedics, writer was also on route.  At approximately 0303 hours, the writer arrived on site and observed a male individual wearing a blue jacket, black pants, have a hospital wristband stated his name as Patrick Hyslop. At 0330 hours, paramedics arrived on site in unit #788. The writer briefed them on the situation and cleared from site. S/G Manav remained on site for further investigation.   This report is submitted for your information.  Abhaypreet Singh

At 12:20 PM, the writer was patrolling inside the store when he observed a slim white female, approximately 5’6” in height. She was wearing a baseball cap, a medical mask, a black zip-up hoodie, sky blue ripped jeans, and dark brown shoes. She was also carrying a large black cloth bag. She had black hair that was positioned on the right-hand side.  When the writer first observed her, she was selecting a Milka Alpine Toffee Bar priced at $4.69. At that time, she was also holding raspberries priced at $4.99 and a Spicy Salmon Roll priced at $7.80 in her hand.  The writer continued observing her. She then asked the writer, “Are you following me?” The writer replied, “No.” She then asked, “Do you work here?” and the writer gave the same response.  After that, she went to the produce section. At that moment, Manager Ray also accompanied the writer because he had overheard the conversation, and both of them began observing the suspect.  At approximately 12:26 PM, the female attempted to exit through cashier counter #2 without paying for the items. The writer and Manager Ray approached her and recovered the merchandise. The suspect then left the store approximately 12:26 PM.  The total value of the recovered merchandise was $18.00.

A white male with long hair, wearing a white beanie, over-ear headphones, gray sweatpants with zipper pockets, a black hoodie, and a black jacket with “73” written on it entered the store. The time of entry was not noted.  He selected two bottles of Smartwater and concealed them inside his backpack. Upon noticing that he was being observed during the entire act, he approached me and stated that he would put the items back.  He then removed both bottles of Smartwater from his backpack, placed them back in the aisle, showed that his backpack was empty, and exited the store. The time of exit was approximately 13:50.

Incident Report  At approximately 4:45 PM, a female entered the store wearing a black jacket and blue trousers.  The writer was patrolling near the self-checkout section*and began observing the female from a distance.  During observation, the writer noticed the female concealing merchandise inside a Walmart bag while inside the store. The female then attempted to leave the store without paying for the concealed items.  As the female exited the store, the writer approached and confronted her. Initially, she returned two items, but the writer could clearly see that additional merchandise remained inside her bag.  The female then attempted to run away, at which point the writer secured the bag and retrieved it. The writer issued a verbal PPA. The female became aggressive and began verbally abusing the writer.  The writer then **returned inside the store and asked store staff to provide the receipt to verify the merchandise. The recovered merchandise was valued at approximately $60

Date: February 6 Time: Approximately 17:25 hours Location: Sobeys – Queen Street  At approximately 17:25 hours, the writer observed a suspicious Black male inside the store. The suspect selected multiple meat products from the meat section and placed them into a shopping cart. The suspect then proceeded to Aisle 6, where he transferred and concealed the items into a green bag.  The suspect exited the store without attempting to pay for the concealed merchandise. The total value of the unpaid merchandise is estimated to be approximately $150.  The writer followed the suspect outside the store and identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer. Upon being approached, the suspect became aggressive and fled the area on foot. A verbal PPA (Provincial Protection Act warning) was issued, and the suspect was verbally banned from the property for a period of six (6) months.  The MOD, Chris, was informed of the incident and requested to secure and retain CCTV footage for future reference.  Police report number: 2026-4000738  Suspect Description: 	•	Black male 	•	Medium build 	•	Wearing a red and black checkered jacket 	•	Camouflage-pattern pants 	•	Black winter toque with pom-pom 	•	Black gloves  For additional information or access to surveillance footage, please contact:  Store Manager: Craig Location: Sobeys – Queen Street Phone: 902-422-9884 Email: 00574mgr@sobeys.com

Event: Attempted fraud & mischief Location: Sobeys 574 Queen St, 1120 Queen St, Halifax, NS B3H 2R9 Item: Chicken Drumsticks Value: $7.57 (excluding taxes)  Details of Subject: Gender: Male Age: around 20-22 yrs Complexion: white complexion  Height: around 5’8” Weight: around 100 lbs Hair: wearing a blue cap Wearing a black jacket, black pants, white shoes and a blue coloured handbag.  On 05/02/2026, writer Amandeep Singh Jaggi was scheduled to work as Loss Prevention Investigator at Sobeys 574 Queen St from 1500 hrs to 2200 hrs.  At approximately 1800 hrs, while writer was patrolling the store, writer observed a male entering the store. The subject entered the store”Meat” section.  The writer witnessed subject selecting above mentioned item and another item from shelves. The subject was observed removing price tag from cheaper item. Due to presence of customers around, subject moved to other aisle.  The subject removed the price tag from one item (cheaper) and pasted it on another meat item (expensive) followed by selecting more items from store.  The subject dropped one item back to the shelves and proceeded towards self-checkout. The subject paid for merchandise and made a way towards exit.  The writer stopped subject at exit identifying himself as Loss Prevention Investigator for the store and explained about the price fraud subject did.  Initially, subject refused to accept however, admitted when writer presented the item with damaged price tag.  The writer warned subject against trying to attempt the same again else subject would be trespassed from property followed by subject leaving the premises.  MOD was notified of the same.  End of Report

Date: January 3, 2026 Time: 00:45 – 00:49 hours Location: Main Lobby, Security Desk, Parking Lot, Airport Road Involved Parties: 	•	Ex-Resident: Justin 	•	Resident: BH365 	•	Floor Coordinators: FC Iman, FC Stephanie Lee, Security Team Lead Navdeep Singh.  At approximately 00:45 hours, an ex-resident identified as Justin was observed standing outside the main lobby entrance, engaged in conversation with resident BH365. Due to the individual being an ex-resident and present during non-visiting hours, I proceeded outside to visually verify the situation.  While doing so, Justin approached the security desk and requested permission to make a phone call. I denied the request, clearly explaining that he is no longer authorized to be on the property and that visiting hours were over. He was instructed in a calm and professional manner to leave the property immediately.  Justin became argumentative and continued questioning the decision, refusing to comply with the direction to leave. As the situation was escalating verbally and the individual was non-compliant, I contacted Floor Coordination via radio for assistance.  FC Iman responded promptly to the lobby and began speaking with Justin, reiterating that he must leave the premises immediately. Justin was informed that failure to comply would result in police being contacted. FC Stephanie Lee also engaged in conversation with Justin to further reinforce the instructions and de-escalate the situation.  After continued explanation by staff, Justin eventually complied and left the building area, exiting through the parking lot in an agitated and visibly upset manner, proceeding in the direction of Airport Road.  At approximately 00:49 hours, resident BH365 was observed exiting the building again and briefly speaking with Justin on Airport Road, outside of the property boundaries. Shortly afterward, both individuals left the scene in separate directions. No further interaction or disturbance was observed

Date: January 3, 2026 Time: Approximately 02:00 hours Location: Main Lobby (Public Area) Involved Parties: 	•	Resident: HW362, CD370 	•	Vehicle: Red Subaru  At approximately 02:00 hours, resident HW362 was observed inside the main lobby, which is a public/common area, holding a beer can in her hand. Based on property rules prohibiting open alcohol containers in public areas, I approached HW362 to address the situation.  I politely confronted HW362 and instructed her to dispose of the beer can immediately. HW362 denied consuming alcohol and stated that she was not drinking. However, the beer can was visibly open, which indicated a possible violation of property policy regarding alcohol consumption in common areas.  Due to her refusal to comply with the instruction and the ongoing presence of an open alcohol container in the lobby, I contacted Floor Coordination for assistance. Floor Coordinators responded and attempted to speak with HW362 to explain the policy and resolve the situation.  Before the conversation could be completed, HW362 exited the lobby and left the property, accompanied by CD370. Both individuals entered a red Subaru vehicle and departed the premises shortly thereafter. No further interaction or disturbance was observed following their departure.

While the writer was on break at approximately 4:20 PM, an incident occurred inside the store. When the writer returned from break, the supervisor informed the writer that a male suspect had stolen steak meat valued at approximately $200. According to the supervisor, a tall male suspect wearing a black jacket came to the meat section and asked the butcher to pack four packs of steak meat. The butcher complied with the request. After receiving the meat, the suspect concealed all four packs inside his jacket and proceeded toward the exit door. The butcher then immediately informed the supervisor. The supervisor approached the suspect near the exit and questioned him. However, supervisor didn’t call security as they thought not to disturb security while they are on their break. The suspect denied taking any items from the store and exited the premises without returning the merchandise. After being informed, the writer reviewed the CCTV footage with the manager. Camera footage confirmed that a male suspect, approximately 5’9” in height, wearing a black jacket, exited the store at approximately 4:22 PM with four packs of steak meat concealed inside his jacket. No recovery of merchandise was made. No further interaction occurred after the suspect exited the store.  Writer complained about the suspect to the police as manager wants to do so. Writer is still waiting for the police file number

At 12:39 PM, during a regular patrol, a white male was observed skateboarding in the parkade on level P4. This is the third time within the past six months that the same individual has been observed skateboarding in the same area. The security officer requested the individual’s name in order to issue a written trespass under the Private Property Act (PPA); however, the individual refused to provide identification. As security officer did not have his name, a written PPA trespass could not be issued. The individual was verbally informed that the property is private and that he is not permitted to remain on site without authorization. The individual complied with the direction and exited the property without further issues.

At approximately 16:20 at Coleman’s, NFLD, I observed a male individual enter the store. The individual appeared to be approximately 50–55 years old, about 5’8” tall, wearing a black hat, red coat, and black pants. He was carrying a backpack upon entry and was acting suspiciously.  I maintained observation from a distance. When the individual reached the meat section, he placed his backpack into the shopping cart and attempted to put meat products inside the bag. Upon noticing this behavior, I began patrolling the meat section. The individual then started moving from aisle to aisle, seemingly attempting to avoid or hide from me.  I remained positioned near the meat section to keep visual contact. Shortly after, the individual ran through an aisle toward the meat section. I stepped in front of him, which caused the individual to appear startled and become verbally aggressive toward me.  The individual then proceeded to the cashier area, where he attempted to take a photograph of me. I informed him that he was not permitted to take my photo and advised that if he continued, I would take his photo as well.  After this interaction, the individual left the store without stealing any merchandise.

A person was heard yelling the name “Thomas” near the security office at approximately 5:30 AM, so I went to check and met a male and female approximately 45–55 years old who were searching for their son, Thomas. They stated that Thomas has mental health issues and had gone out to celebrate with friends but did not return home. Thomas called them around 5:00 AM, stating he was near the ferry terminal, had missed the last ferry, and was not in a clear state of mind. He was last seen in casino around 6:00 AM wearing a green jacket and black snowshoes, and police are actively attempting to locate him and are tracking his phone, with hopes that he will be found safely.

At 8 o’clock two men entered the store. First man about 5’10, 180 pounds wearing a black hat and black puffy jacket.  Started walking around the store, went to the whiskey aisle.  Then proceeded to vodka aisle.  Picked up a bottle of Don Julio tequila and two cans of black fly. Store staff followed him around as did I. He continued back-and-forth through the store going from the vodka aisle to the whiskey aisle then rearranging some wine bottles. Attempted to steal, but was deterred from doing so. . A second man about 6 foot wearing a gray toque and light blue to white puffy coat and grey backpack.  Asked for a face mask.  Started walking back-and-forth from vodka aisle to whiskey aislePicked up a flask of lambs.  When got to the cash said he forgot his wallet.  Proceeded back into the store around a different cash grab three bottles of Smirnoff vodka original 200 mL then slipped them directly into his coat left. Both men who were in the store for roughly 20 minutes.
fields.merchandise_description: 3x 200 mL bottles of Smirnoff vodka original

fields.summary: At around 11:29AM a lady beeped at the EAS machine at exit.  Writer stopper her and asked if he bought anything from pharmacy which have tag.  She replied no and showed a receipt of baking soda in her hands. Writer asked again and she denied and then left the store. Later on writer asked the MOD to check camera footage and found out she concealed an hair spray in her left pocket of jacket.  She’s banned from store now

While conducting routine patrol, I observed a white female wearing a black jacket and blue jeans filled her cart mostly with meat items. This individual has a prior history and was previously apprehended with approximately $850 worth of groceries, all of which were recovered at that time. Today, once her cart was full, I was standing toward the exit area to monitor other potential suspects. Shortly after, the same female was seen coming from the deli aisle and entering the cashier lane. She bypassed the checkout process, passed other customers, and attempted to exit the store without paying for any merchandise. I stopped her inside the store by securing the cart and informed her that she was already banned from the premises and would be charged for trespassing on private property as well as theft. She appeared nervous and immediately fled the store toward her vehicle, which was parked approximately 200 meters away. There was an unknown male waiting for her inside the vehicle. I attempted to approach in order to capture a photo, but due to the distance, the vehicle left the area quickly and was not identified. All merchandise was recovered safely, and store management has been notified. Time of exit 12:38pm

Incident Report  Date of Report: January 2, 2026 Time of Incident: Approximately 2015 hrs Location: Sobeys Aberdeen Reporting Officer: Rhythm Oberoi  Involved Parties: 	1.	Andrew Penny – Wearing a black hat, black pants, black jacket, and a camouflage hoodie. 	2.	Unknown male – Wearing a black jacket, black hat, black pants, and brown shoes.  Incident Details: On January 2nd at approximately 2015 hrs, Andrew Penny entered Sobeys Aberdeen accompanied by another male. The reporting officer immediately recognized Andrew Penny and instructed him to leave the premises, as he was previously banned from the store.  The unknown male began verbally abusing the reporting officer, repeatedly yelling and using offensive language. Andrew Penny proceeded to the meat section of the store. The reporting officer informed the store manager of the situation. The unknown male subsequently left the store without taking any items.  The reporting officer approached the meat section and observed Andrew Penny selecting two steak packs and one chicken pack. The reporting officer contacted the non-emergency police line and warned Andrew Penny that he should leave right now given his previous ban from the store. Andrew Penny attempted to exit the store with the items concealed in his jacket.  The reporting officer followed Andrew Penny into the parking lot and not any further, Andrew Penny then left the premises, walking toward Starbucks. The store manager subsequently contacted the police to report the theft. The stolen items are valued at approximately $60.  Due to the store closing, no immediate action could be taken on-site, and the matter will be addressed by law enforcement on the following day.  Conclusion: The incident involved theft by a repeat offender. All details have been documented, and the matter has been reported to the police for further action.

At approximately 12:52 p.m., four individuals entered the store who had previously been banned and removed for trespassing. I immediately informed the manager on duty, who began monitoring and following them. I contacted the police and reported the incident. The primary suspect, Victor Ratsma, has been PPA’d multiple times in the past. He exited the store at approximately 12:53 p.m., while the remaining individuals left at approximately 12:58 p.m. Police were en route at the time of this report.

At approximately 16:45, four male youths was walking in the store, when writer saw them. All appeared to be approximately 5’8” tall and were carrying backpacks. One was wearing a grey hoodie and grey trousers. The second was wearing a black hoodie, black pants, sneakers, and was carrying a yellow backpack. Of the two youths who later ran, one had black hair and was wearing a black jacket over a white T-shirt with sky-blue jeans. The other had brown hair and was wearing a black jacket, black trousers, sneakers, and a black backpack.  When the writer began observing them, they were walking through different aisles. The group proceeded to Aisle 11, where they were handling drinks. The writer positioned himself behind the shelf to observe them clearly. At that time, the youth with black hair and a black jacket appeared to open his jacket zipper, but the writer could not see clearly because their backs were facing him.  Shortly after, the writer heard the youths say, “Oh, he saw us, he saw us,” referring to another employee who was walking through the meat section. According to that employee, it was possible the youths placed something into a backpack, but this could not be confirmed.  After this, two of the youths ran toward the self-checkout area while making those comments. The writer asked the employee what had happened and was informed of the above. The writer continued observing the group. The two youths returned from self-checkout, rejoined the others, and the group went to Cashier Counter 8, where they paid for two bottles of soft drink. During this time, they were watching the writer.  One youth began making facial expressions and hand gestures toward the writer while speaking. The writer approached and asked what he was saying. The youth responded, “Why are you standing there?” The writer replied that He can stand anywhere, it shouldn’t matter to them.The youth repeated the question, and the writer replied “he had the right to stand anywhere in the store and that it was not the youth’s concern. The group then left the store while using inappropriate language toward the writer.  The writer followed to request that they leave the store, but they exited before the writer could speak with them. A self-checkout cashier later informed the writer that when the two youths first came to self-checkout, they stared at her and then left the area. After about one minute, they returned to the store, appearing to know they were being observed. The cashier stated the youths seemed to be walking around the store with the intention of creating an issue and repeatedly looked at the writer.  After several minutes, the writer believed the youths were intentionally causing a disturbance. The writer informed Manager Dave of the full situation; however, he was leaving for the day. Manager Ken arrived shortly after, and the writer informed him as well and received permission to remove the youths if necessary.  While the writer was speaking with management, the four youths were watching from Aisle 10 near the meat section. The writer then went to self-checkout and asked the cashier to page a manager for confirmation. During this time, the youths stood near the entrance watching the writer and the cashier.  At that moment, a woman approached the writer and the cashier and identified herself as the aunt of one of the youths. She stated the youths were not stealing and questioned why they were being watched. The writer informed her that he had not accused or approached the youths regarding theft. She then asked if the writer worked for the store, and the writer confirmed that he did.  The store manager was present near the self-checkout area and informed the woman that staff could not discuss the situation with her and that only the youths’ parents could be contacted. The woman stated she would call the child’s mother.  The manager then approached the youths. At that time, Cashier Supervisor Alex informed staff that one of the youths was already banned from the store. The group exited the store at approximately 17:20. The woman left a few minutes later and, according to the cashier, asked in an aggressive manner whether the youths had left.

One white male noticed near to prepared food items having stuff in his cart  he changed alternative aisles  he then moved straight to exit near to customer service with stuff in his cart. He didn’t made any payment . I approached him in the parking lot and asked for the stuff  he concealed fries in his jacket and i took cart from him and recovered the stuff Description of suspect - white 5.8ft tall male wearing black jacket with black pants and black spectacles Suspect left the store around 2.20pm

fields.summary: A white male was observed in the produce department and then proceeded directly to the meat department, where he selected salmon and fresh slim meat. He then moved into the aisles in an attempt to conceal the items; however, I maintained continuous visual contact. The subject abandoned the Reese’s Puffs and proceeded to the pharmacy area. He again failed to conceal the meat, removed all meat items in the cart, and exited the store through the self-checkout area. Exit time- 7:30pm Description: white male, in his 30s, wearing whole black with grey scarf around his neck and black mask on

At 15:54, The woman who is banned from the store except pharmacy and her boyfriend who is banned completely from the store entered the store.Her boyfriend entered the washroom and when he came out the writer asked him that he is completely banned from the establishment and not allowed to use the washroom either. So, he replied “ I am not ban, I am not her boyfriend “ and went to towards exit. So, the writer told him and his girlfriend who is only allowed to pharmacy that the next time the writer will call the cops for them. She said “okay I will tell him”and left the store.

The security received a report from the monitoring department regarding an alarm originating from elevator number 7 at the Muir Hotel. Upon investigation, security confirmed the elevator was in operational condition. Concurrently, a guest emerged via the stairs, explaining they had been temporarily trapped in the elevator, prompting them to activate the alarm. The guest further stated they managed to open the elevator doors on the third floor and subsequently used the stairs. The Manager on Duty has requested that this elevator be placed in service mode to prevent further use.

Around 15:45, the writer was patrolling the area when he heard the beeping sound from the anti-theft system, so the writer approached the individuals. There were two females. One of them was Jessica, a white female, approximately 25 years old, about 5’8” tall, average build, wearing a sky-blue winter cap with a “Best Coast” tag, a black winter jacket over a white zip-up hoodie, black pants, and white shoes with black soles. She was wearing earrings and a nose ring and was carrying a white backpack. The other female was Alicia Frye, approximately 30 years old, shorter in height, with blonde hair and average build, wearing a light purple jacket over a white sweater, trousers, sneakers, and carrying a black side bag. When the system sounded, the writer asked them if they had purchased anything. They replied, “No, we just got medicine from the pharmacy,” and showed the medicine bottles. The writer checked the items and confirmed that the bottles were not the reason the system sounded. The writer then approached Manager Samir and informed him of the incident, requesting that he check the cameras. When the manager reviewed the footage, he saw that both females entered the store around 15:37 and went to Aisle 2, where Jessica took one Old Spice deodorant, worth approximately $20. They then went to the pharmacy to get their medicine. While they were at the pharmacy, Jessica went to the cashier counter, took a Scene card, and returned to the pharmacy. From there, she took one Benylin Cold & Flu medicine, worth approximately $15, and put it inside her jacket pocket. She then asked something of the pharmacy manager. After that, they went through Aisle 2 and exited the store without paying for any items.  Manager Samir confirmed concealment and theft after reviewing the cameras. The total value of the stolen merchandise is approximately $36 before tax. Pharmacy manger Peiman is going to PPA Jessica and going to give her PPA form on tomorrow.
fields.merchandise_description: Old spice Deod CRM Vanilla Shea and medicine

fields.summary: Description of Incident:  At approximately 12:15 p.m., a white male, believed to be in his early 20s, entered the store. The suspect was wearing a red and black zippered jacket and blue-colored pants. After entering, the individual moved through various sections of the store and placed multiple items into a bag.  While patrolling near the self-checkout area, the writer observed the suspect pick up a bag containing groceries and attempt to exit the store without paying. The writer immediately followed the suspect and attempted to recover the bag. The suspect pushed the writer and fled the store.  The writer followed the suspect outside in an attempt to photograph the vehicle’s license plate; however, the suspect’s vehicle did not display a license plate.  The writer notified the manager, and store surveillance footage was reviewed. The items taken were identified using a void receipt for documentation purposes.  The total value of the stolen groceries is estimated to be **$70–$80**.  No further incidents to report at this moment

Date: 7 January 2026, At approximately 1100 hours, the writer and a trainee came downstairs and observed a suspicious white female wearing black pants and a black hoodie. The subject was pushing a white shopping cart not belonging to the store, which contained multiple bags filled with grocery items.  The subject selected multiple frozen meat products and placed them into the cart. After noticing she was being observed, she returned the items to the shelf. The writer also observed the subject consuming a soda inside the store without paying.  Later, the subject proceeded to the self-checkout area and advised the cashier that the items were too expensive. She returned buns, gourmet cheese, and croissants and attempted to exit the store, stating that all items in her possession were from another store and that a social worker had given her the items.  Outside the store, the writer identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer and observed a salad with a Sobeys label inside the subject’s bag, along with other items including pistachios, frozen products, and two meat products believed to be from another store. The subject was advised that if Sobeys merchandise was confirmed, she would be banned from the site. The writer returned to the store with the recovered Sobeys product.  While outside, the subject pointed at the writer and trainee and made a throat-slitting gesture, indicating a death threat. Manager Chris was informed of the incident. Supervisor Angie reviewed the CCTV footage and confirmed that the subject stole merchandise valued over $100.   Police have been informed.   For additional information or access to surveillance footage, please contact: Store Manager: Craig Location: Sobeys – Queen Street Phone: 902-422-9884 Email: 00574mgr@sobeys.com
Customer dropped Budweiser 12 bottle pack beer ba accident.

Incident Report Date: January 03, 2026 Time: 1556-1617 hours Location: Sobeys Prince Street, Truro (871) Reported By: LPO Gurpal Singh  At approximately 1556 hours, an elderly woman in her 60s, dressed entirely in black, entered the store accompanied by Brittney Clark, a woman in her 30s. Clark was wearing a blue and grey jacket, grey cap, grey muffler, and black pants. The elderly woman was carrying a small cart. At 1557 hours, both individuals proceeded through the produce and seafood sections, where they selected cremini mushrooms, supreme seafood mix, white chowder, and lobster-flavoured chunks, placing the items into the small cart. They then went to the meat section and selected one package of naturally smoked bacon. Additional items were taken from various aisles, including cat litter, toilet tissue, black pepper, bathroom cleaner, garbage bags, cream soda, cream blend, and strawberry shortcake. Brittney Clark was observed assisting the elderly woman by placing items into the cart. At approximately 1611 hours, the elderly woman walked through the self-checkout area with the small cart containing approximately 15 items without completing payment. At 1612 hours, upon exiting through the main doors, the writer stopped the individuals and identified himself. Brittney Clark, who is known to the writer, stated that she had forgotten to pay for the items and mentioned that she was experiencing personal difficulties. Both individuals agreed to return to the store and paid for all merchandise at Checkout 6 at 1617 hours. While at Checkout 6, the writer informed Brittney Clark that she is not permitted to return to the store for the next six months due to this incident and previous occurrences. She acknowledged and agreed to the ban. The elderly woman was advised that if a similar incident occurs in the future, the Truro Police would be contacted. The writer notified Manager Lenny of the incident and obtained images of both individuals from the CCTV system. The total worth of the items is $84.06 that has been paid. End of report.

This time , it was a different girl and her name was “ Laura”. She also called the Alicia and asked her when she’ll be back. The writer talked with Alicia on a phone call and she informed the writer her brother died recently that’s why she’s not home and she”ll be back by Monday afternoon. The writer aware Alicia regarding the importance of document as well.   Nothing further to add, this report is for information purposes.

At approximately 1745 hrs, LPO received a call from manager Krista that she noticed a suspicious female with a cart full of meat and frozen items. LPO came to front self checkout and looked for the female person of interest. LPO could not find anyone in the store resembling the description given by manager. LPO checked the cameras and found out that the female left from self checkout with a cart pretending to use washroom, she put the cart near ladies washroom and then left straight out without paying. LPO was able to get the clear CCTV pictures of the female. LPO also called non emergency police service as the price of the grocery would be approximately 200$ or more. LPO also filled out and completed Sobeys non emergency reporting form. LPO notified Branon and sent the CCTV pictures of the female. LPO will be sending the incident number once received from police officer tomorrow.   The description of the female includes,  Caucasian, female, in her 20s, slim build, height approximately  5’7, black long female jacket, black pants, dark brown shoulder length hair, had a black mini bag, brown leather shoes.
fields.merchandise_description: Turkey, other meat items, pizza and biscuits ( took whole cart out )

fields.summary: Thomas Walsh, a man that has stolen from multiple NLC locations since the new year entered the store. Knowing his intentions I walked with him the entire time he was in the store. After he confronted me for doing so I told him we have no problem as long as he isn't trying to leave with the product, he then stated that his intention was to leave with the product in his bag so I grabbed it from him, led him to the front of the store, removed the product from his bag and gave it back to him. All the while exchanging choice words. Thomas is a slight white male approximately 5'7" with dark shirt hair and a small goatee.
fields.merchandise_description: One 60oz Iceberg white rum, 26oz Lambs white rum, and two 40oz captain Morgan spiced rum.

fields.summary: At around 18:45, a guy wearing construction vest, black&red cap , carrying blue walmart bag and holding a cigarette entered the store and he picked one room freshener and a black box magnum condoms from the shelf and putted the magnum box in his walmart bag. When he came at cash register he just paid for the room freshener and didn’t pay for magnum box. So, when he was about to exit the sensor beeped and the writer asked him for a receipt of magnum box but he denied and ran out from the store at 18:48. The writer gave a note to supervisor Dante for store manager to check the cameras.

While I was in Dominion getting food for break a man that has come into this store and the Sobeys location just up the street two days in a row, had come in and taken the listed items. He is approximately 5'8" with dirty brown hair, wearing a dark hat with a yellow logo on the front with something resembling a horseshoe, a blue windbreaker, jeans, and white shoes. He grabbed a cart and headed straight to the ice berg where he picked up the two 60oz Vodkas and placed them in his cart, when he moved to the Rum he put on Vodka into his jacket and proceeded to an endcap where he placed the other two 60oz into his jacket. He then picked up two bottles of champagne and placed them in his cart, one of the employees was making eye contact with him as he was coming up through the aisle and he called out to her asking what she was looking at and stormed out if the store after she tried to call for me and other employees. He was in the building for less than two minutes.  We have the information of where he was picked up from a Bugdens taxi. A manager at the Sobeys location chased him out of the store and he ran behind the building, abandoning his taxi outside. She questioned the driver and thats the address he gave her.

At around 6 PM writer was positioned at entrance after doing a patrol in the store when MOD Neil came told someone got out the store without paying  with their cart. The employee standing on the self checkout told him that.  Later on we both went outside and by then they have managed to put the stuff in the white Honda civic and managed to flee on foot while making rounds in the plaza at fuel station and mcd. Till then writer have contacted the police and they were on their way.   Writer positioned his car near the Honda to get a better view of the thief if they try to get in their car.   After seeing no one near the car 2 suspects who first left the store came back from the mcd and got into the car. Later on right then 2 more people 1 men and 1 women came out of the store running and all 4 get into the car and fled the scene .   Police came around 7 minutes later and took all the necessary details and left.   Later on the 2 people who left in the end ditched their cart full of meat and groceries including dog food and bedsheets in the candy aisle.  They all 4 fled the scene.  No estimate of the theft amount  Deterred merchandise costs- 743$  End of the report.
fields.merchandise_description: A lot of Meat, dog food, women’s slippers, bedsheet, other grocery items.

I, Vishal Mangvana, working as an LPO at Sobeys Westside, observed a suspicious female enter the store at approximately 5:18 PM while I was patrolling the sales floor. The suspect appeared to be a female approximately 35–40 years old, wearing blue jeans, a black hoodie, black sneakers, and glasses. I identified her as suspicious and began monitoring her movements. At approximately 5:40 PM, the suspect was observed filling a shopping cart with merchandise. She then proceeded toward the entrance doors, appearing as though she intended to exit the store. At that point, she seemed to realize she was under observation by staff and recognized me. She immediately abandoned the cart and exited the store without the merchandise. Upon exiting, the suspect entered a black Dodge truck that was waiting in the parking lot, and the vehicle left the premises immediately. The total value of the abandoned merchandise was $380.22. All items were fully recovered and handed over to Sobeys staff. The store manager was informed immediately. The manager decided not to contact police, as all merchandise was recovered. The suspect has been noted for PPA (Police to be Advised) should she return to the store in the future.

On Tuesday, 6th January 2026, at approximately 19:20, the writer noticed a old white woman, think built, 5”5 wearing black long jacket and grey lower entered inside the store with black reusable bag and a cart. The suspect picked up some items from meat section and concealed them inside the reusable bag and kept roaming around the store . After that, she took two chicken wraps and headed towards the self checkout section and trying to pay over there but she didn’t have the money . Then, she left the wraps at the counter and again roaming around the store and then, went towards the ATM machines. She stood there for 5 minutes and then left the store with unpaid items. The writer confronted her outside the store and aware her regarding the unpaid items . The woman was intoxicated and acted like she’s doesn’t understand what the writer trying to say to her . The MOD was also present outside with the trainee guard . She kept refusing to give the items and abusing the writer . Then, she tried to enter inside the taxi but the writer informed the taxi driver regarding the suspect. Then, she kept walking and refused to give the items back. The MOD and the writer banned the suspect from the store and told her if she comes back HRP will be called .   The writer informed the HRP as well and explained the situation to them and gave all the appropriate information to the officer .

At approximately 1700 hrs, LPO noticed a suspicious male with approximately 4+ packs of meat in the cart. LPO followed the male and noticed the male was putting lot of items in the cart from seafood, deli, meat, floral and grocery department. LPO followed the male and called manager Nate and alerted him to stay on the front entrance. The male had another Male with him who had a cart and had concealed minimal items in it. Both males were in search of going out without paying. As both males noticed manager Nate at front entrance and LPO at the self checkout, both males ran away from back grocery receiving door near pharmacy leaving both of their carts on the floor near aisle 6 at approximately 1723 hrs. LPO and manager Nate took the carts and received scanned price of the items which was $444.03 CAD with taxes combining both carts. Manager Nate also sent both male’s CCTV photos to LPO. LPO reported this incident to Branon and provided photos of both males. LPO also filled out Sobeys non emergency form. LPO will be reporting the incident to Non emergency Halifax police tomorrow due to closure of their call centre at 1640 hrs. LPO will provide the incident number to Branon once received from police. Nothing further to report.   The description of first male, ( concealed majority of items in a whole cart )   Caucasian, male, light moustache and beard, age approximately in his 40s, height approximately 5’7, green jacket, green adidas cap, black pants, black shoes, black t shirt, had optical glasses. INCIDENT REPORT Date of Incident: January 9 Time: Approximately 20:15 hours Location: Russell Street entrance – Construction Site Reported by: Saravanan Ganesan Incident Description: At approximately 20:15 hours, the writer observed a female individual at the Russell Street entrance of the site who had already opened the rope barrier that was tied across the entrance to restrict access. Based on observation , the woman was wearing a dark-colored jacket and dark pants. The writer immediately intervened and instructed the woman not to open or access the restricted area. The woman stated that she was “just curious” and explained that she would like her daughter to attend this school once the construction is completed. The writer informed the woman that the site is a restricted construction area and that opening the barrier and entering the area constitutes trespassing. The woman acknowledged the instruction, complied without resistance, and exited the site area. The rope barrier was secured back in place. There was no escalation or further issues during the interaction.  After the individual left, the writer conducted a patrol of the entire site. No damage, safety concerns, or additional unauthorized persons were observed. All conditions were found to be normal

Incident Report Date: 10 January 2026 Time: Entry at approx. 1840 hrs | Exit at approx. 1848 hrs Location: Sobeys Pictou Store Number: 232 Position: Loss Prevention Officer Reporting Officer: Bhupinder Singh On 10 January 2026, the writer was conducting a regular patrol within the sales floor when the writer observed a male individual who appeared suspicious. The individual had visible fresh injury marks on his face, swollen eyes, and appeared to be under the influence of alcohol. At that time, the suspect was observed carrying two deli items. The writer began monitoring the suspect while maintaining continuous observation, concealment, and selection. While in Aisle 5, the suspect was observed concealing one deli item inside his jacket. The suspect then continued shopping, selected one pop bottle, and proceeded to Checkout 2. At checkout, the suspect paid only for two items and failed to pay for the concealed item inside his jacket. The writer maintained uninterrupted observation and waited until the suspect exited the store through the main entrance, confirming failure to render payment. Once outside the store, the writer approached the suspect, identified himself as store security, and informed the suspect of the unpaid merchandise concealed inside his jacket. The suspect initially denied the allegation. The writer advised the suspect that police would be contacted immediately if the merchandise was not returned or paid for. At that point, the suspect voluntarily surrendered the concealed item to the writer. The recovered merchandise was identified as chicken wings valued at $16.35. All merchandise was successfully recovered, resulting in no loss to the store. The suspect refused to provide personal information and left the scene voluntarily. The writer issued a verbal trespass notice for six (6) months and advised the suspect not to return to the property. The incident was then reported to store management. No police involvement was required. Suspect Description Gender: Male Age: Approx. 55–60 Clothing: Black jacket, blue jeans Hair: Grey Physical Condition: Visible facial injuries, swollen eyes Behavior: Appeared intoxicated Result: ✔ Successful apprehension ✔ Merchandise recovered ✔ No loss ✔ No police involvement Report completed by: Bhupinder Singh Loss Prevention Officer

Incident Report  Date: January 10, 2026  Time: 1351-1415 hours  Location: Sobeys Prince Street, Truro (871)  Reported By: LPO Gurpal Singh  At approximately 1351 hours, a female suspect wearing a black cap, brown camouflage jacket, black pants, and black shoes was observed inside the store. The individual had already collected multiple items from various sections of the store. At the start of the writer’s shift, LPO Rishab Puri informed the writer that the same female had previously been apprehended by him at Robie Street Sobeys and was currently present in the store. At the time, LPO Puri was working at the NSLC located next to Sobeys and had entered the store to purchase food. Upon entering the store at approximately 1351 hours, the writer observed the suspect coming through the pharmacy aisles. The suspect had four green reusable grocery bags filled with merchandise and additional items placed in a large shopping cart. All managers had already been notified of her presence and suspicious behavior. The writer positioned himself near the self-checkout area, and the suspect appeared to notice the writer’s presence. At approximately 1354 hours, the suspect, later identified as Rose Brooks, exited through the main entrance. Manager Lenny stopped her and asked if she had paid for the merchandise. Manager Lenny brought her back in the store. At this time, writer was also present there. The suspect stated that she was going to pay and proceeded toward the checkout area. She then abandoned her partially loaded cart near checkouts 4–5, selected two to three items, and paid for those items at checkout 6. Upon exiting the store, the suspect did not proceed toward her vehicle and stated to the writer that she had paid for the items she was carrying in her hands. The writer advised her that he was not outside for her. The suspect then remained standing in the area between the NSLC and Sobeys. At approximately 1355 hours, as instructed by Manager Lenny, the writer contacted Truro Police due to the suspect’s suspicious behavior and to issue a ban. At 1408 hours, Constable Amanda Assoun, badge #155, of Truro Police arrived on scene. The suspect was issued a six-month ban under file number 2026-42679. The suspect was identified as Rose Brooks, date of birth February 16, 1980, residing at 30 Martin Crescent, Millbrook. Her phone number is 902-843-2213. The total value of the merchandise left in the large shopping cart was $272.62. The writer informed Manager Lenny that the suspect had been banned from the store, obtained the transaction receipt from checkout 1 from the cash supervisor, and secured still images of the suspect from CCTV footage. End of Report

On Saturday, at approximately 1815 hours, the writer observed a suspicious female wearing a black jacket and black pants, pushing a shopping cart and carrying a handbag. The female proceeded to the natural resources section and was observed openly selecting items and placing them into the shopping cart. The writer further observed the female opening some product packaging and consuming items before concealing all unpaid merchandise into her handbag. The female abandoned the empty shopping cart and exited the store without making any attempt to pay for the merchandise. The writer approached the female along with an accompanying male outside the store and requested the return of the unpaid items. Both individuals became aggressive, denied the theft, and fled the scene in a blue SUV bearing a Nova Scotia Temporary Permit (3694579). The suspect was issued a six-month ban from the property. The incident was reported to MOD Karla immediately after occurrence. The total value of the stolen merchandise is estimated to be approximately $50. Action Taken: Incident details and suspect image documented for reference. MOD Karla notified immediately following the incident.

Medical Emergency – Resident 362 KW Time: 16:40 – 17:07 At approximately 16:40, Floor Coordinator Anna informed security of a medical emergency involving Resident 362 KW, who was located in Room 369 CJ at the time. The Fire Department arrived at 16:45 and was escorted to Room 369 CJ by Team Lead Ataye. A Code Blue was initiated by Team Lead Nibir. An ambulance arrived at 16:47, and emergency responders assessed the resident. Resident 362 KW was transported by ambulance for further medical care. Emergency services departed the property at approximately 17:07. The situation was managed efficiently, responders had full access, and everything was brought under control without further issues.

Loss Prevention Officer Baljeet observed a white male suspect, approximately 6’3” in height, wearing a black safety vest jacket and blue jeans, pushing a large shopping cart with a black backpack placed inside it. Officer Baljeet first observed the suspect in the produce department selecting kiwis and placing them into the cart. While monitoring the suspect’s movements throughout the store, Officer Baljeet immediately recognized him as an individual previously involved in theft incidents and currently under an active six-month ban from the store. Before Officer Baljeet could make contact, the suspect proceeded to the pharmacy aisle, where he abandoned the shopping cart and exited the store without making any purchases at 6.18 amOfficer Baljeet followed the suspect outside, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and advised the suspect that he was still banned from the store and not permitted to return. The suspect stated that he would not come back and left the property with his black backpack, which potentially contained unpaid merchandise. Officer Baljeet then returned to the store and reported the incident in full to Store Manager Jeff Cross, advising him of the possibility of concealed theft items and requesting a review of CCTV footage, to which the manager responded that he would review the footage at a later time.

Incident Report  Date: January 11, 2026  Time: 1515-1538 hours  Location: Sobeys Prince Street, Truro (871)  Reported By: LPO Gurpal Singh  At approximately 1515 hours, Brittney Clark entered the store wearing a black bandanna, dark orange hoodie, black joggers, and black shoes. She was carrying a small shopping cart and a green reusable grocery bag. She proceeded to the produce and deli sections, where she selected a pizza dough ball and salad items. At approximately 1525 hours, she attended the meat section and selected center pork chops. Between 1525 and 1535 hours, Brittney Clark moved through several aisles, including aisles 3, 5, and 7, selecting a total of seven additional items. Brittney Clark had previously been apprehended by the writer on December 7, 2025, and was also accompanying another individual who was apprehended on January 3, 2026. On January 3, 2025, she was verbally banned by the writer for a period of six months. Despite this ban, she returned to the store on today’s date. With permission from the Manager on Duty, Glen, the writer contacted Truro Police to issue a ban notice. At approximately 1529 hours, Truro Police were called while Brittney Clark was in Aisle 7. At approximately 1535 hours, a police officer arrived at the store. The officer recognized Brittney Clarke and approached her directly, instructing her to leave the premises. Brittney Clarke argued with the officer before abandoning the shopping cart containing all selected items. She removed her empty grocery bag from the cart and exited the store at approximately 1538 hours. The officer then left the store without providing confirmation regarding the enforcement of the ban. At approximately 1548 hours, the writer contacted Truro Police again to request an update. The police dispatcher provided the file number 2026-46916 and advised that the attending officer would follow up after completing the PPA. The writer obtained a receipt for the recovered merchandise, totaling $46.07, and informed the Manager on Duty, Glen, of the updates received from Truro Police. Suspect images were also obtained from CCTV footage. Suspect Information: * Name: Brittney Clark * Date of Birth: May 5, 1987 * Address: 371 Queen Street, Truro, Nova Scotia, B2N 2C4 End of Report

Loss Prevention Officer Baljeet was on his scheduled break. Upon returning from break, Officer Baljeet was informed by the Manager on Duty, Angela, that a theft had occurred during his absence. Angela advised that a white male, approximately 35 years of age, wearing a brown coat and pushing a small cart filled with meat products, exited the store at approximately 6:27 PM without paying for the merchandise. Angela further stated that the store paging system was not functioning at the time of the incident, which prevented her from paging Officer Baljeet immediately. Officer Baljeet requested to review the camera footage related to the incident; however, he was unable to operate the camera system and therefore could not review the footage at that time. No further action could be taken due to the lack of immediate access to video evidence.

Writer received a call at 0443 hrs from S/O Ajay regarding a continuous beeping noise coming from room number 12. Writer arrived on site at 0451 hrs and proceeded near room number 12. Writer heard a loud beeping noise coming from inside the unit, which appeared to be medical equipment. Writer knocked on the door approximately 5–6 times but received no response. A wellness check was initiated, and writer contacted non-emergency to request assistance. S/O Ajay also confirmed that the beeping noise had been audible since approximately 0300 hrs.  During this time, a male resident from room 11 approached writer and advised that a female named Dyana from room 9 had keys to the apartment. Writer contacted Dyana, explained the situation, and requested her assistance. She agreed and entered the apartment to check on the resident.  Dyana advised writer that the noise was coming from the resident’s medical equipment, which was alerting her to take medication. The female resident in room 12 is deaf and was unable to hear the beeping sound. No further issues were observed. She thanked writer for double wellness check as it was important . Writer and S/O Ajay cleared the situation and departed the area.

03:30 Writer completed first patrol. Writer heard a constant beeping sound coming from unit 12.  04:30 Writer completed second patrol. Writer noticed that the sound has been constant since last patrol. Writer informed Supervisor Chirag.  Supervisor Chirag arrived at site. Writer gave a brief overview of the situation. Writer and S/S Chirag approached the unit 12. S/S Chirag knocked hard on the door around five times, no response to the knocking was observed. S/S Chirag contacted 811 helpline, they informed us to contact non-emergency about the scenario. S/S Chirag contacted non-emergency and informed them about the beeping sound coming from unit  12, which seemed to be concerning noise.  A gentleman from adjacent unit (unit 11) came out and informed us that Dyana from unit 9 helps the old lady in the unit 12. Writer and S/S Chirag approached unit 9 and informed Dyana about the situation.  Dyana opened unit 12 and checked the unit for the resident's well-being. Dyana came out and informed writer  and S/S Chirag that unit 12 resident is completely fine. Dyana informed that the resident uses a medical equipment that makes the beeping sound. According to Dyana couple of days ago, paramedics and police checked up on unit 12 regarding the medical equipment.  S/S Chirag left the site. Police arrived on site. Writer informed police about the incident, that everything is in order and the resident is safe. Police informed writer that they knew about the unit 12 resident situation and about her equipment. Dyana informed that unit 12 resident is deaf and uses wheelchair. All in order.   Personal involved:  Supervisor Chirag  Unit 11 Gentleman  Unit 09 Dyana Unit 12 resident  Police: CST. CAMERON CHAFFEY #604

fields.summary: Incident Report  Date: 11 January 2026 Entry Time: 16:45 Exit Time: 16:49 Location: Sobeys penhorn, Dartmouth, NS B2Y 4B1 LPO: Wahegurupal Singh  Details: A male individual, approximately 30–38 years old, wearing a light green cap, dark blue jacket, dark blue pants, light blue T-shirt, and shoes, with a brown beard, entered the store and proceeded directly to the meat section.  While observing the individual, I noticed suspicious behavior. I continued to monitor him as he picked up chicken box valued at approximately $21. After noticing my presence, the individual returned the items to the shelf and immediately exited the store without making a purchase. The proof video of the incident has been saved .  No merchandise was taken from the store.  Action Taken: Subject was observed and monitored until exit. No further action required.

Store: Sobeys at Robie St Store No.: 594 LPO: Amanpreet Singh  Incident Report While patrolling inside the store, the writer observed two male individuals standing in the deli section with a fully loaded shopping cart. Both individuals appeared to be approximately 35–40 years of age, medium build, with white skin tone. Descriptions: 	•	Male #1: Light grey Reebok hoodie, black hat 	•	Male #2: Dark grey hoodie, black woolen cap, glasses resting on head The male wearing the dark grey hoodie was identified as Andrew Penny, a known repeat offender of theft at Pictou County Sobeys. Previous police involvement includes: 	•	Police File Number: 20251897221 	•	Attending Officer: Officer Reid 	•	Badge Number: 206 	•	Additional File Number: 20251748694 While under observation, the suspects were continuously looking toward the entrance doors. Due to their suspicious behavior, the writer maintained surveillance. When another customer entered the store, both suspects exited through the main entrance with fully loaded shopping carts without making any attempt to pay for the merchandise. Both male individuals exited the store together at approximately 1533 hours. Upon exiting, the writer followed and attempted to stop both individuals. The writer identified himself as a store loss prevention officer. Both individuals failed to comply with verbal commands. The writer was able to stop and hold onto one shopping cart. During this time, the second suspect fled the area by running toward the Boston Pizza store with the remaining cart. As the writer was working alone, only one shopping cart was recovered. Recovered Merchandise Value: 	•	Total value of recovered cart: $469.47 Loss: 	•	Estimated value of unrecovered merchandise: $700.00 Result: The writer successfully recovered merchandise valued at $469.47. Theft occurred involving the remaining items estimated at $700.00. Police were notified; however, no police incident number was provided at the time of this report. The store manager was informed of the incident.

fields.summary: On Sunday, January 11th, 2026, at approximately 1710 hours, the writer Abhaypreet Singh observed a suspicious female wearing a Pink coloured jacket, black pants, pushing a shopping cart in the frozen aisle. The individual proceeded to the snacks section where she was seen openly placing items into the bag from the cart. She later abandoned the cart and exited the store through the self-checkout area without making payment of all the items.   The writer Abhaypreet approached the individual outside the store, identified themselves as Loss Prevention Officers, and requested that she either return all unpaid merchandise or pay for the items. She complied and paid for all the unpaid items.  A verbal PPA (Private Property Act notice) was issued, and the individual was banned from the premises. MOD Drew was informed of the situation and requested to obtain an image of the suspect for future reference.  Recovered Merchandise: The following items were successfully recovered with a total value of $65.02:  	•	1 × Paprika Smoked- $11.99 each 	•	1 × Sesame Oil- $6.99 each 	•	2 × Tahini Golden- $8.49 each 	•	1 × Eggplant Roasted- $3.99 each 	•	1 × Vitamins B12- $21.99 each    Total Recovered Value: $65.02  Action Taken: 	•	All items were successfully recovered and returned to the store. 	•	Verbal PPA issued; suspect banned from store property. 	•	Incident details and suspect image to be reviewed and documented for reference. 	•	MOD Drew notified immediately after the incident.

While on patrol in the Sobeys parking lot at Aberdeen Business Centre at approximately 1:20 PM, I received a call from staff reporting that a male individual with a large amount of belongings was hiding inside the male washroom. I immediately attended the location along with a maintenance staff member. We knocked on the washroom door and requested the individual to come out. After multiple requests, the individual exited the washroom carrying a large amount of personal belongings. A female individual also exited the washroom at the same time. Both individuals are known to be frequently seen in the area and appeared to be homeless. They were advised and warned not to use the washroom in this manner again. After the warning, both individuals left the Aberdeen Business Centre without further incident

They then proceeded to the cashier, paid for the remaining items, and exited the store. At 6:36 PM, a female customer came to the office and reported that while she was purchasing a donation ticket, someone took her shopping cart.  Management reviewed the CCTV footage. However, after reviewing the footage a second time, the individual’s walking pattern and behavior, along with another female waiting for her outside the store, indicated the cart was taken intentionally

fields.summary: Loss Prevention Officer Baljeet observed a suspicious white male, approximately 37 years old, around 6 feet tall, wearing a white cap, white jacket, and blue jeans, in the meat department placing pork into his cart, after which he proceeded to the frozen food section and added ice cream to the cart. Officer Baljeet maintained continuous observation of the suspect as he moved throughout the store, and at 5:15 PM, the suspect exited the main entrance without paying for any merchandise. Officer Baljeet approached the suspect outside, identifying himself as Loss Prevention Officer Baljeet, and informed him that he had not paid for the items. The suspect initially claimed that he had forgotten his wallet and then stated that he did not want the merchandise. Officer Baljeet offered the suspect the opportunity to return inside the store to complete payment, which he refused, and he left the premises in a white Hyundai Elantra with license plate HNA669. Following this, Officer Baljeet issued a six-month store ban to the suspect and contacted the RCMP via the non-emergency number to file a theft complaint regarding the incident.
fields.merchandise_description: Pork, bacon , steak , ice creeam , mashroom , energy drinks , diapers, fruits , vegetable

fields.summary: A leak has been identified in the men's restroom on the second floor of Founders Square. Security contacted on-call maintenance personnel, Rutvik Patel, and provided photographic documentation of the leak.  Update: With the approval of the on-call maintenance , at approximately 6:57, security placed an "out of order" notice on the affected fixture and positioned a bucket beneath the leak.

The same female customer who previously stole almond milk from NFLD Coleman’s returned to the store today. She was carrying two large black bags and was wearing a brown coat and blue jeans.  Upon entering the store, she purchased some groceries and then began roaming around the store. I maintained observation during this time. She then made another small purchase and appeared to be attempting to avoid me and get out of my sight.  When I noticed that she was watching me, I repositioned myself to avoid direct visibility. After that, she returned to the store for a third time and went to the produce section, where she attempted to select grapes. Once she noticed me again, she immediately left the store

At approximately 5:00 PM, the suspect later identified as the accused, was observed in the store acting suspiciously. The woman was wearing a mask covering her face and head, blue jacket, white trouser, white sneakers which raised initial concerns. Given her unusual attire, I began to follow her through the store. The accused proceeded to pick up several items, including medicines from the pharmacy section, bread from the dairy section, and other miscellaneous products. Despite the obvious observation, she continued to place the items into her cart without attempting to pay for them. As the woman made her way toward the exit, I attempted to stop her. However, she ignored the request and walked briskly out of the store, heading toward Victoria Road. Despite my attempts to intervene, she kept moving without engaging, continuing on her path. The suspect did not stop when confronted and was last seen moving away from the store. LP called police immediately and reported the incident. Management was notified.

The individual proceeded to the snacks section, where he was observed placing a chocolate bar into his jacket pocket before exiting the store without paying for the item.  The writer, Abhaypreet, approached the individual outside the store, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and requested that the unpaid merchandise be returned or paid for. The individual complied and returned the item.  A verbal PPA (Private Property Act) notice was issued, and the individual was banned from the premises. MOD Patrick was informed of the incident and requested to obtain an image of the suspect for future reference.  Recovered Merchandise:  The following item was recovered with a total value of $3.41:  1 × KitKat chocolate — $3.41 Total Recovered Value: $3.41  Action Taken:  Unpaid merchandise was successfully recovered and returned to the store. Verbal PPA issued; individual banned from store property. Incident details and suspect image to be reviewed and documented. MOD Patrick notified immediately following the incident.

While patrolling the store, I observed a Black male wearing a white hoodie, grey pants, and a black jacket, carrying a backpack. The subject was approximately 5’9” in height and was located in the pet aisle with a half-filled shopping cart. I initiated surveillance and followed the subject throughout the store. The subject continued adding merchandise until the cart was full and appeared aware of my presence, as he became visibly nervous. He then increased his pace and began walking throughout the store without a clear shopping pattern. The subject proceeded to the cashier area and remained there for approximately 2–3 minutes. I positioned myself nearby and maintained observation. Suddenly, the subject attempted to bypass customers and exit the store without paying. I followed him through another checkout lane and stopped him inside the store by securing the cart. I informed the subject that he was banned from the store and not permitted on the premises. While exiting the store, the subject removed one unit of butter valued at $5.99 from the cart. I attempted to recover the item; however, the subject pushed my hand away and fled the store with the merchandise.  The subject appeared familiar but was not immediately recognizable. Upon review, I believe he was previously banned on December 24. I provided all details to the Front End Manager, who will verify the subject’s identity with the Store Manager. Police involvement will be initiated once confirmation is completed.

Exit time: 6:00 pm Description: white male, in his 30s, wore light  brown jacket with black pants and black toque. He has black bag pack as well.

At approximately 2240 hrs, the writer saw a Caucasian male wearing black hoodie, black pants and black shoes with a meat products going towards the natural foods. The male went to aisle 6 where he concealed the product under his jacket. After that the male went towards self checkout and exited the store without paying for anything. The male cooperated and left the product at a table outside the store. The writer took the product back to the store and gave it back to the staff.  Total apprehension 22$

At approximately 3:00 PM, while the writer was patrolling inside the store and moving through the produce section, the writer and one of the store employees observed a middle-aged white female, average build, approximately 5’8” in height, wearing a black hat, black jacket over a grey hoodie, a purple muffler, grey pants, and shoes, and carrying a side bag. The female was seen taking a large wooden flower box, which is store property, and exiting the store through the main entrance without paying for any items.  When writer saw her while taking exit then writer also went behind her.At that time,the female returned inside the store holding a price tag in her hand and walked toward the self-checkout area. When she was going towards the self checkout area, she told the employee who was just behind the writer, “These are my items.” The employee then informed the Deli Manager, Ben, about the situation, while the writer stayed near the items to ensure they were not removed.  Manager Ben approached the female and asked if she needed any help. She replied yes and stated that she was trying to contact for a taxi. Ben then asked her for her name, but she did not respond and walked back outside the store. Ben then came to the writer and asked for the full explanation of what had happened and about the items involved. While the writer was showing Ben the wooden flower box, till that time female returned again and stated, “These are my items and I am waiting for a taxi.”  Ben asked her to provide a receipt, but she again stated only that the items were hers and did not show any proof of purchase. At that point, the writer informed her clearly that he personally witnessed her taking the items and leaving the store without paying. She then stated that her bag was hers, but the writer advised her that he clearly saw the wooden box in her hands when she was going from store to lobby area by entrance.  The writer then asked her either to show a receipt, complete the purchase, or return the items. The female replied that she was not going to purchase the items because she had forgotten her wallet and only had $15 with her. She then voluntarily showed the contents of her bag to the writer and stated that the items inside were purchased from Dollarama. Shortly afterward, she left the store in a hurry without completing any purchase.The female exited the store at approximately 3:04 PM. The receipt includes the following items: Baking Soda priced at $1.69; SS Top Sirloin Roast at $29.05; two FS Lean Ground Beef items priced at $15.74 and $15.25; two Bacon Maple Flavour items at $7.49 each; two Wieners Regular items at $5.99 each; Haddock Fillets (TP) priced at $10.30 and $7.34; Haddock Fillets (IP) at $6.11; Winter Heather at $9.99; two Tropical Zodiac Premium plants at $7.99 each; and one African Violet priced at $4.50, bringing the total purchase amount to $156.28 including tax.

On Tuesday, January 13th, 2026, at approximately 1230 hours, the writer, Abhaypreet Singh, observed a suspicious juvenile walking through the aisles. The individual was described as having a white complexion, slim build, blond hair, approximately 3 feet in height, wearing all black clothing, and believed to be around 12 years of age. While the writer was passing nearby, the juvenile shouted from behind and began making inappropriate comments. When the writer asked if there was a problem, the juvenile stated that he was speaking to his friend. The writer disengaged and began walking away. At that time, the juvenile stated, “I will call 20 people on you.” The writer asked if this was a threat, to which the juvenile replied yes and then fled the area.  At approximately 1245 hours, the juvenile returned to the property accompanied by a group of approximately 8–10 individuals and resumed the same behavior. He continued to disrespect and harass the writer. The writer reported the situation to the Manager on Duty (MOD), Jamie. As a result, the writer and MOD Jamie approached the juvenile in Aisle 9. The juvenile continued making inappropriate comments toward both the writer and the MOD ‘you are goose’, causing a disturbance and refusing to leave the store. As a result, he was issued a six-month ban from the property.  While exiting the store, the juvenile continued making threats and inappropriate comments toward the writer. The writer and MOD Jamie disengaged once the individual left the store.  This report is submitted for your information.  Abhaypreet Singh

While conducting patrols at approximately 5:30 p.m., I observed two individuals, one male and one female, approximately 25 years old, shopping together. The male was wearing a white toque, black jacket, and black jeans, and the female was wearing a long black coat and blue jeans. Both individuals appeared nervous and displayed suspicious behavior. I began observing them as they repeatedly approached the front exit without completing a purchase. Upon noticing my presence, they exited the store. I secured the shopping cart they had been using and brought it to the cashier for receipt verification while store management and I monitored surveillance from the office. Shortly after, a third individual entered the store, a male wearing a black jacket, black pants, and black toque, who appeared to be associated with the first two individuals. He pretended to shop while repeatedly scanning the front exit. I maintained focus on the front door, at which point the individual exited the store with a full cart of groceries. He stated that one bag of items had been brought in from outside, and a verbal intervention was initiated.

At approximately 5:10 PM, the suspect later identified as the accused, was observed in the store acting suspiciously. The woman was wearing a mask covering her face and head, black jacket, white trouser, black sneakers which raised initial concerns. Given her unusual attire, I began to follow her through the store. The accused proceeded to pick up several items, including medicines from the pharmacy section, bread from the dairy section, and other miscellaneous products. Despite the obvious observation, she continued to place the items into her cart without attempting to pay for them. As the woman made her way toward the exit, I attempted to stop her. However, she ignored the request and walked briskly out of the store, heading toward Victoria Road. Despite my attempts to intervene, she kept moving without engaging, continuing on her path. The suspect did not stop when confronted and was last seen moving away from the store. LP called police immediately and reported the incident. Management was notified.

On February 9th, 2026 at approximately 1438 hours the writer Bailiff Brennan McGinnis-Morton successfully served Kenneth Howe his NSPHA lease violation letter. The writer approached Kenneth's open door where Kenneth was standing. Kenneth responded to his name and took his letter without any issues.

Two girls were in the store for approximately one hour. One was wearing a red cropped hooded jacket and red sweatpants and carrying a black backpack.
