They initially picked up two deli meat meals each costing around $8.72 and proceeded to the back aisles, where they opened a backpack. When I approached, they zipped it up. As I continued to follow them through the aisles, they returned one of the meals to the shelf and then went back to the rear aisles again.  I attempted to follow them once more but lost continuity when another customer delayed my movement. By the time I reached the area again, they may have already placed the chicken meal into the backpack, resulting in a loss of continuity on the second item.

Security Incident Report Store Name: Sobeys at Prince St Store No.: 871 Date: 14 January 2026 LPO: Amanpreet Singh During routine patrol inside the store, the writer observed one female individual enter the store at 1914 hrs. Description of Individual White female Large build Curly dark brown hair Wearing a black hoodie, black jacket, black jeans, and black shoes Carrying a green handbag The individual first proceeded to the seafood section, where the writer observed her select and conceal two (2) packs of lobster claws, valued at $30 each. She then headed to the pharmacy section, where she concealed both packs inside her green handbag. Afterward, the individual proceeded to the midsection, where she concealed one (1) chicken breast, valued at $30. It was later observed that she placed the chicken breast inside her jacket. The individual then moved to Aisle 2, where she concealed one (1) sauce bottle, valued at $5. She also concealed additional items from Aisle 2; however, those items could not be clearly identified on CCTV footage. The writer maintained continuous surveillance on the individual. At approximately 1922 hrs, the individual passed through cash counter #5 and exited the store without making any attempt to pay for the concealed items. The writer followed the individual outside the store and verbally advised her to stop. The writer identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer working for Sobeys. The individual refused to comply and continued walking. The writer was unable to recover the stolen items as the individual was holding her bag and began running toward the DQ store. The writer verbally banned the individual from the store and immediately contacted police. The store manager was informed of the incident. CCTV footage was saved under the file name “White Woman – 14 Jan – $100” for further investigation. Result Theft occurred with merchandise valued at approximately $100 No recovery of items Police were informed CCTV footage saved for further investigation Report completed by: Amanpreet Singh Loss Prevention Officer   Police file no- 2026-62355 Officer- kelly quinn

At approximately 1031 hours, S/O Sunpreet observed an unidentified male sleeping inside the building lobby. The male was unresponsive when verbally addressed and appeared to be under the influence of alcohol or other intoxicants. Due to the individual’s condition and failure to respond, S/O Sunpreet contacted non-emergency police for assistance. However, police did not arrive during the initial period after the call, and the matter was escalated to M/S.  The writer arrived on site at approximately 1131 hours and observed that police had since arrived. After approximately 15–20 minutes, the male regained consciousness. Police officers assessed the individual and confirmed that he was intoxicated. With police presence, the writer and police escorted the male out of the building lobby without further incident. The male complied with directions, and no damage or injuries were reported. The area was secured afterward, and normal operations resumed.

At approximately 1713 hrs, LPO noticed a suspicious male who was trying to pay for milk, banana and bread at checkout 5 but was hiding meat and other groceries in his black bag in a cart costing approximately $100+. LPO asked the male if he is going to pay for the other items in the bag. The male informed LPO that his friend is sending him money and then he will pay. LPO stayed there in case the male doesn’t run away with a bag. The male then tried to run away with a bag and LPO tried to stop the cart. The male became aggressive as LPO stopped the cart and tried to attack LPO. Some items fallen on the floor as the male tried to snatch the bag. LPO resisted but The male tried to pull out knife from his jacket to intimidate the LPO. LPO noticed a black colour back handle of knife from the male’s jacket so LPO stayed back and didn’t follow the male. LPO immediately called 911 as the male can be dangerous towards other customers. Upon checking the cameras, LPO noticed that the male left from exit towards McDonald’s side at approximately 1716 hrs. LPO was able to recover items costing $ 36.69 cad with taxes. The items include, bathroom cleaner $4.50, margarine pack $9.49, 1% milk pack $7.29, pot patties $8.79,  wieners sausages $5.99 CAD. The male left banana, dempsters bread pack and milk on checkout 5. LPO provided CCTV Photos of the male to police and informed to police to trespass the male from the store and press charges. The police gave the email to provide LPO’s personal statement and left at approximately 1735 hrs. LPO filled Sobeys reporting system form and sent personal statement to police officer. LPO also reported this incident to manager Branon and sent CCTV photos of the male for his reference.   The description of the male includes,  Caucasian male, in his 40s, black and green jacket, black and crème colour winter trapper hat, dark blue jeans, grey t shirt, slim build, grey beard, height approximately 5’3.

The police officer entered in the store at approximately 1510 hrs and LPO took the police officer in aisle 2 where the female was concealing items. On 15th January 2026, Thursday the writer started exterior parking patrol at around 0008 hours. While patrolling, the writer saw a BLACK Ford Pick truck with no number plate waiting in the residential parking lot ( upper level) with head lights on and the individual sitting inside the car was smoking ( Description: White skinny male with a small tattoo on the left cheek). At around 0010 hours writer approached the individual sitting in the car and the moment writer approached the car , the individual sitting in the car opened the window and told the writer not to do anything because he is waiting for someone. The writer told him that if he wants to wait for someone he needs to be at the front. The individual told the writer that the person is not a tenant at Stoneybrook apartments and the person he is waiting for is gonna come from the back (back of the residential parking lot).The individual seems to be high on marijuana and asked the writer to give him 5 minutes. The writer completed the parking patrol and waited in the parking lot to make sure to see the individual leaving the property. After waiting for 10-15 minutes , writer approached the individual again at around 0028 hours and the individual asked the writer to give him five more minutes . Then around 0030 hours, an old lady who is a tenant at the Stoneybrook apartments also noticed that car and asked me if I need any help. The writer said to the lady that she is okay and will handle the situation. The lady insisted and waited for the writer in the parking lot. Then at around 1231 hours, writer approached the individual again and told him that now he needs to leave, he cannot wait here because this is not a public parking. The individual started arguing and said “ I am waiting for my girlfriend, I cannot leave her . What am I supposed to do. She is not picking up my call. I don’t know which house is hers.” After this conversation writer told the individual to leave. The individual looked upset and angry after hearing the writer. At approximately 0033 hours, individual left the parking lot and was off property. The writer reported this incident to the Supervisor Chirag at 0035 hours. All in order.

At approximately 1200 hrs, the writer arrived at 2313 gottingen st, Halifax, NS, B3K 3B8. The writer knocked at apartment door 982 multiple times. But got no answer. The writer stationed there around 10-15 mins. But no one opened the door. No further step taken by the writer.

14th Jan- These 2 teenagers stole 1 chocolate bar each. The writer approached them when they exited. The teenagers said that they did not take anything. The writer told them that there is a video of them showing the concealment but they ran towards the parking lot then to the back of the store. The writer told the manager about the incident. 15th Jan- The individual wearing a black hoodie, identified as the same juvenile involved in a theft incident the previous night, was observed in the store today accompanied by his father. The writer informed the store manager of the prior theft and advised that it was at the store’s discretion whether to confront the individual.  The manager subsequently approached and informed the juvenile’s father regarding the incident. The father became agitated and demanded to view video footage of the theft. The manager declined the request in accordance with company policy and contacted the police.  Upon arrival, the police were shown the video footage by the writer, which clearly depicted the juvenile committing the theft. As a result, the police issued a one (1) year ban to the juvenile from the Stellarton store only.  The family refused to provide identifying information to both store staff and police; therefore, the juvenile’s name remains unknown. Sergeant Paul Veenhuis attended and handled the matter on site.  Nothing further to report.

fields.summary: The writer was patrolling the store when, at approximately 3:20 PM, a Black male, approximately 5 feet 5 inches tall, wearing a red jacket, entered the store. The individual proceeded to roam around the back aisles, where chips and frozen items are located. The writer kept the individual under observation and witnessed him place one bag of Lay’s chips valued at $3.50 into his bag. The cashier then notified the manager of the suspicious activity. The individual also attempted to take M&M candy packets, but was unsuccessful as both the manager and the writer were already monitoring him. Later, the individual approached the manager and asked if he could keep the M&M candies, stating that they were expiring soon. The manager verified that the candies were not expiring until September and refused his request. After this, the individual left the store, placing all items back on the counter except for one pack of Lay’s chips, which he stolen before exiting the store. However, writer didn’t stopped the guy because it’s just cost $3.50 and manager didn’t want me to stop him.  No further incidents occurred.

On Thursday, at approximately 1800 hours, Bailiff Officer Abhaypreet Singh arrived at 3370 Barrington Street.  At approximately 1806 hours, the Bailiff Officer knocked on the door of the above-mentioned address. A white female, approximately 50 years of age, medium build, wearing a pink T-shirt and black pants, responded to the name Kimberley Briand. The Bailiff Officer served the NSPHA evidence package to Ms. Briand, which she accepted without hesitation.

On the mentioned date, the writer observed a male customer wearing a red cap, black jacket, and black pants, approximately 5 feet 8 inches tall, inside the store. The customer was carrying a green Sobeys reusable bag. The writer began monitoring the individual due to suspicious behavior. The customer spent approximately 20 minutes in the store selecting various grocery items, including produce, bakery items, and one carton of chocolate milk. During this time, the writer observed the customer placing multiple unpaid items into his Sobeys bag. The customer then proceeded to the self-checkout area around 9:55 am. At the checkout, he only scanned and paid for the carton of chocolate milk and attempted to leave the area. Upon noticing the writer, the customer stopped. The writer approached the customer and informed him that there was unpaid merchandise in his bag. The customer cooperated and agreed to return the bag to the writer without resistance. A verbal warning was issued to the customer. According to the cashier, the individual is a regular customer. Upon reviewing the unpaid merchandise, the total value of the items was calculated to be $37.62. No further action was taken, and the incident was resolved peacefully.

The writer was monitoring a Black male wearing a green jacket and black pants from the time he entered the store. The individual was observed concealing two packets of meat and two bottles of drinks inside his pockets. At approximately 13:42, the individual exited the store without paying for the concealed items. The writer approached the individual and informed him that the items had not been paid for. The individual refused to return the merchandise or make payment and continued to leave the premises. The writer issued a verbal warning and informed the individual that he was banned from the store. The total value of the unpaid merchandise was approximately $25. No further incidents occurred.

A white Canadian, husky, middle-aged male was wearing a blue Puma hoodie, a blue-and-red coloured toque, black trousers, and black Under Armour sneakers with white soles. At approximately 15:20, while he was exiting the store after paying for items, the anti-theft system sounded. He did not stop when the alarm activated. The writer approached the individual and requested him to stop. The suspect asked the writer what he meant, and the writer explained that there might be an item for which the cashier forgot to deactivate the security tag. The cashier then informed the writer that the suspect did not have any items with security tags. The writer first checked the suspect’s bag  with his permission and found nothing inside. When the writer requested that the suspect walk through the system again without any items, the suspect made excuses but eventually complied, and the system alarmed again. At that point, the suspect stated that he had a bar inside his hoodie for which he had forgotten to pay. He apologized and paid for the item. The suspect then left the store at approximately 15:23. The total value of the unpaid item was approximately $6 including tax.

On Sunday, at approximately 1300 hours, the writer Abhaypreet Singh observed a suspicious female wearing a black jacket, medical face mask, and black pants pushing a shopping cart and carrying a handbag. The individual proceeded to the meat section, bakery, and natural resources section, where she was observed openly placing items into her cart. The writer continued surveillance as the individual moved through various aisles and attempted to avoid the writer. She appeared aware of the writer’s presence and made racist comments toward the writer; however, continuity was maintained at all times, and the individual was not given an opportunity to conceal any items. At the end, she abandoned the cart at the cashier counter and exited the store without taking any merchandise.  Description – White female, late 30s, medium build, blonde hair, visibly dirty and infected hands.  The writer informed the incident to MOD Garron and returned the items to the cash supervisor.  The items were successfully recovered with a total value of $362.37.  Action Taken: All items were successfully recovered and returned to the store. Incident details and suspect image to be reviewed and documented for reference. MOD Garron notified immediately after the incident.

The writer was doing patrol around 16:56 and saw a suspect wearing a light khaki jacket over a hoodie. He was carrying a side cloth bag of the same color and was wearing pants. He was a tall, thin white male with a brown beard. This individual is already banned from the store due to multiple previous incidents. The suspect entered the store and went to the frozen section. The writer recognized the suspect from prior incidents, so he began observing him. While in the frozen section, the suspect took one Pork Gyoza pack, valued at approximately $7. At that time, the writer was nearby. When the suspect exited the store without paying for the item, the writer approached him outside and recovered the merchandise. The writer then obtained a void receipt from Manager Chirag. The suspect left the store at approximately 17:00.

Two teenagers entered the store together. Suspect 1 selected two packs of canned drinks, while Suspect 2 selected four packs of energy drinks. Both suspects then moved through different aisles and appeared to attempt concealment. They separated while inside the store.  I began observing Suspect 1; however, he did not successfully conceal any merchandise. Suspect 2 later approached without any items in his hands. I observed Suspect 2 concealing canned drinks inside his hoodie pocket, which was clearly visible. When Suspect 2 noticed that he was being observed, he removed the concealed items and placed them back in the natural location before exiting the store.  Suspect 1 continued walking through multiple aisles and later returned the energy drinks to the drink aisle. He then exited the store without any merchandise. Exit time: 4:15 pm Description: Suspect 1: White male, wearing an all-black tracksuit, carrying a black backpack. Suspect 2: White male, wearing a skinny brown tracksuit, carrying a black backpack.

At approximately 3:00 a.m., a male and a female entered the store. Around 3:32 a.m., the male kicked the female, causing her to be pushed into the wall, which resulted in damage to the wall.  While I was speaking with both individuals, the male suddenly ran away from the scene. The female appeared to be heavily intoxicated and was unable to communicate clearly or understand what was happening. She later took food and left the premises on her own.  I informed all staff working at Fat Bastard Burrito about the incident and showed them the damaged wall. I also requested images or video footage of the male involved. Staff advised that they would request the footage from the manager and forward it to me once available.

Incident Report  Date: January 12 Time: Approximately 1835 hours Location: Sobeys Westside Reporting officer- Rhythm Oberoi Merchandise recovered-$113.64  At approximately 1835 hours on January 12, a white male about 40years old wearing a red checkered jacket brown pants black shoes and a grey hat entered Sobeys Westside. The writer observed the individual behaving suspiciously and began monitoring him.  The suspect proceeded through the produce section and then entered the meat aisle, where he selected two (2) packages of haddock fillets and three (3) packages of Sterling Silver Beef Rib Steaks. Continuous observation was maintained as the suspect moved into Aisle 7 and subsequently into the frozen aisle.  From a position not visible to the suspect, the writer observed him conceal all three (3) steak packages inside his jacket. The suspect then proceeded toward the store exit. The writer followed closely and, upon the suspect exiting the vestibule, approached him and identified himself as the Loss Prevention Officer on duty.  The writer instructed the suspect to return the concealed merchandise and advised that police would be contacted if he failed to comply. The suspect apologized and returned one (1) package, then attempted to walk toward the parking lot. The writer instructed the suspect to stop and return the remaining items. The suspect immediately complied by returning the remaining two (2) packages and apologized again.  The writer instructed the suspect to follow him back into the store (for a PPA) however, the suspect refused and fled toward the parking lot. The writer secured the recovered merchandise and returned to the store. Store management was notified of the incident.  All merchandise was fully recovered with a total value of $113.64. No police were contacted. There was no loss to the store.  Nothing further to report.

Date January 16, Friday. At approximately 2020 hours, the writer observed a suspicious male inside the store. The subject was pushing a shopping cart full of grocery items and appeared to be attempting to exit the store through the main entrance without proceeding to checkout.  Subject Description (from observation and image): 	•	Gender: Male 	•	Race: White 	•	Approximate Age: 30–40 years 	•	Height: Approximately 5’9”–6’0” 	•	Build: Slim 	•	Clothing: Black quilted jacket, grey sweatpants, dark-colored baseball cap with a red “B” logo, dark footwear 	•	Accessories: Backpack; holding a mobile phone in hand  As the subject approached the entrance, he appeared to notice the writer, who had earlier moved toward the entrance area. The subject then stopped and did not exit, appearing cautious.  At approximately 2032 hours, the subject proceeded to Cash 3, abandoned the cart containing unpaid merchandise, and exited the store without making any attempt to pay. The writer successfully recovered unpaid grocery and produce items valued at $470.16.  Additionally, the writer observed another female leaving from the Cash 3 area with full grocery bags. Based on timing and behavior, it is suspected that she may have been associated with the male subject and possibly waiting for him outside, though no direct interaction was observed.  The Manager on Duty (MOD), Chris, was informed of the incident. The MOD instructed the writer to contact police and file a report. Police were subsequently notified.  For additional information or access to surveillance footage, please contact: Store Manager: Craig Location: Sobeys – Queen Street Phone: 902-422-9884 Email: 00574mgr@sobeys.com

Altercation and De-escalation (1:12 AM) At approximately 1:12 AM, residents ED257 and CP357 were observed arguing verbally in the lobby area. As I was positioned in the lobby at the time, I immediately intervened to prevent further escalation. I spoke calmly to both parties and successfully de-escalated the situation without the need for physical intervention. Shortly after, FC Sherlyn, FC John, and FC Shelby arrived on scene for support. SK250 was also present and began making unrelated and disruptive comments that appeared to escalate tensions. FC Sherlyn confronted SK250 and instructed her to stop, which stabilized the situation. Following this, ED257 exited the building to smoke and later departed the property in a cab, while CP357 returned to his assigned room. The area was monitored closely, and no further verbal or physical altercations were observed between the involved parties for the remainder of the shift

Incident Report –Apprehension   Date: January 12 Time: Approximately 2035 hrs Location: Sobeys Westside Reporting Officer:Rhythm Oberoi Recovered Merchandise- $192.44  At approximately 2035 hrs, a white female entered Sobeys Westside wearing a red jacket, grey hoodie, grey sweatpants, and black shoes. The suspect was carrying a black reusable bag. The writer found the suspect suspicious due to her manner of walking and suspected she may have been under the influence of alcohol. The writer began monitoring the suspect upon entry.  The suspect proceeded to the bakery aisle, where she selected two boxes of donuts and cakes. She then moved through various aisles selecting multiple grocery items, including toilet paper, cereal, milk, and frozen food items. The writer informed a store employee of the situation, and both the writer and the employee maintained continuous observation of the suspect.  While in aisle 1, the suspect concealed one box of donuts into her black reusable bag, which the writer clearly observed. The suspect then began walking rapidly toward the exit. The writer followed the suspect, and as she attempted to exit through the store vestibule, the writer and the store manager approached her. The manager held the cart from the suspect. The writer identified himself as the Loss Prevention Officer on duty and informed the suspect that she had not paid for her merchandise.  The suspect did not resist or retaliate and immediately abandoned the shopping cart. The writer requested that the suspect return inside the store, which she agreed to do. Inside the store, the writer requested the suspect’s identification; however, she only provided her first name as “Jessica” and refused to provide any further information. The suspect then began crying and left the premises voluntarily. The writer did not pursue her further and secured the abandoned cart.  The store manager was present throughout the incident. The total value of the recovered merchandise was $192.44.   All items were fully recovered, and there was no loss to the store. Police were not contacted. A suspect photograph could not be obtained as the store camera system was not functioning at the time of the incident.  Nothing further to report.  Reporting Officer: Rhythm Oberoi

fields.summary: Resident Movement, Vehicle Concern, and RNC Notification (1:20 AM – 1:33 AM) At approximately 1:20 AM, residents CD370, HW362, and KL344 were observed leaving the premises via cab. At approximately 1:33 AM, the same residents returned to the property in a white Dodge Durango, which raised concern due to prior activity. I immediately informed FC Sherlyn of the vehicle and resident involvement. FC Sherlyn contacted the RNC, provided vehicle details, and shared possible locations where the vehicle might be located. In response, the RNC increased patrol presence in the surrounding area. I also notified the patrol vehicle with all available information. Due to increased activity and monitoring requirements, I relocated my post from Door 2 to the area above Door 3 to maintain better visibility and control. Upon re-entry, CD370, HW362, and KL344 proceeded to unit 370, and an increase in resident hallway traffic was observed and monitored to ensure safety and compliance.

fields.summary: The writer was on her break when Supervisor Komal contacted security after observing two individuals involved in suspected theft. According to the supervisor, one suspect was a male approximately 5 feet 5 inches tall, wearing all black clothing. The second suspect was a female of similar height, wearing a pink jacket and black pants. The supervisor observed the female conceal unpaid merchandise inside her jacket, while the male was carrying a black backpack containing unpaid merchandise and attempting to leave the store. When both suspects attempted to exit the store, Supervisor Komal approached and attempted to stop them. The writer was present at the time and assisted as backup. The black backpack was successfully retrieved from the male suspect. However, the unpaid merchandise concealed by the female suspect could not be recovered. Due to the inability to retrieve all unpaid merchandise, Supervisor Komal instructed the writer to contact the police. The writer contacted the police, and a file number 26-9240 was provided.

Incident Report –Apprehension   Date: January 19, 2026 Time: Approximately 1445 hrs Location: Sobeys Stellarton Reporting Officer:Rhythm Oberoi Recovery value- $104.11  On January 19, 2026, at approximately 1445 hrs, a white female entered Sobeys Stellarton wearing a pink vest, grey top, black tights, black beanie, and black boots. The suspect had lip piercings and was carrying a green reusable bag. Based on her behavior and appearance, the writer deemed the individual suspicious and began monitoring her.  The suspect initially proceeded to the bakery aisle, where she selected white bread, tortillas, and muffins. She then moved to the deli section and picked up one cold sandwich. Afterward, the suspect entered the frozen foods department and selected multiple items, including chicken wings and mac and cheese.  The writer maintained continuous and clear observation of the suspect from a distance without alerting her. The suspect later moved into aisles 2 and 3, where she concealed all selected items into her green reusable bag, leaving the white bread and two other items in the shopping cart.  While in aisle 2, the suspect appeared to wait for the store entrance to clear before attempting to exit. The writer maintained uninterrupted observation and allowed the suspect to proceed. Once the entrance was clear, the suspect abandoned the shopping cart, picked up her green bag along with a pack of toilet paper, and exited the store.  As the suspect stepped out of the vestibule, the writer approached her, identified himself as the Loss Prevention Officer on duty, and instructed her to return the unpaid merchandise. The suspect initially failed to comply and continued toward the parking lot. The writer warned the suspect that police would be contacted immediately if she did not stop and return the merchandise. At that point, the suspect stopped, apologized, and returned all unpaid items.  The writer secured all recovered merchandise and observed the suspect enter a black GMC Terrain with another female occupant. The vehicle’s license plate number was recorded as HHA022, and a photograph of the plate was taken. The writer then returned inside the store and notified the manager of the incident.  The manager decided to contact the police. Constable Jordan Wheeler (Badge #169) attended the scene at approximately 1600 hrs and provided file number 2026-83081.  The total value of the recovered merchandise was $104.11. There was no loss to the store, and all items were returned to store inventory.  Nothing further to report.

On Monday, January 19th, 2026, at approximately 1500 hours, the writer, Abhaypreet Singh, observed a suspicious white male wearing a Bell company beanie, a jacket, and black pants. The individual was observed in the snacks aisle selecting multiple chocolate items. The individual was seen looking around before concealing the chocolates inside his jacket pocket.  The writer maintained continuous observation of the individual as he proceeded past the cashier area and exited the store without making payment for the concealed items.  The writer approached the individual outside the store, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and requested the return of all unpaid merchandise. The individual was informed that the incident had been recorded and that police could be contacted. The individual complied and returned all unpaid items.  A verbal PPA (Private Property Act) notice was issued, and the individual was banned from the premises for a period of one month. MOD Matt was informed of the incident and requested to obtain an image of the suspect for future reference.  Recovered Merchandise:  The following items were successfully recovered: 2 × Reese’s chocolates – $2.99 each 1 × KitKat chocolate – $2.99 Total Recovered Value:$10.03  Action Taken:  -All unpaid items were successfully recovered and returned to the store. -A verbal PPA was issued, and the suspect was banned from store property for one month. -Incident details and suspect image to be reviewed and documented for reference. -MOD Matt was notified immediately following the incident.

Incident Report Date: 18 January 2025 Time: Approx. 2034 hrs Location: Sobeys Aberdeen, New Glasgow Store Number: 370 Position: Loss Prevention Officer Reporting Officer: Bhupinder Singh On 18 January 2025, while performing a routine patrol, the writer observed two youths acting suspiciously. The suspects split apart, and the writer followed the individual exhibiting more suspicious behavior. Suspect Description: 	•	Black hoodie 	•	Blue hoodie underneath 	•	Cap on 	•	Blue jeans The suspect entered the pharmacy section and selected a pack of Magnum condoms. The suspect then proceeded to Aisle 4, where he opened the pack and removed the condoms, concealing them in his hoodie pocket. The writer maintained continuous observation and concealment throughout the incident and waited for the suspect to exit the store. The store manager had been informed prior to the suspect’s exit. At approximately 2034 hours, the suspect exited the store without paying for the item. The writer approached the suspect, identified himself as store security, and advised that the item had not been paid for. The suspect complied calmly, handed over the concealed condoms, and was cooperative throughout the interaction. The writer issued a Property Protection Agreement (PPA) for six months and advised the suspect not to return during this period, warning that police involvement would occur if he did. Outcome: 	•	Merchandise recovered: Magnum condoms, $15 	•	No loss to the store 	•	Successful apprehension 	•	Manager informed 	•	Police not involved All 10-4

Incident Report Date: 18 January 2026 Time: 1803 hrs – 1812 hrs Location: Sobeys Aberdeen, New Glasgow Store Number: 370 Position: Loss Prevention Officer Reporting Officer: Bhupinder Singh On 18 January 2026 at approximately 1803 hours, the suspect entered Sobeys Aberdeen, New Glasgow. The suspect was described as a male, approximately 30–32 years of age, wearing a dark blue jacket, black trousers, black cap, black gloves, red shoes, and carrying a large backpack. The writer observed the suspect from the time of entry due to suspicious behavior. Upon entry, the suspect placed his large backpack in the eating area and proceeded directly to Aisle 2. The suspect selected a large pack of batteries valued at $26.20 and concealed the item inside his jacket pocket. The writer maintained continuous observation and concealment throughout the incident. The suspect then proceeded toward Checkout Counter 5, where he selected a packet of chips and paid for the chips only. The batteries were not presented for payment. The writer maintained continuity and waited for the suspect to pass all points of sale and exit the store. At approximately 1812 hours, once the suspect exited the store through the main entrance, the writer approached him, identified himself as store security, and advised the suspect that he failed to pay for the batteries. The suspect remained calm, cooperative, and compliant at all times. The suspect voluntarily handed over the concealed batteries to security. The suspect identified himself as Blake, DOB 16 May 1994, with an address listed as Marsh Street Shelter, New Glasgow. A PPA (Property Protection Agreement) was issued for a duration of 6 months, and the suspect was advised not to return to the store for this period. He was informed that failure to comply would result in police involvement. No loss occurred as the merchandise was fully recovered. Store management was notified of the incident. Police were not involved. All 10-4.

Incident Report Date: 19 January 2026 Time: Entered at approximately 1913 hrs / Exited at approximately 1916 hrs Store: Sobeys Aberdeen, New Glasgow Store Number: 370 Reporting Officer: Bhupinder Singh Position: Loss Prevention Officer Details: On 19 January 2026, at approximately 1913 hrs, the writer was conducting a routine patrol inside Sobeys Aberdeen, Store #370. During the patrol, the writer observed a male suspect with his face partially covered, displaying suspicious behavior. Suspect Description: Gender: Male Age: Approximately 18 years Build: Slim Clothing: White hoodie, blue cap, blue jeans Face: Covered The suspect proceeded to the deli department, where he selected one roll valued at approximately $7.00. The suspect then moved to Natural Resource Aisle 1, where the writer observed the suspect conceal the item inside the front pocket of his hoodie. The writer maintained continuous observation, continuity, and concealment of the suspect throughout the incident. The suspect did not make any attempt to pay for the concealed merchandise and exited the store without rendering payment. Upon exiting the store, the writer immediately approached the suspect outside, identified himself as store security, and requested the suspect to stop. The suspect ignored verbal commands and continued walking toward the NSLC area. The writer advised the suspect that police would be contacted if he failed to stop; however, the suspect continued to flee. As a result, the writer issued a verbal trespass warning for six (6) months, advising the suspect that police would be contacted immediately if he returned to the property. The suspect then left the area. The total loss for this incident is $7.00. The suspect was accompanied by another individuals; photographs of other individuals have been captured and attached. Management was notified and made aware of the incident. No police involvement at this time.

Name: Vishal Mangvana  Position: Loss Prevention Officer (LPO)  Store: Sobeys – Westside  Date: 17 jan 2026  Time: Approximately 3:32 PM While patrolling the sales floor at approximately 3:32 PM, I observed a suspicious female enter the store. The subject selected multiple items from the vestibule area and continued selecting merchandise from various aisles throughout the store. I maintained constant observation and ensured all four elements of theft were met. The subject was wearing a black jacket with a blue hoodie underneath, black pants, a black and camel-colored cap, white and sky-blue shoes, and black gloves. She was also carrying a maroon purse. Around 3:42 The subject proceeded toward the exit and attempted to leave the store through the entrance doors without making any attempt to pay for the merchandise. Once the subject exited the vestibule area, I approached her, identified myself as store security, and requested her identity. At that point, the subject fled the area on foot, abandoning the shopping cart. All merchandise was fully recovered. The total value of the recovered merchandise was $639.91. The Manager on Duty was notified immediately, and police were contacted. Officer Ryan Thibeau attended the scene approximately 10–15 minutes later. The police file number for this incident is 202675287. The suspect’s identity will be shared once confirmed by police.

fields.summary: When the writer was patrolling inside the store, he observed a suspect who is already banned by the writer and store management due to multiple previous incidents. The suspect was identified as Brody Fox. He was wearing a black winter cap under the hood of a grey hoodie, green trousers, and a red scarf around his neck. He was carrying one large grey bag and one cream-coloured bag containing clothing. When the writer first observed him that day, the suspect was walking toward the frozen section, where he selected one sushi pack, valued at approximately $9 before tax. The suspect took the item in front of a store employee and attempted to leave the area without paying. At that time, the writer approached the suspect and requested that he return the item. The suspect initially did not respond, but the writer was eventually able to recover the merchandise.  While the writer was returning to the store, the suspect followed him and began recording a video, stating and blaming that “the writer had manipulated him into stealing”. For personal safety, the writer also began recording. The writer requested that the suspect leave the store immediately. As the suspect was exiting,Store Manager Christine arrived and also asked him to leave. The suspect began yelling at her and used abusive language, stating, “You bitch ruined my lunch.” The suspect then exited the store but returned a few seconds later and continued yelling at Manager Christine. After several minutes, the suspect finally left the store. The writer later filed a police complaint regarding the incident. The incident occurred at approximately 13:25.

At approximately 1325 the writer responded to a call from store manager about a suspicious male in the store. The male was wearing red beanie and black jacket and pants. The male was Caucasian and slim build. The male had 2 big packs of baby diapers and was actively trying to find an exit through the cashiers. The male then spotted writer and the manager watching him which made the male deter the products and leave the store. Total deterrence 30$

Incident Report  Location: Sobeys Aberdeen Date: January 17, 2026 Store Number: 370 LPO Name: Nijo Joy  Time: Approximately 1830 hours  Report:  At approximately 1830 hours, the writer was conducting regular patrol inside the store. During patrol, the writer observed a female individual displaying unusual shopping behavior, which drew the writer’s attention.  The writer began surveillance and followed the individual through multiple areas of the store, including the frozen aisle, pharmacy, and natural health section. The individual selected several items; however, no concealment was observed at that time.  While in the fresh zone area, the writer observed the individual selecting grapes and consuming them while continuing to walk through the store. The writer maintained continuous observation to determine if any concealment or further theft-related activity occurred.  After several minutes, the individual proceeded toward the checkout area. The writer informed the checkout staff of the situation and advised them to contact the manager. The writer explained that grapes are sold by weight and that consuming them prior to checkout could result in underpayment.  The situation was explained to the store manager, who advised the writer to continue close observation. At the conclusion of the transaction, the individual paid for all selected items.  Although no recovery was required, the writer identified the individual for future reference and shared her description with the manager and checkout staff.  Suspect Description: 	•	Gender: Female 	•	Age: Approximately 50–55 years 	•	Build: Slim 	•	Clothing: Navy blue jacket, white pants 	•	Additional Features: Tattoos on arms  Outcome: Subject paid for all items. No further action taken. Individual identified for future reference.  Report Filed By: Nijo Joy Loss Prevention

Store: Sobeys – 985 Prince St. Store No.: 871 Date: 21 January 2026 LPO: Amanpreet Singh Report: Writer started shift at approximately 1600 hours. At approximately 1535 hours, writer was informed by Store Manager Lenny that a theft had occurred. Writer then reviewed CCTV footage to gather details of the incident. Upon review of CCTV footage, writer observed one male individual enter the store at approximately 1516 hours. Description of Individual: Male Brown skin Medium build Approximate height: 5'10" Wearing a black jacket with puffer cap Grey woollen cap Dark grey trousers Black shoes White sunglasses Vehicle: White SUV Writer observed the individual obtain a large shopping cart and begin selecting items throughout the store. The individual filled the cart with various grocery items including fruits, vegetables, tissue paper, and placed multiple meat items into a green Sobeys reusable bag. Items observed being concealed included approximately: 6–7 packages of chicken breast 7–8 beef steaks 7–8 packages of pork Writer observed the individual proceed to the self-checkout area and exit the store without making payment for the concealed items. The individual exited the store at approximately 1535 hours. After exiting, the individual was observed loading the stolen groceries into a white SUV and then leaving the premises. Additional Information: Writer was advised that the same individual was previously involved in a theft valued at approximately $800 at Sobeys located on Larry Uteck Boulevard. CCTV Evidence: CCTV footage related to this incident was saved on the store computer under the file name “Brown Moustache – 21 Jan 2026.” Result: Estimated total value of stolen merchandise is approximately $800. Police were notified regarding the incident. Police incident no- 202693415 Officer- MADELAINE MACDONALD

On Sunday, January 18th, 2026, at approximately 2045 hours, the writer, Abhaypreet Singh, observed a suspicious white-complexioned, medium-build male wearing a black jacket, red hoodie, and black pants. The individual was pushing a shopping cart containing a large quantity of cheese and meat products. The individual was observed attempting to conceal items into a green shopping backpack in the Natural Resources section.  The individual became aware of the writer’s presence and discontinued the concealment attempt. He then began moving through multiple aisles in an apparent attempt to evade observation; however, the writer maintained continuous and close observation, preventing further concealment.  The individual later proceeded toward the cash checkout area but exited the checkout without paying for any items. The writer approached the individual near the exit and escorted him back to the checkout point. The individual paid for one yogurt item only, abandoned the remaining merchandise in the cart, and fled the scene.  The writer notified MOD Gary and returned all recovered merchandise to the store.  **Recovered Items:**  * 3 × Mac & Cheese — $5.00 each * 1 × Salmon — $9.99 * 1 × Salami — $5.29 * 1 × Little Trios — $7.29 * 1 × Yogurt — $3.99 * 2 × Krema Yogurt — $7.49 each * 1 × Cheese — $8.49 * 2 × Ham — $6.79 each * 1 × Loaf — $4.99  **Total Value:** $84.80  **Action Taken:**  * All items were successfully recovered and returned to the store. * Incident details and suspect imagery to be reviewed and documented for future reference. * MOD Gary was notified immediately following the incident.

NFLD COLEMANS After returning from my break, I resumed regular patrol duties. During patrol, I observed a female customer wearing a red coat and black pants, accompanied by her two children, arguing with a bakery employee. I remained at a distance and continued to monitor her behavior.  At approximately 16:10, the store manager, Kay, informed me that a male and a female were standing near the liquor store with a cart containing groceries and that there was a concern they might exit without paying. When the individuals attempted to leave the store, I stopped them and asked if they had paid for the groceries. They stated that the items were purchased at Sobeys. As I had not observed them taking any merchandise from our store, I allowed them to leave.  During this time, the female customer I had been monitoring earlier exited the store without paying. I followed her into the parking lot and asked her to pay for the items she had taken from the store. She showed me a receipt for potato chips and fresh-cut food but did not mention the cake.  The manager obtained the phone number and email address she had previously provided to order the cake. The manager contacted her and requested payment for the cake. After some time, the female returned to the store and paid for the cake

She was followed for a period of time; however, the writer did not have full confirmation of theft at that moment. Reporting Officer Kamaljit Singh  Time 0050 Hrs  Location Amos Jewelry Shop Around 0050 hrs  writer observed an individual standing unusually close to the Amos pewter Jewelry Shop. The writer approached the individual and offered help and he refused. The individual appeared to be intoxicated. The writer offered to help him locate his residence.  Shortly after, the individual began verbally abusing the security officer. The writer told  the individual to leave the property. The individual then attempted to open the Amos Pewter Jewelry Shop door.  The writer issued a verbal PPA warning and again asked the individual to leave the property. The individual did not comply, and the writer contacted the non-emergency police line.  During wait time of call individual became aggressive toward the writer. The writer stepped back; however, the individual continued to approach aggressively. The writer pushed the individual back slightly to create distance. The individual then walked around the Salter parking lot.  A few minutes later, the individual began walking toward the NS Power building. Once the individual approached the NS Power building , the writer cancelled the police call.  The writer again instructed the individual to leave the property. The individual continued verbally abusing the security officer for a short period before leaving the property towards Halifax Port around 0130 hrs

fields.summary: At approximately 2015 hours, the trainee, Amandeep , informed the writer of a suspicious female inside the store. The suspect was described as a white female wearing a blue tracksuit, white shoes, and a blue beanie.  The suspect was observed concealing one meat product, one avocado night bar, and additional grocery items. She later proceeded to Cash 3, where she paid only for one cantaloupe, failing to scan or pay for the concealed merchandise.  After exiting the store, the writer and the trainee approached the suspect outside and identified ourselves as Loss Prevention Officers, requesting that she return to the store. The suspect immediately fled on foot through the parking lot with the unpaid merchandise.  A photograph of the suspect was taken for documentation purposes. MOD Craig was informed of the incident. No physical apprehension was made due to safety concerns.  Estimated Value of Stolen Merchandise: Approximately $30.00

Loss Prevention Officer Baljeet observed a suspicious white male, approximately 40 years of age, wearing a black jacket, black trouser , and a white baseball cap later identified as jonathan Hanson , inside the store. Officer Baljeet initially observed the male in the meat department selecting packs of fish and steak, after which the male proceeded to aisle number 3 where Officer Baljeet observed him conceal all the selected merchandise inside his black jacket. The male then moved to the chips aisle, selected one bag of chips, and at approximately 7:00 p.m. made a cash payment at cash register number 7 for the bag of chips only, failing to pay for the concealed merchandise. The male then exited the store without attempting to pay for the concealed items. Officer Baljeet followed the male outside the store and safely approached him, identified himself as Loss Prevention Officer Baljeet ,and informed the male that he had not paid for the merchandise concealed inside his jacket. The male complied and returned the concealed items, after which Officer Baljit escorted him to the main office and contacted the store manager, Nicole, to whom he explained the entire incident. Officer Baljeet then contacted the RCMP via the non-emergency line and filed a theft complaint. At approximately 7:20 p.m., an RCMP officer arrived at the store, and Officer Baljit provided a full account of the incident. The RCMP officer issued a one-year ban to the male and provided a file number 202676085,  to Officer Baljeet . At approximately 7:30 p.m., Officer Baljeet issued the male a PPA  for theft, and both the suspect and the RCMP officer departed the store .

At approximately 1:55 PM, while standing near the checkout area, I observed a white male, approximately 6.0 feet tall, wearing a grey hoodie, black jacket, and a cap, holding two vacuum cleaners. The suspect attempted to exit the store without making payment. I approached the individual and requested a receipt. He stated that he had paid at the auto parts section and claimed that he had thrown away the receipt. I asked for proof of purchase, which he refused to provide. When I attempted to retrieve the merchandise, the suspect forcefully struck my left hand and immediately exited the store with the items. I followed him outside and was able to capture the vehicle license plate number. The incident was reported to the police, and all relevant details were provided.

While patrolling inside the store, the writer observed an older, average-built white female with brown hair, wearing a long blue winter jacket, jeans, brown shoes, and a grey sweater underneath the jacket. While she was walking in aisle 1 or 2, she appeared suspicious to the writer. The writer also observed her placing an item inside her bag but he was not 100% sure about that, as he didn’t saw any selection.   The writer informed the cashier supervisor and cashier about the suspicious activity and then went to check the security cameras. On camera, the writer confirmed that the lady took a meat pack worth $33. The writer later observed her take an air freshener worth $10 and then proceed to aisle 8.  The writer continued monitoring her as she went to cashier counter 5, where she only paid for cat food. At that time, the cashier questioned her about the other items. The suspect became embarrassed, started making excuses, and blamed the cashier. The writer intervened, requested her to calm down, and attempted to de-escalate the situation.  The lady then exited the store. After she left, the writer approached her and informed her about the concealment of the meat and air freshener. She denied the allegation and walked to her vehicle. The writer took a photo of her black SUV with license plate HBN-604.  The writer returned to the store and advised the cashier not to blame customers. With the manager’s permission, the writer reviewed the full camera footage and confirmed that the lady had also taken a cake worth $5 and a hot chocolate pack worth $7.  The suspect entered the store at approximately 20:53 and left around 21:15. After confirming the entire incident, the writer filed a police report. The total value of the stolen items was $55 before tax.

January 23, Incident Report – Theft / Recovery with Payment  Time: Approximately 1835 hours  At approximately 1835 hours, the writer observed a suspicious male inside the store. The subject was described as a white male, with curly brown hair, wearing a long black coat and a red beanie. The subject entered the store carrying a white grocery bag that appeared to be empty.  The subject was observed casually shopping. After trainee Amandeep returned from break, he also made the writer aware of the same individual. Both the writer and trainee observed the subject place noodles and olive oil into the grocery bag.  The subject was observed concealing items on two separate occasions. The writer and trainee continued surveillance and later observed the subject exit the store without making any attempt to pay for the concealed merchandise.  Based on continuous observation from entry to exit and the concealment observed, the writer and trainee approached the subject outside the store, identified themselves as Loss Prevention Officers, and requested the return of the unpaid items. The subject initially denied the accusation, but shortly after admitted to the theft, apologized, and agreed to resolve the matter.  The subject returned to the store and paid for all grocery items, totaling $39.41. No merchandise loss occurred. The subject was issued a verbal warning and advised of store policy before being released.  Subject Description: 	•	Gender: Male 	•	Race: White 	•	Hair: Curly brown hair 	•	Clothing: 	•	Long black coat 	•	Red beanie 	•	Bag: White grocery bag (initially empty)

Writer received a call from S/O Bhawanjot at 0201 hrs advising that he observed three females around one of the site houses 3373 Romans ave containing equipment. He increased presence in the area. Upon noticing him, the individuals ran toward the parking lot where a company Advanced Roofing pickup truck was parked. fields.summary: Incident Report – Suspicious Activity / Recovery  Time: Approximately 1823 hours  At approximately 1823 hours, the writer observed an elderly male wearing a red and white winter cap enter the store with a shopping cart. The subject proceeded directly to the meat section, where he selected sausages and two packages of bacon, with a total value of $23.26, and placed them in the cart.  The subject then went to Aisle 2 and was observed attempting to conceal the meat items inside his jacket. After noticing that the writer was observing him, the subject removed the items from his jacket and placed them back into the shopping cart.  The subject then exited the store without making payment. The writer followed and successfully recovered all unpaid merchandise. No physical contact occurred, and no injuries were reported. The recovered items were returned to store inventory.  No police involvement was required.

Time: Approximately 1400 hours  At approximately 1400 hours, the writer and trainee Amandeep observed a suspicious male and female couple inside the store. Both subjects were dressed in all black clothing and were acting together while selecting merchandise.  Subject Descriptions (from observation and image):  Male Subject: 	•	Gender: Male 	•	Race: White 	•	Approximate Age: 25–35 years 	•	Height: Approximately 5’10”–6’0” 	•	Build: Average 	•	Clothing: Black baseball cap, black hooded sweatshirt, black leather-style jacket, grey sweatpants, black shoes  Female Subject: 	•	Gender: Female 	•	Race: White 	•	Approximate Age: 25–35 years 	•	Height: Approximately 5’5”–5’7” 	•	Build: Average 	•	Clothing: Black baseball cap, black jacket, black pants, black footwear  The subjects were observed selecting chicken strips, croissant meat, rotisserie chicken, BBQ chicken, and a bacon egg sandwich. They later proceeded to the natural section, where both subjects were seen concealing the items inside their bags.  At approximately 1405 hours, the writer and trainee approached the subjects outside the store, identified themselves as Loss Prevention Officers, and requested the return of the concealed merchandise. The subjects complied without incident and returned all items.  The total value of the recovered merchandise was $30.05. A verbal ban of six (6) months was issued to both subjects. No physical contact occurred, and no injuries were reported.  This report is for information purposes only.

At approximately 01:55 hours, the writer observed three female individuals walking from the McAlpine Avenue stairs toward Romans Avenue. One individual was wearing a cream-colored jacket with grey joggers, another was wearing a black hoodie with grey joggers, and the third individual’s clothing was not clearly observed. At that time, the writer was conducting routine patrols. After completing patrol of Unit 3383, Romans Avenue, the writer observed the same individuals near Unit 3373, Romans Avenue, moving toward the parking area near Unit 3371. The writer conducted a second patrol of Unit 3373 and observed the female individuals attempting to break into a pickup truck with “Advanced Roofing” written on it. Upon noticing the writer, the individuals immediately stopped their actions and fled the area. The writer continued patrol duties. Upon returning to the location, the female individuals were again present near the same area and once more stopped their activity and moved away when noticed. The writer then conducted an exterior patrol of nearby units. During this time, the individuals scratched the writer’s personal vehicle, which was parked nearby. The writer observed the damage right away after it occurred and confronted two of the female individuals, asking if they had noticed anyone scratching the vehicle. The individuals denied any knowledge and left the area quickly. The writer returned to Chisholm Street to continue exterior patrols. No damage was observed to any of the units the writer was assigned to secure. However, scratch marks were also observed on the Advanced Roofing pickup truck, consistent with mischief or attempted break-in activity. Due to the individuals leaving the area quickly and no damage to the assigned units, the writer did not further confront the individuals. The writer immediately informed the Mobile Supervisor after confirming the damage to his vehicle. At approximately 02:47 hours, the same female individuals returned to the area, approaching from the Bayers Road side and later exiting through McAlpine Street. During this time, the individuals were observed constantly staring at the writer’s vehicle. As they passed the writer, they repeatedly looked back toward the writer and the vehicle, which appeared suspicious in nature. No further damage was observed at that time, and the individuals eventually left the area. Scratches were observed on the writer’s personal vehicle. Scratch marks were also observed on a pickup truck marked “Advanced Roofing.” No damage was observed to any of the secured units. The writer continued patrols of all assigned areas and immediately notified the Mobile Supervisor. The incident was documented accordingly. Police were not notified, as no damage occurred to client property and the suspects fled the area.

Incident Report – War Memorial Site  Date: January 22 Time: 8:15 p.m. – 8:43 p.m. Location: War Memorial Site  At approximately 8:15 p.m., one male individual was observed sitting at the War Memorial site. The individual appeared to be under the influence of drugs and was smoking. He offered me drugs, which were immediately refused.  The individual was disrespectful and verbally inappropriate toward security staff. He stated that his name was “Jonie,” which appeared to be false. During his presence, he continuously changed his sitting position, moving from one area to another, and later sat on the pillars, creating a safety concern. He was instructed to return to a safe sitting position, which he initially resisted.  At one point, the individual attempted to touch the other security guard, but the attempt was unsuccessful. After this, he became increasingly aggressive and verbally abusive. We maintained professionalism, ignored the provocation, and avoided escalation.  The individual then made false allegations, claiming that we were harassing him, which was untrue. He also threatened to damage the statues/property, spoke to himself, and left the premises at approximately 8:43 p.m.  After the individual departed, I returned to the patrol vehicle to document the incident and notify the group.  Subject Description: 	•	Bald male 	•	Wearing a black jacket 	•	Blue hoodie 	•	Black gloves 	•	Black/light grey jeans 	•	Blue and white sports shoes 	•	Wearing glasses 	•	Appeared highly intoxicated and unable to walk properly

Incident Report  Address: 1120 Queen St, Halifax, NS B3H 2R9 Store Number: 574 LPO: Wahegurupal Singh Entry Time: 14:26 Exit Time: 14:35  A male wearing a yellow hoodie, black cap, black pants, black shoes, and a black mask, who appeared to be fully intoxicated, entered the store at approximately 14:26. The writer observed the individual and began monitoring his activity.  The individual selected multiple meat boxes and placed them into a shopping cart. INCIDENT REPORT – THEFT Date: 25 January 2026 Store: Sobeys Aberdeen, New Glasgow Store Number: 370 Time of Occurrence: 17:52 hrs – 17:57 hrs Loss Prevention Officer: Bhupinder Singh Suspect Descriptions Suspect 1 (Female): Approx. Age: 35–40 years Clothing: Purple hoodie with “Champion” written on it, black leggings Accessories: Red spectacles, leather bag Hair: Black Complexion: White Suspect 2 (Female): Approx. Age: 35–40 years Clothing: Black jacket, black leggings Incident Details At approximately 17:52 hours, two female suspects entered Sobeys Aberdeen Store #370. At approximately 17:57 hours, after both suspects exited the store, the store manager informed the writer that she believed the suspects had not paid for merchandise. The writer immediately reviewed CCTV footage and confirmed that no point-of-sale transaction occurred. The suspects were observed proceeding directly to the pharmacy section, where they selected approximately eight (8) items each, with an estimated value of $160 per suspect, resulting in a total loss of approximately $320 to the store. Both suspects were observed concealing the merchandise inside their personal bags. They then positioned themselves in the checkout queue for approximately 20 seconds, before leaving the queue without making payment. Suspect 2 exited the store first, followed shortly by Suspect 1. Both suspects exited the store separately at approximately 17:57 hours. Total time spent inside the store was approximately five (5) minutes. Action Taken Store manager was informed CCTV footage reviewed and documented Incident reported to police Incident Classification Theft Police File Number 2026-110388 Report completed by: Bhupinder Singh Loss Prevention Officer Sobeys Aberdeen – Store #370

Incident Report – Trespass / Aggressive Behaviour / Recovery  Date: January 25, 2026 Time: Approximately 1745 hours  At approximately 1745 hours, the writer observed a male suspect inside the store who was previously banned. The writer positively identified the individual as the same suspect from prior incidents by comparing him with previous reference images on file.  The writer, along with MOD Sam, approached the suspect and informed him that he was trespassing and must leave the store immediately, or police would be contacted. Upon being advised, the suspect ran toward the store entrance while pushing a shopping cart containing unpaid merchandise.  The writer held onto the cart, identifying it as Sobeys property, and calmly instructed the suspect to leave the store without the merchandise. The suspect became aggressive and forcefully pushed the cart into the writer’s legs multiple times. During this struggle, the cart was knocked over and the writer’s mobile phone fell to the ground.  The suspect then picked up the writer’s phone and attempted to leave the store with it. The writer stood in front of the suspect and advised him that the phone was personal property and that he could not leave with it. The suspect stated, “I will smash your phone.” The writer replied calmly that he could try, but the suspect would not be allowed to leave with the merchandise.  At approximately 1754 hours, the suspect threw the writer’s phone toward the deli area and exited the store with 2 red bull cans worth approximately $10.  All unpaid merchandise was successfully recovered, with a total value of $79.68. No injuries were reported.  The suspect is known to police from a previous incident, Police File Number: 2025-4007956.  ⸻  Suspect Description (from image and observation): 	•	Gender: Male 	•	Race: White 	•	Approximate Age: 30–40 years 	•	Height: Approximately 5’8”–5’10” 	•	Build: Medium / stocky 	•	Clothing: 	•	Blue and white hooded jacket with hood up 	•	Dark pants 	•	Accessories: 	•	Black backpack with white stripes on the side 	•	Other Identifiers: 	•	Known banned subject 	•	Aggressive behavior toward staff

At approximately 1925 hrs, the writer was stationed near the front entrance and suddenly the writer observed fire alarm activation. The writer immediately headed toward the fire penal. The writer also called maintenance manager and informed him about it. After informing dave as per the fire penal indication the writer went to second floor and started patrolling the whole area. The writer observed a water leakage in the electrical room. The writer immediately send pictures to Dave as well. Meanwhile the writer also updated that the water pouring down to first floor as well.  Dave mentioned the writer that he is on the way and he already informed the firefighters as well. Meantime, the writer advised everyone to evacuate the building.  At approximately 1745 hrs, fire fighters were on site. The writer escorted them to the electrical room through the nearest stairs. Initially fire fighters were not sure the reason behind the leakage. After that they asked the writer to escort them to the sprinkler room. The writer did escort them and they turned off the water supply of the building.  Meanwhile, dave was on site and after opened the electrical room fire fighters informed the writer that it happened due to broken pipe and advised the writer to call electrical company who handles the sprinkler room of the building.  At approximately 2010 hrs, firefighters were of site and Dave did call the electrician. Meanwhile, the writer was on standby. At approximately 2030 hrs, the electrician was on site.  No further step taken by the writer.

During routine duties, a cutting machine and a metal pole were observed left outside the building near an open doorway. At the time of observation, construction workers Isaac Robinson and Luke Statanz were actively working in the area, and the nearby door had been opened by them to facilitate their work. Both on-duty security guards assumed the equipment had been temporarily placed outside as part of the ongoing work activities and therefore did not intervene immediately. At approximately 19:30 hours, the workers completed their tasks and departed the site. Following their departure, the cutting machine and the metal pole were taken inside the building to ensure safety, prevent damage, and avoid unauthorized access. No damage, loss, or injuries were reported in relation to this incident.

While on shift at approximately 11:00 PM on January 28, 2026, water was observed accumulating on the floor near the equipment area. A pipe was draining into a garbage can that was not suitable for holding water. Upon further inspection, it was identified that the garbage can was broken at the bottom, which caused water to leak onto the floor.  The issue has since been resolved. The damaged garbage can was removed/replaced, and no further leakage was observed. No injuries occurred, and the incident appeared to be related to the container setup rather than a pipe failure.

On Sunday, January 25th, 2026, at approximately 1900 hours, the writer observed a suspicious female wearing a Green coloured jacket, blue pants, pushing a shopping cart in the meat aisle. The individual proceeded to the meat section where she was seen openly placing items into her cart. The writer Abhaypreet approached the individual outside the store, identified themselves as Loss Prevention Officers, and requested that she either return all unpaid merchandise or pay for the items. An incident occurred involving an intoxicated female at 1709 lower water street B3J 1S5. The individual, dressed in all black, wearing grey gloves, and carrying a red and black backpack, approached a worker named Tony and requested assistance, stating she had been punched and was bleeding. Details: Upon approaching Tony, the female claimed she had been physically assaulted. Shortly after, her behavior escalated, and she began using sexually abusive language, directing harassment towards Tony. Sanchi intervened in an attempt to de-escalate the situation. Despite efforts to calm her, the female's erratic behaviour continued. She made claims about laundry bins containing the organs of her friends and repeatedly demanded that the police be called. Her level of intoxication was evident as she ran around the premises. The situation further escalated when the female attempted to punch Sanchi. Sanchi, in an attempt to protect themself and prevent her from reaching Tony, extended their arms to create a barrier. The female was interfering with Tony's ability to perform his work duties. Given the female's intoxicated state, erratic behaviour, and potential danger to herself and others, the police were contacted.

At 11:30 while posted above the clinic resident CH349 came to S/O Daniel Temple asking if I could remove resident HW362 from his room I told him that I would contact a F/C to assist him. Approximately 1:55 pm , coleman's mount pearl.  Security officer billyjo reporting.  As I was doing my rounds i noticed a male and female Making there  way through the parking lot to come to the store. They are too customers that I witness a few times over the year Trying to take stuff. The female was approximately 5ft 8 inc. Roughly 160 lb. She had a troubly face blue eyes and a little gap in her top front teeth. She talked with a low deep voice. The young gentleman was in his early thirties with a black mohawk, 2 earrings in his left ear, a black mustache and goatee, and a blue teardrop tattoo on the left side of his face. He had on a pair of black pants, blue sneakers, a black hoodie and a Gray zip up jacket with a backpack on black. I've seen them coming, so I stood in the porch and then turned to walk away so they could see the security logo on the faculty jacket. The two of them proceed to stop in front of the door looking in at me and then she made her way in by herself , he stayed outside. She walked around in front of the Delhi case for a few minutes and then made her way over to the hot Delhi food. Then she asked young worker a few questions about the food and then ordered a small onion rings , small fries and a gravy. Then she's stood around for another few minutes in front of the deli cass looking around. Then she proceeded to walk over towards me and then walk down by the cashiers. She paid for the food I watched every move she made while still watching the young gentleman out by the door with at this point he made his way into the porch. She paid for her food and then left the building and started to walk around the parking lot. I made my way down to the start of the building to retrieve something from my car, while still keeping an eye on both of them. As soon as you notice me , leaving the side door to go outside , they made their way back up to the parking lot to come back into the store. I quickly made my way back in , started walking up through. We came in contact just in charge of the store to the main doors. They both stopped and looked at me like they just got a little shock that I was there in the store. They turned and started to walk back out.But then came back to me and asked me to call them a cab. They were talking to me like they were very nervous. So I just smiled and said it will be my pleasure.It is really too cold out to walk.I was just making conversation. They proceeded to stand in the ports kept looking into the store. And they were a little ticked off that they could not get anything. I kept doing my job around the store kept my eye on the porch in the meantime. There were a few times it looked like they were going to try and come back in. But I kept locking eyes with them. After about 15 minutes, their cab arrived.And the left. The reason why I remember this couple is because about 3 years ago a Sunday evening, 15 minutes before close.There was eleven people in the store 7 where what do things in their carts without looking at prices. I ended up stopping every one of them.  every one of them had  steaks and bacon and ribs and cheese , and a few Other odds and ends. Put this one particular couple at one point , the gentleman left and the girl from today came over to me very upset and ditched her cart. I asked her why her man left so abruptly with his head down. She said, I set off his p.D s d. I just smiled and said I'm sorry.Tell him to feel free to try and  shoplift somewhere else.

Around 9:35 PM, the writer was patrolling the store when he received a call from the self-checkout area. The cashier informed the writer that a woman had just exited the store carrying a heavy bag; however, the cashier was not sure if any theft or concealment had occurred. The writer went outside but observed two to three women and was unable to identify which individual had left with the bag. The writer then returned inside the store and, with the manager’s permission, went to the camera room to review CCTV footage. Upon review, the writer observed a middle-aged white female of average build with red hair, carrying a large white cloth bag with black V-shaped lines. The suspect entered the store, went to the meat section, and selected two packs of lean beef burgers, each valued at approximately $35. She then proceeded to the natural foods aisle, where she concealed the items inside her bag, and exited the store through the closed cashier counter near the self-checkout area. The suspect then left the parking lot in a silver sedan. The incident occurred at approximately 21:35, and a police complaint has been filed.

Incident Description On January 28, 2026, at approximately 01:50 AM, I was performing my duties as the Security Supervisor at the transitional housing facility 106 AirPort road St.John’s. A resident from Room 349, identified as Cameron, approached the security officer Steve Convay stationed on the second floor. Cameron requested that a female resident, identified as Holly Whitten (Room 362), be removed from his room, stating that she had struck him in the face. The second-floor security officer notified the Floor Coordinator. Following this, a response team consisting of two Support Staff members and two Security Officers (myself and Shana Wodland) proceeded to Room 349. Sequence of Events 1. Entry and Verbal Commands: Security Officer Shana Wodland, Security Officer Steve Convay, and one Support Staff member entered the room. They requested Holly Whitten to leave the room approximately 5 to 6 times. Ms. Whitten aggressively refused to comply. 2. First Physical Contact: Officer Wodland attempted to escort Ms. Whitten by gesturing toward the exit and repeating the request to leave. At this point, Ms. Whitten pushed Officer Bowland in the chest. 3. Escalation: I entered the room and positioned myself between Ms. Whitten and the door to facilitate her exit. I requested that she leave the premises. 4. Assault: Ms. Whitten exited into the hallway. Once in the hallway, she turned toward me and struck me in the face with both fists simultaneously. Post-Incident Action Immediately following the assault, Support Staff contacted the police. Security and staff remained in the second-floor hallway to monitor the situation and wait for the arrival of the authorities. Signature: Vitalii Dorofieiev Date: January 28, 2026

January 28, 2026.  106 Airport Road, Horizons at 106 S/O Steve Conway   At  approximately 12:30 Holly Whitten, (HW362) began creating a disturbance in the hallway outside of her boyfriend Cameron Hewitt's (CH349) room. Floor coordinators and a support worker were paged and successfully intervened, and brought the situation under control.  At approximately 13:15 HW362, returned to boyfriend, CH349's room and started banging on the door again after roaming the halls disturbing residents and staff.  At 13:45 CH349 approached me and requested the removal of of HW362. Upon receiving the request I contacted James BR, the support worker on duty,  and asked him to page a floor coordinator to our location at room CH349. I immediately paged a team lead for assistance. Once we all assembled out side of the room, we were let in by the occupant, CH349. Upon entering the room the team lead and myself approached HW362 and asked her to leave the room on her own accord. She refused and we began to help escort her from the room. Once we made contact with HE362 we were met with resistance.   As we began moving toward the door our team supervisor arrived and completed the extraction with the assistance of our team lead. Once in the corridor, HW362 threw a punch and struck out supervisor in the face. The Floor coordinators were asked to call police and report the incident. We tried to calm HW362 down while we waited for police. Once calm she kept requesting to leave for a cigarette and kept taunting out supervisor and team lead with personal insults. This continued until approximately 14:00 when police arrived. At approximately 14:05,  HW362 was escorted by police, floor coordinators, and security to our safe room where she was interviewed and taken into custody by police. At 14:10 HW362 was removed from the building by police. The supervisor, team lead and myself then met with an officer in our security room where he made note of our personal information and asked us to forward our individual statements to his email address.
