At approximately 18:39 hours, the writer observed a suspicious suspect inside the store.  The suspect selected multiple items including meat products, a chocolate bar box, a bottle of biotin, and a watermelon. The total value of the items was $159.07.  The writer observed the suspect traveling through different aisles and concealing items inside a bag.  The suspect later proceeded to self-checkout and paid only for a bag of Doritos, leaving the remaining items unpaid.  The writer approached the suspect outside the store, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and requested the return of the unpaid merchandise. The suspect complied and returned all items.  The suspect became emotional and stated he  is a student, asked not to involve police, and said he would not return to the store.  The writer issued a verbal warning and a 6-month PPA (Property Prohibition Act) from the store.  All items were successfully recovered. Total Value Recovered: $159.07

Received a call from Sam, the Cleaner Supervisor, who was working at Founder's Square. She notified security that the fire alarm was beeping at Founder's. Security was at Barrington at that time and promptly arrived at Founder's. The fire brigade subsequently arrived, and security provided them with access. They inspected the fire panel, which indicated an alarm in the Nova Scotian basement Chronicle. They thoroughly checked every area in the basement. Security also assisted them in accessing various rooms. Stanley Holland ( On call maintenance) is also present . Despite these efforts, the root cause of the alarm has not yet been identified.  Update- Anup Gupta has instructed security personnel to remain founder until everything goes well . He further stated that a D&L engineer is expected to arrive shortly to conduct monitoring activities and will contact security upon their arrival.   Update - At approximately 9:30 PM, the engineer arrived and conducted a thorough inspection of the control panel and surrounding areas. Subsequently, he utilized his system to identify the root cause, tracing it to the washroom in the Old Triangle building. The bar staff at the Old Triangle reported that a teenager had activated the alarm switch located near the washroom, a fact confirmed by surveillance footage. Following this, the engineer reset all fire panel systems and the elevator. Operations have since returned to normal. Now security can resume patrolling

When the writer was patrolling the store area, he noticed a woman in the meat section selecting meat and placing it into her cart, which appeared suspicious to the writer. She was a white female of average build, approximately 5’4” tall, with black hair, wearing a black jacket, black trousers, and brown Chelsea boots. She was carrying two bags: one brown and coffee-coloured bag and one white grocery bag, which was empty when she entered the store (confirmed through CCTV footage). From that point, the writer began observing her. When the writer first noticed her, she had selected two packs of meat, each valued at approximately $30, and placed them in her cart. She also selected pepperoni sausage valued at approximately $10. She then went to Pharmacy Aisle 2, where she concealed the two packs of meat. After that, she returned to the deli section, where she concealed additional items. She then proceeded to Pharmacy Aisle 3, where she abandoned the cart. Inside the cart, the writer later observed two boxes of raspberries, each valued at approximately $5, and one green grapes pack valued at approximately $6.50( confirmed these items after reviewing cameras).The suspect then took an orange juice bottle and one meat pack, proceeded to Cashier Counter 7 or 8, and exited the store without paying.  The writer approached the suspect outside the store, identified himself, and explained the incident. The writer requested that she either pay for or return the unpaid items. The suspect stated that she was going to get her wallet, at which point the writer informed her that she was not permitted to take merchandise outside the store without payment. At that moment, the writer heard a male yelling from behind him. The male, who appeared middle-aged, approximately the same height as the female, wearing a black jacket and jeans and clean-shaven, intervened and prevented the writer from continuing the interaction. He stated that they had paid for the items at another store, told the writer to check the cameras, and continued yelling in an apparent attempt to distract the writer and was also nearby the writer, so the female could leave. Both individuals then left the area.  The writer returned to the store and reviewed the CCTV footage with the manager on duty. Upon review, it was confirmed that the female entered the store at approximately 2:15 PM, selected fruit and an orange juice bottle, concealed merchandise, and exited the store at approximately 2:21 PM without paying. Assistant manger informed the writer that she also did theft before and banned inside the store. Police complaint has been filed. The total amount of the theft is around $70 as well that was around $30.

Incident Report  Sobeys Aberdeen Business Centre (Store #370) LPO: Bhupinder Singh  File Number: 2026-146589  On February 2, 2026, at 16:26 hours, Loss Prevention Officer Bhupinder Singh was patrolling the Sobeys Aberdeen Business Centre (Store #370) when he observed four individuals enter the premises. The group consisted of two females (Suspects 1 and 2) and two males (Suspects 3 and 4), the latter being associated with the "Maritime Boys" social media group. The writer maintained constant surveillance and continuity of the group as they moved to the pharmacy section. Suspect 1, wearing a black "Maritime Boys" hoodie and black leggings, and Suspect 2, wearing a green cap and jacket with checkered pajama pants, selected four grooming shavers in total. The writer observed the suspects move to Aisle 3, where they concealed the merchandise inside their clothing and discarded the empty packaging, which was later recovered and handed to Manager Rena.  While the theft was being committed by the two females, Suspects 3 and 4 proceeded to the checkout and paid for their own items, including cucumbers, Vaseline, and condoms. The two females only paid for a box of Compliments garbage bags, leaving the store at 16:42 hours without paying for the concealed grooming shavers. Upon them exiting the store, the writer approached Suspect 1 and Suspect 2, introduced himself as Loss Prevention, and informed them that the pharmacy items had not been paid for. At no point did the writer request a physical search of any individual.  The interaction quickly escalated as Suspect 1 ignored the writer and went directly to the driver's seat of their vehicle. Suspect 2 engaged in a heated verbal confrontation with the writer, while Suspect 3 (the lead male) began filming the writer with camera-equipped glasses, claiming he was protecting the females and intended to post the footage online. Suspect 4 remained distanced from the incident and had no interaction with the writer. The group then fled the scene in a gray GMC Terrain, Nova Scotia plate HHA 022. The suspects attempted to obstruct the view of the plate by opening the trunk, eventually removing the plate and placing it inside the vehicle before driving off. The total loss to the store is $80.00. The New Glasgow Regional Police were contacted, and a report was filed under File Number 2026-146589 with Officer Sharpe (Badge #278).

Subject: Theft Prevention  Store Name: Sobeys Pictou   Store Number: 232   Date: January 28, 2026   Time: 1920 hours   LPO Name: Nijo Joy    Incident Report:  At approximately 1920 hours, the writer was conducting regular patrol inside the store. During patrol, the writer observed a female individual in the pharmacy section acting in a suspicious manner. The individual was described as having blonde hair, wearing black pants and a coffee-colored jacket, slim build, and approximately 45–50 years of age.  The individual selected multiple items from the pharmacy aisle, including toothpaste, soap, and face wash. She also selected additional items from the produce section and other aisles. The writer maintained continuous observation of the individual for approximately 20 minutes while she remained inside the store.  The individual later proceeded to the self-checkout area. While at self-checkout, the writer observed the individual conceal the toothpaste, face wash, and soap under her bag. She paid for the remaining items but did not scan or pay for the concealed merchandise.  As the individual began to exit the store, the writer informed staff at the checkout area that the individual had not paid for all items. The writer and checkout staff approached the individual, at which time she stated that she had forgotten to pay and attempted to complete payment. The individual then stated that her card was not working and subsequently exited the store.  The writer noted the individual’s behavior for future reference. The concealed merchandise was successfully recovered, preventing loss. The total value of the recovered items was $22.27.

Officer Baljeet observed a suspicious white male suspect, approximately 37 years of age and about 6’3” in height, wearing a green winter jacket, grey jeans, and a black winter cap, carrying a reusable shopping bag, inside the store. The suspect was first observed in the Daily Aisle where he selected two large chicken sandwiches, after which he proceeded to the Natural Source department and concealed the merchandise inside his reusable bag. The suspect then continued moving from aisle to aisle in a manner consistent with counter-surveillance behavior, appearing aware that he was being followed. At approximately  5:09 PM , the suspect exited the store without making any attempt to pay for the concealed merchandise. Officer Baljeet followed the suspect outside the store and identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer; however, the suspect did not respond, did not engage in any conversation, and immediately fled the area on a bicycle traveling toward Commercial Street. The suspect was verbally advised that he was banned from the store for a period of six months due to concealment and theft of merchandise. Officer Baljeet reported the incident to the manager on duty, Courtney, and advised that the suspect may have exited the store with additional unpaid merchandise concealed inside the reusable bag.

Store: Sobeys – 985 Prince St. Store No.: 871 Date: 04 February 2026 LPO: Amanpreet Singh Deterrence Report During routine patrol inside the store at approximately 1725 hours, the writer observed an elderly male individual enter the store. The individual appeared to be intoxicated and was having difficulty walking straight. Description of Individual: 	•	Skinny build 	•	White skin 	•	Long white beard 	•	Wearing a black hat The writer maintained observation from a safe distance and observed the individual conceal one meat roll from the meat section inside his jacket, valued at $30. Due to the individual’s level of intoxication, the writer continued monitoring without direct engagement. The individual later approached the writer in the main section of the store and stated that he knew he was being observed and claimed that he was not attempting to steal any items. While walking, the individual dropped the concealed meat item from inside his jacket. The writer then observed the individual return to the meat aisle, select additional items, proceed to the checkout, pay for all selected merchandise, and exit the store. No further incident occurred. Result: Successful deterrence – $30 recovered

According to EHS, the individual claimed to be a chef and stated that his chef knife was lost while EHS was assisting him. I was not present at the exact time of the initial incident. I reviewed camera footage for further information. On the FP Saltyard South camera, a person was observed near the corner close to a restricted section; however, the individual was not clearly visible. Other individuals assisting were seen standing nearby. EHS arrived on scene at approximately 19:43 hrs. A second EHS unit arrived around 19:50 hrs. EHS advised that the individual was stable but required additional testing at the hospital, as he was still experiencing difficulties. EHS transported the individual from the scene at approximately 20:35 hrs. End of the Report.

When the writer was patrolling around 17:32, he observed two thin brown youths, approximately 5’4” tall, walking from the deli section toward the aisles. One was wearing a black jacket over a red hoodie, dark trousers, and carrying a black backpack. The other had black curly hair, was wearing a black jacket, and carrying a yellow backpack with multiple designs or cartoon images. One suspect was holding two bottles, one sushi pack, one Sprite bottle, and possibly one kimchi pack from the deli. The other suspect was also holding two bottles. As they appeared suspicious, the writer began observing them.  Suspect B began making comments and stopped the writer. The writer asked if he had said anything, and Suspect B replied, “No, I am asking you if you said anything.” The writer then moved away. At that time, Suspect A dropped all the items in the pops aisle. They then moved to other aisles. The writer later observed them again in the international foods aisle, where Suspect B stopped the writer and asked if he was following them. The writer replied no. Suspect B then asked why the writer was present wherever they went. The writer replied that he was doing his own shopping. Suspect B stated that they were not going to steal. The writer replied that he had not accused them and asked if he had said anything to them, to which they replied no.  The writer then informed Manager Charlie about the entire situation. While the writer was speaking with the manager, the suspects passed by them, and Suspect B made facial expressions toward the writer, which the writer ignored. While the writer and manager were talking, the suspects returned and stated to the manager that the writer was following them. The writer replied that he is doing his own business.Suspect B then told the writer that he was terrible at his job. The writer warned them that they could not make such statements. The suspects asked the manager if the writer was undercover and again made similar statements, attempting to escalate the situation. The writer did not respond, and the manager requested that they leave the store.  The suspects then went to Cashier Counter 5 and spoke with cashier Maeve, after which they returned to the writer and the manager. They claimed that the cashier told them the writer had falsely arrested someone a few weeks earlier for purchasing gum from Dollar Tree and that the writer had stopped that person wrongly. The suspects again stated that the writer was terrible at his job and should leave his position. The writer felt shocked and hurt, as these were false allegations that could affect his professional career and employment. The writer immediately denied the allegations, and both the writer and manager asked who had told them this information. The suspects again pointed to cashier Maeve.  The writer then clearly warned the suspects that a PPA could be issued, as they were attempting to escalate the situation. The manager also requested that they leave. At that point, the suspects stated that they had dropped items in an aisle. The manager asked if they wanted to purchase those items, and they replied yes and went to retrieve them. When they returned, they handed the items to the manager and stated they no longer wished to purchase anything. They then exited the store, during which Suspect B again made facial expressions toward the writer.  Following this, the manager and cashier supervisor investigated the allegation involving cashier Maeve. She stated that she did not make those comments and that the suspects were the ones making those statements and asking questions. She also stated that earlier the suspects had asked her to purchase food for them, and when she declined, they used inappropriate language toward her. The suspects left the store at approximately 17:45. The total value of the items they abandoned in the store was approximately $30.

Around 16:40, the writer was patrolling the store area when he observed a male individual, Brody Fox, who is already banned from the store due to multiple previous incidents, entering the store. The writer positioned himself near the produce section so he could stop the suspect if necessary. At that moment, the suspect also noticed the writer, as he is familiar with him, and immediately exited the store through Cashier Counter 7 toward the mall side. The suspect was wearing a khaki jacket, carrying a large side bag, wearing a red baseball cap, sky-blue pants, and glasses, and had long hair. After the suspect exited, the writer informed Manager Chirag of the situation.  The writer remained near the entrance due to the possibility of the suspect returning. Approximately 10 minutes later, around 16:58, the suspect returned from the mall side while covering his face with a scarf. The writer was positioned near the entrance and immediately contacted store management. When the suspect entered the store, the writer requested that he leave immediately; however, the suspect did not respond. Shortly after, the managers arrived and supported the writer, also requesting that the suspect leave the store. Manager Chirag called 911 while the writer and Manager Christine remained with the suspect.  The suspect did not comply and continued walking inside the store. During this time, Manager Christine began recording video footage of the suspect. The suspect pretended that he did not recognize the writer or the store manager and stated that he had never seen Manager Christine before. He also denied ever being in the store previously. The writer informed the suspect about a prior incident in which he had been caught stealing merchandise. The suspect attempted to escalate the situation by stating that staff were unfairly preventing him from purchasing items. Manager Christine informed him that he was not permitted to enter the store due to his ban.  Police had not arrived at that time. No theft was committed, and no physical harm occurred. After approximately 10–12 minutes, at around 17:08, the suspect exited the store and walked toward Park Lane.

At approximately 8:24 PM, the Front Desk received a call from the pool area. Several female guests, including a 17-year-old (rooms 106, 107, and 326), reported a male guest making them uncomfortable by staring at them inappropriately. They stated the guest entered the pool area with three small children, was using gym equipment nearby, and making uncomfortable eye contact.  The children were also reported to be disturbing guests in the pool. The guests requested that the individual not return to the pool area. The DM and Security proceeded upstairs to speak with the guests from rooms 106, 107, and 326. After reviewing camera footage, the individual was identified as a guest from rooms 222/223 staying with family. A verbal warning was issued, and the guest was advised to avoid using the pool area at the same time as the reporting guests to prevent further issues His daughter translated during the conversation. The guest claimed that other guests had put his children at risk; camera footage did not confirm this. MOD reiterated expectations for respectful behavior and explained the decision.  Security and MOD then followed up with guests from rooms 106, 107, and 326 to ensure they were okay. They were informed that the guest from room 223 would not use the pool at the same time and were advised to contact the Front Desk or Security if any further issues occur.

As a Loss Prevention Officer at Sobeys Stellarton writter was patrolling on floor. At approximately 4:30 PM, a suspicious male entered the store. He was wearing a black jacket, black cap, blue jeans, and red shoes, and was carrying a large empty side bag on his shoulder. Due to his behavior and appearance, I flagged the individual and maintained continuous observation. The subject remained in the store for approximately 20 minutes, during which time he selected multiple items from the sales floor and placed them into a shopping cart. At approximately 4:50 PM, the subject attempted to exit the store without making any attempt to pay for the merchandise. As the subject exited into the vestibule area, I immediately confronted him and identified myself as store security. The subject looked at me and left the area immediately. He did not respond to any questions or provide identification. I secured the shopping cart containing the unpaid merchandise. Store Manager Justine was notified immediately, and police were called right away. Officer Paul Veenhuis responded to the call and arrived at the store approximately 10 to 15 minutes later. The police report number associated with this incident is 2026180319. The subject was later identified by Officer Paul Veenhuis as Mackenzie, Kenneth Marc, date of birth January 27, 1981. The subject was verbally PPA’d by me. A copy of the police-issued PPA will be shared as soon as it is received by the store. The total value of the merchandise involved was $525.94, and all items were successfully recovered. No injuries were reported. Report completed by: vishal mangvana  Loss Prevention Officer Sobeys Stellarton

Incident Report  Time: 1157 hrs Location: Historic Properties File Number: 26-17373 Police Officers Present: 	•	Constable Badhe #432 	•	Constable #609  Involved Parties:  Male: 	•	Name: Lochlan Nelson 	•	DOB: August 24, 1999 	•	Age: 26  Female: 	•	Name: Jay Jeffer 	•	DOB: January 31, 2001  ⸻  Narrative:  At approximately 1157 hrs, the writer was conducting door checks at the RBC location when a male later identified as Lochlan Nelson approached the writer. The male had visible bleeding on his right cheek, was wearing only a T-shirt, and appeared distressed and crying. He requested police assistance and help from the writer, stating that he did not have money or a phone.  The writer asked the male what had occurred. The male stated that he had been assaulted by his girlfriend in a domestic dispute and was seeking a safe place to stay. He further requested a jacket to stay warm.  The writer escorted the male inside to keep him out of the cold. The writer asked the male if he required an ambulance due to the bleeding. The male declined medical assistance. With the male’s consent, the writer provided first aid by cleaning the blood from the male’s face and applying basic care to help prevent further bleeding.  The writer contacted Armour Guard Security Officer Ravijeet and asked if any clothing was available in lost and found and if the front door could be opened so the male could wait inside until police arrival.  While speaking with the male, he advised that both he and his girlfriend were from British Columbia and identified as First Nations. He stated that his girlfriend regularly abused him.  The writer contacted non-emergency police and provided all available information.  At approximately 1217 hrs, police arrived on scene in vehicle C32. Officers spoke with the male and obtained his statement. A second police unit was dispatched to the Marriott Hotel, Room 301, to obtain the female’s statement.  After speaking with both parties, police determined that the male was the primary aggressor and that he had initiated the physical assault. The male was placed under arrest and taken into custody. Officers advised that charges would be laid.  The writer obtained the file number and provided a written statement to police before clearing the scene.

fields.summary: Reported By: Khushal Date: 06/02/2026 Time: 1820 hrs – 1902 hrs Address: 1875 Barrington Street, Halifax, NS Subject Description: White male Approximately in his 30s Wearing blue jeans, black hoodie with Fila logo, grey winter cap White towel over shoulder Identified as Robert Shane Comeau At approximately 1820 hrs, the writer began routine patrol and observed a male individual near the front mirror gate acting suspiciously. The writer approached the individual and asked if he required assistance, as it was unclear whether he was a registered guest. To verify access, the writer asked if the individual possessed a Barrington Hotel key card. The individual presented a Barrington card. The writer escorted the individual to the washrooms and continued patrol. While conducting door checks, the writer later observed the same individual attempting to enter restricted rooms designated for authorized business meetings only. The writer advised him that these rooms were closed and not accessible to guests. During conversation, the individual stated he had been kicked out of KFC and Subway, which increased suspicion regarding his status. To confirm his registration, the writer escorted him to the front desk for verification. Front desk staff were unable to locate any room registered under his name. Security and the Staff Supervisor accompanied the individual to verify his claimed room. The individual initially stated he was staying in Room 206, then later changed his statement to Room 210. Upon verification, it was confirmed that no guest was registered in either room. While returning from the floor, the writer observed the individual in possession of multiple hotel key cards, including Barrington and other hotel cards. At the front desk, the writer asked him to present the Barrington card from his wallet. The individual then claimed he might be staying at Hotel Halifax, further contradicting his previous statements. Staff later informed security that records showed Robert Shane Comeau had an RCMP charge from 2021 for possession for the purpose of trafficking a controlled substance and was released the same year. At approximately 1853 hrs, the writer observed the individual seated across from GoodLife Fitness and instructed him to leave the property immediately, advising that police would be contacted if he failed to comply. After continued verbal interaction, the writer contacted the RCMP non-emergency line. While officers were being contacted, the individual began leaving the property. During this time, he made verbal threats, challenged the writer to fight, and stated he would kill the writer if found, while standing near the exit doors. The writer remained calm and professional throughout the interaction. The individual left the property at approximately 1902 hrs. No physical altercation occurred.

Incident Report: Store #370  Date: January 9, 2026 Location: Sobeys Aberdeen, New Glasgow Reporting Officer: Loss Prevention Officer Bhupinder Singh  On January 9, 2026, while conducting a routine patrol at Sobeys Aberdeen (Store #370), I observed a male suspect enter the premises at 5:46 PM. I immediately identified the individual as Marc MacKenzie, whom I had personally served with a trespass notice in December, effective until June. The suspect, wearing a black jacket, blue jeans, and red/black shoes, proceeded to the deli section and selected six items, including cakes, sandwiches, and chicken rice, with a total retail value of $47.50. I promptly notified the Manager on Duty of the situation. With the manager’s assistance, I approached the suspect at 5:48 PM as he attempted to leave, identified myself, and informed him that he was in violation of his active trespass order. I instructed him to surrender the merchandise and vacate the property immediately to avoid police involvement. The suspect complied, handed over all items, and left the store without further incident. All merchandise was successfully recovered in saleable condition, resulting in no financial loss to the store.

During the first patrol, At approximately 8:22 a.m., some trash was observed at two different locations within Stairwell S2. The stairwell was checked thoroughly, and no individuals were found at the time of inspection. No further issues were observed.

At approximately 2156 hrs, LPO noticed a police officer on site. LPO went and asked if the officer is investigating anything and needs any help. The officer informed LPO that today at approximately 1530 hrs, a female reported a sexual assault complaint and the officer was on site to check the cameras. LPO called manager Jeremy and got permission to show the cameras to the officer. LPO then noted the description of people involved and showed CCTV footage to the officer. In the CCTV footage, it was clearly observed by both the officer and LPO that a male’s cart touched her mistakenly and the female was not a sexually assaulted. The officer requested the video from the LPO. The officer gave LPO an incident number which is 26-20021. LPO received permission from manager Jeremy and sent CCTV video to the officer. The officer left at approximately 2202 hrs. LPO made an incident report. Nothing further to report.   The description of the female include,  Caucasian female, light green and black hoodie, light green t shirt brown hair bun, heavy build, black leggings, age approximately in her 40s, height approximately 5’6.

Writer was driving on the bridge when he noticed a male had slipped, causing his vehicle to hit the guardrails. Writer stopped immediately and assisted the male in exiting from the side of his vehicle. All airbags had deployed. Writer asked if he required medical assistance; the male appeared to be in shock. Writer instructed another person to call 911 while checking on the injured individual. Writer attempted to move the vehicle as it was blocking traffic; however, one of the tires detached in the meantime. Bridge patrol arrived, secured the area, and instructed writer to clear the scene. Writer then departed the bridge.

Writer arrived back from break around 2200 hrs and saw a police officer inside. When the writer spoke to employees, he learned that a male had stolen their car from the parking lot. A white male with a ginger beard, wearing a black jacket and carrying a black bag, had entered at approximately 2131 hrs and asked the writer to call a cab for him. The writer called the cab, and the male left around the same time the writer went for break. The writer later learned that the same individual was responsible for stealing the vehicle. The police officer asked the writer to speak with the Sobeys manager and obtain CCTV footage if possible. The writer spoke with the manager, and both went inside to review and obtain the footage for police. The police took the writer’s information and then left the site.

On Monday, February 9th, 2026 at approximately 1820 hours, the writer observed a suspicious male wearing a black jacket, black pants pushing a shopping cart. The individual proceeded to the meat section where he was seen openly placing items into the cart. He later exited the store through the cashier stand area along with cart without making any payment.  The writer Abhaypreet approached the individual outside the store, identified themselves as Loss Prevention Officers, and requested that he return all unpaid merchandise and was informed that the incident had been recorded and police would be contacted. The suspect then abandoned the cart and fled the scene.  A verbal PPA (Private Property Act notice) was issued, and the individual was banned from the premises. MOD Garron was informed of the situation and requested to obtain an image of the suspect for future reference.  Recovered Merchandise: The following items were successfully recovered with a total value of $346.86: 	•	2 × Chicken Breast – $25.94 each 	•	2 × Bacon Thick Cut $17.99 each 	•	1 × Beef – $17.75 each 	•	1 × SS Blade- $40.07 each 	•	1 × Tide Detergent- $28.99 each 	•	1 × Froot Loops- $10.49 each  	•	1 × Vector- $6.99 each   Total Recovered Value: $192.97  Action Taken: 	•	All items were successfully recovered and returned to the store. 	•	Verbal PPA issued; suspect banned from store property. 	•	Incident details and suspect image to be reviewed and documented for reference. 	•	MOD Garron notified immediately after the incident.

Incident Report  Date: February 09, 2026  Time: 1904-1919 hours  Location: Sobeys Prince Street, Truro (871)  Reported By: LPO Gurpal Singh  At approximately 1904 hours, a female in her 50s entered the store wearing a blue cap, black jacket, black pants, and brown shoes. She was carrying two empty grocery bags—one Walmart bag and one Dollarama bag. At 1906 hours, she proceeded directly to the frozen section, where she selected one package of flavoured crisps. She then went to the grocery aisles and selected two Compliments mushrooms and three packages of potato perogies. Afterward, she proceeded to the meat section and selected one package of ground beef. At approximately 1915 hours, she went to the self checkout area, when she paid for only one loaf of bread. She failed to pay for the remaining items, which were concealed inside her bags. Upon exiting the store, the writer stopped her, identified himself, and recovered the unpaid merchandise from her bags. The writer attempted to issue a ban; however, she did not comply and walked toward the Dairy Queen area. The writer then verbally informed her that she is banned from the store for six months. The total value of the items is $39.65 The writer obtained the receipt and reported the incident to the Manager on Duty (MOD). End of report.

fields.summary: Received a call from the MoD at the Barrington Hotel. He informed me that there is an individual in the lobby that was unable to check in and is now loitering. He described the individual as "sketchy" and would like him to be removed.  When I arrived I spoke with the MoD and front desk staff. They informed me that the individual had attempted to check in with no ID or credit card, and offered 40 dollars for a room the individual sat on the couch for an hour before I was called.  When I approached the individual I informed him that he was not allowed to sit in the lobby if he was not a guest. He told me that he was just waiting for an email to be sent. I recognized the individual as the same individual that I had to deal with in a previous incident some years ago in the same situation.  I asked the front desk for more information at this point, and noticed the Barrington Hotel guard was arriving. Afterwards I noticed the individual was beginning to stand up and leave.  The individual left without further incident.

Incident Report  Date: February 07, 2026  Time: 1358-1429 hours  Location: Sobeys Prince Street, Truro (871)  Reported By: LPO Gurpal Singh  At approximately 1358 hours, a female suspect with blonde hair, wearing a black jacket, black joggers, and brown shoes, entered the store. She was pushing a small cart with two grocery bags placed on the upper portion of the cart. She selected few items from produce section. At approximately 1405 hours, the suspect proceeded to the meat section and selected two steaks. She then went to aisle 4, where she concealed the steaks inside one of her grocery bags. At approximately 1415 hours, the suspect continued through the frozen food aisle and aisle 7, selecting several additional items, including ice cream and cheese. The suspect then proceeded to the self-checkout area, where she paid for the selected items except for the two steaks. At this time, the writer had already informed managers Lenny and Shelly of the situation. Manager Shelly and the writer positioned themselves near the self-checkout area. After completing payment for the remaining items, the suspect appeared to notice she was being observed. She then returned and paid for the two concealed steaks before exiting the store at approximately 1429 hours. As the suspect paid for the previously concealed items, the writer did not make contact with her. After she exited the store, the writer obtained the license plate number of the vehicle she was driving for future reference. The vehicle was a black Mazda with license plate GRW655. The writer then explained the incident to manager Lenny and sent a photo to manager Tom at Sobeys Robie Street. Manager Tom provided the suspect’s name and address. The suspect was identified as Brittani Dawn Moore, residing at 28 Guess Drive, Bible Hill. The total value of the deterred items is estimated to be approximately $80. End of report

fields.summary: During a patrol at 01:27, I arrived at the main lobby and observed a former hotel resident who had previously stayed in room 342. At that time, two Security Officers (S/O) and two support staff members were present in the lobby. Later, one more support staff member arrived. The former resident was upset and behaving aggressively. She wanted to make a phone call to someone she knew, and a support staff member gave her a mobile phone. She was on the phone for a long time, and I stayed nearby. At 01:35, I went to the second door. After hearing that the situation in the lobby was stressful and unstable, I returned. Her behavior became worse and unpredictable, and she left the hotel. Two Security Officers continued speaking with her outside, and I went to the main entrance to assist. I replaced one Security Officer at the main door, and we locked the door while waiting for the police. Earlier, one of the floor coordinators had already called the police. While waiting for the police, the woman continued to act aggressively. She hit the main door several times and tried to open it. She also attempted to attack me and the team lead. We gave verbal warnings, but she continued. Using de-escalation techniques, we detained her until the police arrived. When the police arrived, they took control of the situation.

LPO reported the incident to manager Branon, Halifax no emergency police ( Incident: 26-22835 ) and manager Krista. Nothing further to report.   The description of the female includes,  Caucasian female, height approximately 5’8, age approximately in her 30s, full grey winter coat, grey sweatpants, grey shoes, blonde hair, medium build, blue Walmart bag.

A white male was observed in the meat department selecting two pork ribs and attempting to conceal them inside his jacket. I maintained continuous observation. The subject concealed one pork rib inside his jacket while holding the other in his hand.  He then proceeded to the frozen aisle, selected two packs of cheese, and concealed them inside his jacket. While moving through the aisles, he abandoned one pork rib in an aisle.  The subject exited the store without making any attempt to pay at Cashier 3. I approached him outside the store and requested the return of the unpaid merchandise. Initially, the subject became aggressive; however, after verbal communication, he complied and returned the merchandise. All items were successfully recovered. Exit time- 1:13pm Description- white male, in his 50s, wore black jacket with blue pants and white base ball hat and black shoes

By the time I caught up with her she was already done concealing and began to leave the store.

On February 11, 2026, the reporting officer observed a female guest in the lobby area with visible bleeding from the head. The guest approached the front desk in a distressed manner and requested medical bandages. Immediate assistance was offered by the reporting officer and the Manager on Duty, Rob. Emergency Medical Services (911) were offered to the guest on two separate occasions. The guest declined ambulance assistance both times and stated that she had fallen onto a table and sustained the injury independently. Basic first aid supplies were provided upon request. The guest then exited the lobby area voluntarily. It was later confirmed that the female guest is currently registered in Room 215. After  1hour later her tall white friend come in hotel and go to her room to meet her.

Incident Report  Date: February 07, 2026  Time: 1733-1805 hours  Location: Sobeys Prince Street, Truro (871)  Reported By: LPO Gurpal Singh  At approximately 1733 hours, a female individual in her 40s, with black hair, wearing a black jacket over a black hoodie, black joggers, and carrying a pink side bag, entered the store accompanied by a male individual. The male was wearing a black cap, red hoodie, red joggers, and red shoes, and was carrying a black bag. The female was also carrying a red grocery bag that already contained items from Dollarama. Both individuals proceeded toward the washroom area and took ketchup packets from the deli section. At approximately 1752 hours, while in the pharmacy section, the female selected one box of Tampax Pearl and placed it inside her red grocery bag. They later proceeded to Checkout Six, where they paid for chocolates and two beverages only. After exiting the store, the writer stopped the individuals and recovered the unpaid box of Tampax Pearl from the female. She immediately apologized and stated that she forgot to pay for the item; however, she refused to return to the store to complete payment. The writer verbally informed both individuals that they are banned from the store for a period of six months. Photos of the individuals were obtained from CCTV footage for future reference. The manager on duty, Glen, was informed of the incident. End of Report

Incident Report  Date: February 07, 2026  Time: 2002-2010 hours  Location: Sobeys Prince Street, Truro (871)  Reported By: LPO Gurpal Singh  At approximately 2002 hours, a female individual in her 30s with brown hair, wearing a light brown hoodie, dark brown joggers, and black shoes, entered the store accompanied by a male individual. The male was wearing a black coat over a purple hoodie, black joggers, and black shoes. The female was carrying a black purse. Both individuals proceeded directly to the produce section, where they selected bread and a package of sliced lemon cake. At approximately 2006 hours, they moved through the meat section toward Aisle 7 and selected one marble cheddar cheese. While in Aisle 7, the female placed two items into her black purse. The writer was observing the individuals at the time, and the female appeared to become aware that she was being monitored. At approximately 2008 hours, the female proceeded to Aisle 5, removed the concealed items from her purse, and then went to Aisle 1. Both individuals subsequently proceeded to the self-checkout area, where they paid for all items. The total value of the items that had been concealed was approximately $30. The attempted theft was successfully deterred.  They left the store around at 2010 hours. The writer notified the manager on duty, Glen, and obtained CCTV images of both individuals involved in the incident. End of Report

fields.summary: At approximately 01:24 hours, Mary Beth Young arrived at the entrance and requested to speak with a Floor Coordinator (FC). An FC attended, and Mary Beth Young requested permission to use the washroom, which was granted. She also requested to use a phone, stating that she had been left behind by her friend and needed to contact her mother to arrange funds for a taxi. A phone was provided, and she contacted her mother. At approximately 01:36 hours, Mary Beth Young became increasingly agitated during the call, raising her voice and shouting after her mother expressed disbelief regarding her situation. FCs instructed her to lower her voice and advised that continued disruptive behaviour would result in her being asked to leave the building. Mary Beth Young terminated the call abruptly and attempted to make another call. At this point, FC Steph requested that she return the phone. Mary Beth Young responded by throwing the phone, which landed on the waiting couch near the entrance. She then proceeded to use the landline phone. Due to her escalating behaviour, FCs instructed Mary Beth Young that she was required to leave the building. I then instructed the female Security Officer on duty to assist with the removal order. Mary Beth Young became aggressive, gathered her belongings from the floor, and exited the building. Team Lead (TL) Peter informed her that her boots were still in the lobby. Mary Beth Young responded verbally with, “Shut the fuck up,” and proceeded to sit at the entrance doorway. TL Peter informed her that she was trespassing and must leave the property. Mary Beth Young stated that her boots were inside and attempted to re-enter the building. TL Peter held the door closed to prevent re-entry while communicating with staff inside to retrieve her boots, which were located near the entrance. Once retrieved, the boots were passed to TL Peter and placed next to her other belongings outside. Mary Beth Young made multiple attempts to re-enter the building. When unsuccessful, she began kicking the glass doors and door frames. TL Peter and Security Officer Oleg intervened and prevented damage to the glass. Mary Beth Young then began poking and touching staff in an attempt to provoke a response. TL Peter advised her that she was not permitted to touch staff. At the same time, TL Peter was receiving updates from FCs confirming that the Royal Newfoundland Constabulary (RNC) had been contacted and were en route. Mary Beth Young attempted to grab staff radios but was unsuccessful. To prevent damage, TL Peter passed his radio inside the building and instructed S/O Oleg to do the same. During this process, Mary Beth Young removed a phone from the back pocket of TL Peter and threw it to the ground. She then attempted to physically attack staff. At that point, staff had no alternative but to detain Mary Beth Young for safety reasons while awaiting police arrival. Detention lasted approximately 2 minutes. At approximately 01:56 hours, Officer Coley (#983) arrived on scene and attempted to speak with Mary Beth Young. He offered to transport her downtown, which she initially refused. Shortly after, Officer Parsons (#998) arrived, and Mary Beth Young requested transportation, which was provided. The RNC and Ms. Young departed the property at approximately 02:05 hours.

Two kids entered the store with an empty shopping cart. One was Black, wearing a blue hooded jacket and black pants, and was eating something from a bag he had upon entry. The other was White, wearing a black hooded jacket with a grey hoodie underneath and black pants. They moved through multiple aisles and appeared to notice they were being observed. They proceeded to the razor and razor blade section, where one of them picked up a razor. Upon seeing me, they made eye contact, discarded the razor, and exited the store without making a purchase.

Approximately at 13:10 hours, LPO, observed a suspicious man entering into the Lawtons store at Cobequid Road. LPO observed him and started following him and kept an eye on him. The man took three packs of above mentioned items and started putting into his jacket as he was trying to hide them, and then he left the store, without paying and was stopped outside the store by LPO, all the items were recovered by the LPO.

On Wednesday, February 11, 2026, at approximately 1843 hours, the facility experienced a momentary power interruption. Immediately following the restoration of electrical service, the main fire alarm panel began emitting an audible trouble signal. Upon inspection of the panel display, the Security Officer identified a supervisory alert indicating "Sprinkler Pump – No Power Available." In response to the alert, the Security Officer immediately contacted the building maintenance representative to report the fault and the preceding power fluctuation. The maintenance representative informed the officer that the alert was a standard supervisory alarm likely triggered by the brief power surge. It was confirmed that the situation was being treated as a routine maintenance item rather than an emergency. As of the conclusion of this report, the panel continues to display the supervisory alert, though there are no signs of fire, smoke, or water flow at the property. The Security Officer will continue to monitor the panel and the surrounding areas for any changes in status until the end of the shift.

Event: Recovery & Trespass from property  Location: Sobeys 644 Clayton park, 287 Lacewood Dr, Halifax, NS B3M 3Y7 Items recovered: Coffee French Van  Value: 14.99 (excluding taxes)  Details of subject: Gender: Male  Age: around 28-34 yrs Complexion: white complexion  Height: around 5’8” Weight: around 120 lbs Hair: wearing a cap Wearing a black jacket, black joggers, black shoes, black cap and had a green coloured dollarama handbag.  Vehicle Details:  Make: Volkswagen Model: Jetta Colour: Dark blue Number plate: no plate on either side  On 07/02/2026, writer Amandeep Singh Jaggi was scheduled to work as Loss Prevention Investigator at Sobeys 644, Clayton park from 1730 hrs to 2300 hrs. Incident Details:  At approximately 01:24 hours, Mary Beth Young arrived at the entrance and requested to speak with a Floor Coordinator (FC). FC Theresa attended, and Ms. Young requested permission to use the washroom, which was granted by FC. She also requested to use a phone, stating that she had been left behind by her friend and needed to contact her mother to arrange funds for a taxi.  A phone was provided by FC Stephanie, and she contacted her mother. At approximately 01:36 hours, Ms. Young became increasingly agitated during the call, raising her voice and shouting after her mother expressed disbelief regarding her situation. FCs instructed her to lower her voice and advised that continued disruptive behaviour would result in her being asked to leave the building.  Ms. Young terminated the call abruptly and attempted to make another call. At this point, FC Stephanie requested that she return the phone. Ms. Young responded by throwing the phone, which landed on the waiting couch near the entrance. She then proceeded to use the landline phone.  Due to her escalating behaviour, FCs instructed Ms. Young that she was required to leave the building right now. T/L Peter then instructed S/O Kanika on duty to assist with the removal order. Ms. Young became aggressive, gathered her belongings from the floor, and exited the building.  T/L Peter and S/O Kanika informed her that her boots were still in the lobby. Ms. Young responded verbally with, “Shut the fuck up,” and proceeded to sit at the entrance doorway. T/L Peter informed her that she was trespassing and must leave the property. Ms. Young stated that her boots were inside and attempted to re-enter the building.  TL Peter held the door closed to prevent re-entry while communicating with staff inside to retrieve her boots which were located near the entrance, but she again tried to enter the building and then S/O Kanika stopped and keep her away from the door. Once retrieved, the boots were passed to T/L Peter and placed next to her other belongings outside.  Ms. Young made multiple attempts to re-enter the building. When unsuccessful, she began kicking the glass doors and door frames. T/L Peter and S/O Oleg intervened and prevented damage to the glass. Ms. Young then began poking and touching security staff in an attempt to provoke a response.  At the same time, FCs confirmed to Security staff that the Royal Newfoundland Constabulary (RNC) had been contacted and were en route. Ms. Young attempted to grab security staff radios but was unsuccessful. To prevent damage, T/L Peter and S/O Oleg passed their radios inside the building. While giving the radios, Ms. Young removed a phone from the back pocket of T/L Peter and threw it on the ground. She then attempted to physically attack security staff. At that point, staff had no alternative but to detain Ms. Young for safety reasons while awaiting police arrival. Detention lasted approximately 2 minutes.  At approximately 01:56 hours, RNC arrived on the scene and attempted to speak with Ms. Young. The Officer offered to transport her downtown, which she initially refused. Shortly after, female officer arrived, and Ms. Young requested transportation, which was provided.  The RNC and Ms. Young departed the property at approximately 02:05 hours.

My name is Vishal Mangvana, and I am working as an LPO at the Sobeys Westside store. On the date of the incident, while patrolling the sales floor at approximately 5:00 PM, I observed a suspicious male enter the store. The individual appeared to be approximately 30–35 years old, around 6 feet tall, fair in complexion, and was wearing blue jeans, a blue hoodie, a black jacket, a black cap, and brown shoes. He was observed selecting merchandise while moving through the store, and his behavior remained under observation. At approximately 5:09 PM, the suspect attempted to exit the store without making any attempt to pay for the selected merchandise. I immediately approached the individual near the exit and identified myself as store security. Upon confrontation, the suspect became verbally aggressive, used rough and inappropriate language, and refused to cooperate. He did not answer any questions or provide identification and quickly left the area. Store management was informed immediately following the incident, and police were contacted. Due to officers being occupied at the time, dispatch advised us to wait until an officer became available and confirmed that they would contact the store. No police report number has been provided at this time, as it will be generated upon the arrival of the attending officer. The total value of the merchandise involved was $336.18. All items were successfully recovered, and no loss was incurred by the store. No injuries were reported as a result of this incident.

Incident Report – Intoxicated Individual Date: February 7, 2026 Time: 2033 hrs – 2145 hrs Location: 1875 Barrington Street, Halifax, NS Property: The Barrington Hotel Individual Description: Gender: Female Race: White Approximate Age: 25 years Clothing: Black attire Condition: Appeared heavily intoxicated Incident Details: At approximately 2033 hrs, the writer observed a white female, approximately 25 years of age, wearing black attire, sleeping in the Grab and Go area near the front entrance of the hotel. The writer approached the individual and initiated verbal communication. The individual was advised that sleeping in that area was not permitted. The individual appeared disoriented and heavily intoxicated but was responsive. After a few minutes, the writer escorted the individual to the Front Desk in an attempt to assist her in obtaining a room. Upon assessment, the staff supervisor on site denied entry and accommodation due to the individual’s high level of intoxication, citing safety concerns. Due to the individual’s condition and inability to safely remain on the premises, the writer contacted non-emergency police at approximately 2033 hrs and provided a detailed description of the situation. At approximately 2045 hrs, a police officer (Badge #518) arrived on site in vehicle C37. The writer briefed the officer on the incident and the actions taken. The officer spoke with the individual and later contacted her father to inform him of the situation. At approximately 2115 hrs, the police officer departed the premises and advised the writer that the individual’s father was en route. At approximately 2145 hrs, the individual’s father arrived on site and safely escorted her off the premises. No further issues were observed. Throughout the incident, there were no disturbances, injuries, or property damage reported. Note: During this incident, the writer remained with the individual and did not conduct routine site patrols to ensure safety.

fields.summary: At approximately 07:00 hours, I observed a resident via CCTV at Door 4 behaving suspiciously and repeatedly looking through the peephole and approaching Door 4 like he wanted to open it. Team Lead TL Peter immediately proceeded to Door 4 accompanied by Floor Coordinator FC Steph. As we approached the corridor leading to Door 4, I observed the resident look through the peephole again and then run upstairs. I followed the resident up the stairwell. By the time I reached the passageway above Door 3, Security Officer S/O Oleg had intercepted the individual. The resident was identified as MB309. He stated that he was “playing a prank” to see if staff would notice his actions. During further interaction, the resident appeared to be under the influence, as he was observed staggering on the stairs. FC Steph assessed the situation and confirmed that no further action was required at that time. TL Peter returned to the lobby.

As the muffin box was contaminated, it was discarded.

INCIDENT REPORT  Location: Sobeys Aberdeen Date: February 13, 2026 Time: 1555 hours Store Number: 370 LPO: Nijo Joy  ⸻  On February 13, 2026, at approximately 1555 hours, the writer was conducting regular patrol inside the store when information was received regarding two individuals with a previous history of theft.  The writer immediately proceeded to the pharmacy area, where the individuals were reported to be located. The writer observed both individuals continuously to monitor for any signs of concealment or theft. After following them for a period of time, no suspicious activity or concealment was observed. The individuals proceeded to Aisle/Register #5 and completed their purchase.  The writer requested assistance from a manager to gather additional information about the individuals; however, the manager did not have any information regarding them.  Due to their suspicious behavior and prior history, the writer reviewed CCTV footage for further investigation. Upon reviewing the footage, the writer identified a female individual wearing a grey jacket, black dress, with brown hair, approximately 45–50 years of age. The footage showed the female concealing one unidentified item inside her jacket. fields.summary: While on duty, writer observed two white male individuals approximately 5 ft 9 inches in height inside the store after closing time. Writer informed both individuals that the store was closed and asked them to proceed toward the exit. The individuals became verbally aggressive and proceeded toward the exit door. Writer informed them that she could open the door properly for them; however, they ignored the instruction. One of the individuals kicked the exit door, and both subjects then forcefully pushed the door, causing damage and breaking it. The suspects immediately fled the scene in a black-colored vehicle with license plate HAC 786. Police were informed immediately, and a report was filed under file number 26-25024. All details have been documented, and any available footage will be preserved for investigation. Status: Property damage and aggressive behavior. Police notified.

On patrol at approximately 20:25PM while crossing the Salter Zone parking lot, a white male (approx. 20–30 years old, wearing a long brown coat) approached me stating that another individual following him was abusing his child; the male then ran toward the parking lot before providing further details. I approached the second individual, who identified himself as Gagandeep, and he stated that the first male had been harassing his family by taking photos of them and their vehicle because his child was crying after they were unable to purchase ice cream as the cows shop was closed. In my presence, the child cried and repeatedly asked for ice cream from his mother “mama ice cream” while being placed back into the vehicle by Gagandeep's wife, and no visible signs of physical abuse were observed on the child; a second child was seated in the other seat peacefully. The first male was observed watching from a distance, and when I attempted to speak with him, he refused and ran away. Based on my observations, there were no signs of physical altercation or abuse. I advised the family to contact non-emergency police to report the incident, as the male had taken photos of their vehicle and plate number, and I provided them with the office contact number and my name upon request. (902-818-0470 Gagandeep’s mobile number)The family reported the matter to police and departed the area at 20:38. Area cleared with no further incident.

He then went to the dairy section and selected one pack of milk. After that, he proceeded to Cashier #3, where he concealed two Aero bars and three Chunky Mega Caramel chocolate bars in his jacket. fields.summary: At approximately 12:32 hrs, the writer observed a female suspect in the deli section who appeared to be attempting to conceal produce items.  At approximately 12:45 hrs, the writer received a call from an employee in the meat department near the dumplings section. The employee advised that there was a suspicious male in the department. The employee initially described the individual as having red hair, carrying a large backpack, and having selected multiple packs of beef.  Based on this information, the writer began searching for the described individual. Due to confusion and the presence of multiple customers in the area, the writer did not approach anyone immediately to avoid stopping the wrong person. The writer requested the meat department employee to attend the area to positively identify the suspect; however, the employee was unable to leave his department due to staff shortage. The employee later advised that the suspect might have already exited the store.  With the manager’s permission, the writer reviewed CCTV footage and confirmed the suspect’s full description. The male suspect was observed wearing a black jacket over a blue hoodie, light blue jeans, sunglasses, and carrying a large black backpack. He had short brown hair (almost buzz cut) and a beard.  CCTV footage showed the suspect selecting four (4) packs of beef from the meat section, each valued at approximately $25. The suspect then proceeded to the natural food section where he concealed the meat items inside his jacket. The suspect later went to Aisle 1 and selected one body wash item valued at approximately $15, which was concealed in either Aisle 3 or 4. At approximately 12:48 hrs, the suspect exited the store without making payment.  The total estimated value of stolen merchandise is approximately $115.  At approximately 13:15 hrs, the writer observed an individual matching the same description at the bus stop near the store. Might be he was the same person .The individual boarded Bus Number 3 travelling towards Mumford Terminal. Police complaint has been filed.

On 19 February 2026 at approximately 10:45 AM, I, Vishal Mangvana, working as a Security Officer at Aberdeen Business Centre, was conducting a routine patrol of the parking lot when I received a call from the maintenance department. Maintenance informed me that two young individuals, one male and one female teenager, were drinking alcohol and screaming near the mall entrance area. I immediately proceeded to the location to assess the situation. Upon arrival, I observed both individuals behaving in a loud and disruptive manner. I instructed them to leave the premises immediately; however, they ignored my instructions and continued their disruptive behavior. Due to their non-compliance, the maintenance staff contacted the police department. Unfortunately, we were informed that the police were occupied with another urgent matter and were unable to attend at that time. After some time, Brian Diops accompanied me to the scene, and together we instructed the individuals multiple times to vacate the premises. Both individuals became aggressive, shouting loudly and refusing to cooperate. As a result of their continued non-compliance and aggressive behavior, we verbally served them with a Property Protection Act (PPA) notice and clearly instructed them not to return to the premises. No physical confrontation occurred during the incident, and the matter was documented for record and future reference. Names were keigan turple and grace hayden later confirmed by maintenance .

T/L SABRINA started shift at HORIZONS AT 106 on February 14,2026 at 16:00. At approximately 17:35, resident KL (344) entered the lobby while loudly cussing out in vulgar language and appeared visibly agitated. The verbal outburst lasted for several seconds before she momentarily stopped.  T/L SABRINA was monitoring the situation when resident CT (336) attempted to joke with KL(344) in an effort to calm her down. KL(344) responded by cussing at CT(336), which he took in a light heart and laughed it off. Then she moved toward the restaurant area, where she sat in a chair opposite the restaurant and attempted to listen to music using her headphones.  Shortly afterward, KL(344) became frustrated as her headphones were not functioning properly and began cursing again. CT(336) continued attempting to joke with her, which appeared to further escalate her agitation. T/L SABRINA intervened and asked CT(336) to stop interacting with her. F/C GEORGIA also approached and spoke with her. She stopped cursing momentarily, and T/L SABRINA remained nearby to monitor the situation in case of further escalation.  At that time, S/S JAMES, who was seated across from KL(344) in the restaurant area, attempted to engage her in conversation to lighten her mood. She then again responded by yelling at S/S JAMES. T/L SABRINA then calmly suggested that KL(334) move to the visitors’ lounge, as the lobby/restaurant area was crowded and interactions and noise were expected, especially during supper time.  KL(344) began screaming at T/L SABRINA and started slamming her head against a nearby pillar, stating that she was adamant about remaining in that location to listen to her music. T/L SABRINA informed her that if she wished to remain in the area, she would need to remain calm and refrain from creating a disturbance. She reiterated that the visitors’ lounge still remained an option.  KL(344) then began screaming frantically and ran outside the building. F/C LISA arrived shortly afterward and called out to KL(344) from behind, but she did not respond and continued leaving the premises. F/C LISA came to the lobby and addressed those present, stating, “SHE SAID SHE WANTS TO BE ALONE AND IS JUST TRYING YO LISTEN TO HER MUSIC! ANYONE AND EVERYONE TALKING TO HER OR YELLING AT HER IS NOT HELPING!”, before returning to the F/C office. The resident didn’t create any more disturbance after this.

Incident Report: Theft (Sobeys Store #370)  On February 19, 2026, at approximately 14:45, an unidentified male suspect entered Sobeys Aberdeen in New Glasgow (Store #370) wearing a black North Face jacket, black lowers, grey shoes, and a hoodie with a cap worn underneath that obscured his face. The suspect, estimated to be between 35–40 years of age, selected a package of chicken wings ($4.49) from the deli and requested a salad (approx. $5.00), resulting in a total potential loss of $9.49. When questioned by deli staff regarding payment, the suspect stated he would pay at the self-checkout; however, he instead proceeded to Baby Aisle 1, where he concealed the items inside his jacket. Loss Prevention Bhupinder Singh was patrolling the store and was informed of the situation shortly thereafter by a staff member who noted the suspect had bypassed the deli payment counter. By the time the security was alerted and investigated the suspect's movements, the individual had already exited the store through the main entrance at 14:49 without attempting to pay. This incident has been formally reported by Bhupinder Singh to store management for internal documentation, and no police were involved at the time of this report.

While on duty as a Security Guard at the Highland Square Mall on February 19, 2026 in New Glasgow at approx  (19:35) I saw a  male teenager walking by me in the eastern hall of the Mall  I recognized him as a youth that the New Glasgow police were looking for in regard to an incident that occured at the Burger King Restaurant on Westville Road a couple nights before just in front of the Mall property. ( The exact date is recorded in my Log- in notes.).  He was approx  5' 8" in height, slim build and had floppy , afro type hair protruding from a dark tuk hat.  He was accompanied by two other Caucasian male youths. The approx. age of the 3 males were (16 to 18 years old) They were all dressed in black clothing and the shortest of the three males was carrying a black nap sack that had a white strip down the left and right side corners. At (19:36) I called the New Glasgow Police Dispatcher but lost my phone connection so approx. at (19:38) I recalled the Dispatcher who then connected me with Sargeant Paris ( employee # 271). I explained to him that a couple nights before NG police approached me while on duty in the Highland Square Mall at approx (20:30) and asked me if I had seen a certain teenager in the area ( that fit the description in a photo, he showed me). I told Sargeant Paris I just saw the person they were asking about walk passed me in the east hall and was headed toward the east end Mall entrance doors. Three teens were possibly heading toward the Canadian Tire Store  Sargeant Paris said he will have to first find the occurrence report and read up on it to see if there is any justification to seek out the suspect to question or arrest. I said that's fine and ended the call. Approx. at ( 20:07) I was in the progress of doing Mall Lock up procedures towards  the east end entrance doors and turning the corner,  l  discovered the 3 male youths were sitting on the two automatic massage chairs located at the far east end hall. I decided to walk through the East entrance doors for enough privacy in order to recall the NG police to give them the suspect's location . The Dispatcher then informed me Sargeant Paris and all available police are presently answering a 911 call that has the priority so they cannot come to assist me at this time but he will send a police officer my way (asap). I asked for advice on what to do since the Mall is not officially closed till (21:00) hours. He said just let them stay in the Mall and hopefully they will have  an available police officer to come to the Mall soon. I said Ok and ended the call. I then continued my lock up duties and told the 3 males that they cannot stay in the east end or come to the center hall and sit down untill the Mall closes at ( 9 pm). They opted to stay in the black chairs while I continued my duties. Approx. ( 20:27) While I was standing guard outside the main entrance doors and waiting to direct the police to enter the main entrance if they soon show up, I saw the suspect walking with the other two males passed me along the front wall area of the Mall. They were talking out loud about being hungry and looked like they were walking towards Walmart. At approx  (20:43)  The NG Police Dispatcher called me on my work phone and connected me to Sargeant Paris who told me that because they are too busy dealing with a domestic violence incident he will have to delay reading up on the occurrence report concerning the suspect in the Mall. I then told him that the suspect was now walking towards Walmart. He then informed me that he already knows who the suspect is by name and also knows where he lives so if he finds justification in the occurrence report to go arrest the suspect he will do that after he has available time to read the report . I said that would be fine and thanked him for the call then I ended the call. End of report.

At approximately 17:40 hours, while the writer was standing at the entrance door, Cashier Cathy approached the writer and advised that an elderly white female, approximately 5’5” in height, wearing a black jacket, pink pants, white shoes, glasses, and having purple hair, carrying a brown purse, was in the store. Cathy further stated that the female was previously known for theft-related incidents and requested the writer to keep observation on her.  The writer began monitoring the suspect. At approximately 17:45 hours, the suspect completed a purchase at the pharmacy cashier counter and proceeded toward the exit. As she attempted to leave, the anti-theft alarm system activated.  The writer politely requested the suspect to stop and asked whether she had purchased all items in her possession. The suspect replied that she had and presented a receipt. The writer then requested that she return to the cashier to verify the purchased item, as it was possible that the security tag (chip) had not been properly deactivated.  The suspect then returned to the pharmacy area and presented an item to Pharmacy Assistant Rina for verification. Writer was also with the suspect.Rina confirmed that the item shown was not the one triggering the alarm system.  The suspect again attempted to exit the store. The writer requested that she step aside and pass through the exit without the item to verify the alarm activation. At this point, the suspect began raising her voice and making excuses. The writer issued a clear verbal warning and requested that she remain calm and not yell.  Cashier Cathy joined the interaction. Both the writer and Cathy advised the suspect that if she had forgotten to pay for any item, she must either pay for it or return it immediately.  The suspect then admitted that she had taken one pack of Robaxisal ES from the pharmacy section and forgot to pay for it. She stated that she had just come from the hospital and provided multiple explanations for the oversight.  The item, valued at approximately $24.00, was taken to the cashier, and the suspect paid for it. She exited the store at approximately 17:49 hours without further incident.

Date/Time: feb 17,2026 – During. 17:15 – 18:30 Location: Canadian Tire – bayers lake Halifax Front desk / exit area   Witnesses Present: Front End Manager, General Manager (Norman), responding two female police officers Narrative: At approximately 17:15, the writer observed a female customer exiting the store carrying a Walmart shopping bag and wearing a backpack and jacket that covered her head, while she exited the security sensors activated (beeped), The writer approached the customer and politely requested to inspect her receipt as per store policy. The customer voluntarily presented the receipt. While reviewing it, the writer briefly explained the store’s bag check policy to the customer in case she had any concerns. The customer then exited the store without further incident, and the writer returned to the assigned post. At approximately 17:16, the same customer returned to the front desk and attempted to file a complaint regarding the security and the store’s bag check policy. The Front End Manager explained that Canadian Tire maintains a bag check policy, which is enforced when the exit sensors activate (beep). The customer then became agitated, took out her phone, and began recording staff members. The writer, standing at a safe distance, calmly asked the customer to lower her voice and refrain from yelling at staff. In response, the customer became verbally abusive toward the writer, including directing profanity and displaying her middle finger. The Front End Manager observed this behavior and instructed the customer to leave the property due to her disruptive conduct. The customer then contacted police, alleging that staff had threatened her and that security had approached her too closely. The writer informed the customer (from a distance) that the incident was being captured on audio and video surveillance and again requested that she calm down. The customer responded with further profanity (“fuck you”). The writer replied that disrespect would not be tolerated and that continued abusive behavior would create further issues. The entire exchange occurred in the presence of General Manager Norman. Police attended the scene shortly  around 1742, thereafter. Officers took statements from the writer, the customer, and available witnesses. They also reviewed the in-store CCTV footage. Following their review, police determined that the customer had been overly aggressive, had overreacted, and had made false allegations against staff and the security. Police escorted the customer off the property and spoke with the writer regarding her misconduct and unfounded claims. The writer informed police that, due to the customer’s aggressive behavior, verbal abuse, and refusal to follow reasonable directions, she was formally trespassed from the premises. The officer didn’t had incident number as they want to end the situation  The incident was concluded without further escalation. Management later debriefed with the writer and confirmed that the customer’s allegations were unfounded and that her behavior had been disrespectful and inappropriate. Outcome: •  Customer trespassed from the property. •  No injuries, no property damage, no further police action required. •  Incident fully documented on CCTV. End of report.

Incident Report – Lost Youth Assisted by Police  Time: 9:10 PM Location: War Memorial Site  At approximately 9:10 PM, a young male approached me stating that he was lost and required assistance. He appeared visibly distressed and was crying. I calmly asked him for details regarding where he needed to go. He stated that he was trying to reach 100 Forest Road.  The youth had a scooter with him and explained that the scooter’s battery had died. He further mentioned that his foot was injured (blistered/burst), and he was unable to walk properly.  He informed us that he had all the address details of his friend saved on his tablet; however, he did not show the tablet to us at that time. When the police officer arrived, the youth stated that he could provide and show all necessary details and proof from his tablet if required.  While assisting him, a police vehicle was observed passing near the War Memorial. I signaled to the officer, who stopped immediately. I explained the full situation to the officer, and Zion also provided clarification.  The officer, Constable J.M. Brazil (Badge #1003), handled the matter professionally and with care. After speaking with the youth and confirming the necessary information, the officer kindly offered him a ride to his stated destination to ensure his safety.
