Incident Report – Verbal Abuse, Threats & Intoxicated Individual  Time: 9:28 PM Location: War Memorial   At approximately 9:28 PM, one male individual attended the site and was observed removing snow from a grave using his foot in a disrespectful manner.  Video footage has been recorded clearly showing the individual removing snow from the grave using his foot.  I approached the individual calmly and respectfully and asked what he was doing. The individual immediately became aggressive and verbally confrontational. He made personal and discriminatory remarks, including telling me to “go back to your country” and stating that we are “not from here.” He further stated that they can do anything at the site and questioned my authority to stop him.  I informed him in a calm and professional tone that I am a security officer assigned to protect the grave and the site. When he asked for my name, I responded professionally by stating, “Security Officer.”  The individual continued using abusive language and stepped very close to my face in an attempt to provoke a physical altercation. He verbally indicated that he wanted to fight. Throughout the interaction, my hands remained behind my back and I did not make any physical contact.  Based on his unsteady walking, aggressive tone, and overall behavior, the individual appeared heavily intoxicated and not in a normal state of mind.  Before leaving the site, the individual made further threatening statements, saying that he could call “5–6 friends” to the location and that I would “see what will happen.” These statements were made in a warning and intimidating manner while he was exiting the area. The individual then left the site aggressively.  Description of Individual: 	•	Approx. 5’11” in height 	•	Wearing glasses 	•	Blue hat 	•	Green jacket 	•	Sky blue trousers 	•	Black shoes  Due to his aggressive behavior and verbal threats, I discreetly signaled the other security guard to capture photographs from behind without alerting the individual for documentation purposes.  No physical altercation occurred. The situation was managed professionally and without escalation.  Evidence: 	•	Photographs taken and attached for documentation. 	•	Video footage of the snow removal and incident has been recorded. 	•	Video has been sent to Nora for review and record purposes.

OH&S Incident Report  Date: February 21 Time: 14:40 Location: War Memorial Guards on Duty: Jake Druken (Reporting Officer), Brandon  Report:  At approximately 14:40, while on a 15minute break in the patrol vehicle, I (Jake Druken) observed the other on-duty officer (Brandon) motioning for me to exit the vehicle. I immediately exited the patrol car and jogged over to Officer Brandon.  Officer Brandon informed me that an elderly male had slipped and fallen on the stairs of the War Memorial, near the crow’s nest area. Officer Brandon requested that I remain on watch at the memorial while he proceeded to check on the individual.  By the time Officer Brandon reached the individual, the man had already stood back up. The individual stated that he did not hit his head and believed he was not injured.  I advised Officer Brandon that the area had not been salted, as I was on site the previous day and observed Kelloways Ltd personnel shoveling snow but not applying salt to the War Memorial area. Based on this information, I contacted the City of St. John’s to request that the area be salted.  As of 14:51, I had been on hold with the City of St. John’s for approximately 10 minutes awaiting assistance.

At approximately 17:35 hours, while conducting routine patrol inside the store, the writer observed three underage girls walking together through various aisles.  The first individual is described as a white female, approximately 5’7” in height, heavy build, wearing a white t-shirt, black trousers, and carrying a black backpack. The second individual is described as a Black female, approximately 5’9” in height, heavy build, wearing all black clothing, with black hair and carrying a black school backpack. The third individual is described as a slim female, approximately 5’4” in height, wearing all black clothing and carrying a black school bag.  The writer observed the first female select one Strawberry Soda valued at $3.69. The second female selected one Cheeky Cherry can valued at $2.69 and one Beef Jerky valued at $7.49. The group then proceeded to the next aisle, where the third female was observed placing the Strawberry Soda into the second female’s backpack.  Shortly after, the second female selected one Excel Spearmint gum valued at $4.69. The group moved toward the second-last frozen aisle, behaving normally to avoid suspicion. The writer then observed the third female place the Excel gum into the second female’s backpack.  The group later moved toward aisle 5 or 6, where the first female placed additional selected items into her own backpack, with assistance from the other two females. The writer continuously observed the concealment and selection of merchandise.  At approximately 17:48 hours, all three females proceeded past the self-checkout area and exited the store without making any attempt to pay for the concealed merchandise.  Once outside the store area, the writer approached the individuals, identified himself, and informed them of the observed incident. The writer requested that they either return to the store to pay for the items or return the merchandise immediately. The individuals agreed to return all items.  During recovery, the writer also located one Strawberry Lemonade valued at $4.29 among the concealed merchandise.  All items were recovered in sellable condition. The writer issued a verbal PPA (Prohibition of Premises Act notice) to the individuals. The writer then returned inside the store and obtained a void receipt from the cashier supervisor for documentation purposes. The total is 24.92 dollars.
fields.merchandise_description: Beef jerkey teriyaki, excel spearmint, lemonade strawberry, soda strawberry, cheeky cherry.

Officer Baljeet observed an adult white female age round 35 ,  in the meat department select three packs of steak. The suspect then proceeded to the candy aisle, where she placed the steak into a large black reusable bag, concealing the merchandise. The suspect later approached the cash register area with a shopping cart but only paid for bananas, making no attempt to pay for the concealed items inside the black reusable bag. After completing the transaction, the suspect initially attempted to exit through the mall door exit, which was closed, and then redirected toward the main store exit at 9.30 pm . Upon exiting the store, Officer Baljeet approached the suspect outside, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and informed her that she had not paid for the items in her bag. The suspect denied the allegation. When Officer Baljeet attempted to recover the merchandise by taking hold of the bag, the suspect became agitated and pulled away, then fled the scene. She entered a black hatchback vehicle with no visible license plate and left the area at a high rate of speed almost ran over officer Baljeet while reversing the car . Officer Baljeet did not pursue further and contacted the RCMP non-emergency line to report the incident and file a theft complaint.

fields.summary: While working as a security guard at Highland Square Mall, New Glasgow NS, at (17:52) - I received a call on my work phone by the Bluenotes staff to come and stand guard outside the shop until a couple of customers leave, because they are acting suspicious.to the staff. They are a male and female approx in their early to mid twenties,  standing approx. 5' 7"" and both are a slim build. The male has an inflamed pimpled rash on the left side of his face ( according to the Bluenotes supervisor's description, who was an eye witness to the activity of the 2 customers in question). He entered the dressing room with a full blue duffle bag.with a white logo. I stood outside the shop area until the couple in question left the shop and headed towards the center hall area. I stayed for a few minutes to watch 3 teen males move about the shop. One kept a white hood over his head as he shop concealing most of his face. A bluenotes staff person came out to me to say things are ok now. I then told her to watch those 3 males because they were acting a bit suspicious. I then walked to the center of the Mall to see if the couple was still in view.... (18:00) - I then saw a young adult couple ,, ( male & female) that may have been the same couple in question,  exiting the washroom hall and walking to the West end where they exited the Mall. They were each carrying a blue duffle bag with a white logo. End of report.

Officer Baljeet was performing his duties on the sales floor when he was approached by the store manager, Nicole, who informed him that a female individual currently inside the store had previously been banned for theft-related incidents and was only permitted to enter the premises for the purpose of obtaining prescriptions from the pharmacy. Upon receiving this information, Officer Baljeet immediately acknowledged the concern and began actively searching the sales floor for the individual. After a brief patrol, he successfully identified the female matching the description provided. Officer Baljeet maintained continuous observation and followed her movements as she proceeded through the store. The individual was observed entering the soft drink aisle, where she selected one  bottle of soft drink, She then proceeded toward the pharmacy area with the selected merchandise. At that point, Officer Baljeet approached and intervened, informing the pharmacist that the individual was not authorized to shop for general merchandise due to her prior ban. Officer Baljeet then identified himself clearly as a Loss Prevention Officer and advised the individual that she was in violation of the store’s trespass conditions by shopping beyond pharmacy-related items. The individual was cooperative at that time, and Officer Baljeet escorted her out of the store. Once outside, the individual voluntarily surrendered the merchandise that she had taken from the sales floor. Following this, Officer Baljeet escorted the individual back inside to the manager’s office, where he contacted the store manager, Nicole, and briefed her on the situation, including the attempted theft and violation of the existing ban. Officer Baljeet got her personal information, her name was Marlana Hyrtle , born on on 1985/05/10 . In the presence of the manager, Officer Baljeet issued the individual a trespass notice for a period of six months, reinforcing the restriction of her access to the store. The individual complied with the instructions and exited the premises at approximately 2:30 PM without further incident.

I am employed as a Loss Prevention Officer (LPO) at Sobeys, Stellarton location. On the date of the incident at approximately 6:06 PM, while patrolling the sales floor, I observed a suspicious male enter the store with a shopping cart. I immediately flagged the individual and continued active surveillance. The male appeared to be approximately 30–35 years old and was wearing a black cap, black hoodie, black jacket, black lower pants, and sneakers. He had visible tattoos on his neck and face. The suspect proceeded to select various merchandise throughout the store and placed the items into his cart. At approximately 6:25 PM, he moved to the self-serve checkout area where he scanned all the merchandise. However, he pretended to complete the payment process without making any actual payment. At approximately 6:30 PM, the suspect attempted to exit the store without paying for the merchandise. A store employee attempted to stop the individual and requested that he pay for the items. The suspect responded by pushing the employee and, in the process, damaged the gift card section by forcefully pushing the cart into it. As the suspect entered the vestibule area, I approached him, identified myself as store security, and took possession of the bags containing the unpaid merchandise. The suspect attempted to snatch the bags back from me, but I used necessary force to maintain control of the merchandise. During the struggle, the suspect managed to leave the store with one bag. I followed the suspect to the parking lot. He entered a white Nissan sedan with Ontario license plate DBCS469 and sat in the front passenger (right) seat. He placed the bag in front of him and spoke briefly with the driver. Shortly afterward, the suspect exited the vehicle and fled on foot toward the street. I recovered the remaining merchandise from inside the vehicle. The female driver and another occupant stated they had only driven him to the store and claimed they did not know him. All merchandise, valued at approximately $407.59, was fully recovered. The Manager on Duty was informed immediately, and police were contacted via the non-emergency line. Within a few minutes, Officer Grant MacAdam (Badge #167) arrived on scene and gathered all relevant information. Surveillance footage was secured and provided to police for investigation. Police report number 2026210059 was assigned to this incident. The suspect was later identified by police as 36-year-old Kyle Marsters, who was reportedly already known to police. Any further updates will be documented and shared as they become available.

Loss Prevention Officer Baljeet ,  was conducting surveillance on the sales floor when he observed a white male individual, approximately 36 years of age, wearing a black baseball cap, blue jeans, a blue t-shirt, and a green jacket . The individual selected a  frozen pizza and was observed concealing it inside a black reusable grocery bag. The individual then proceeded toward the mall door exit and left the store without making any attempt to pay for the merchandise. Officer Baljeet maintained continuous observation and followed the individual outside the store after confirming the theft. Once outside, Officer Baljeet approached the individual, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and directed him to return the merchandise immediately , he handover the entire bag to officer Baljeet , Upon telling her to come back inside the store , the individual failed to comply and instead fled on foot, running in the direction of commercial Street. Due to the individual’s  attempted theft ,the individual was verbally banned from the store for a period of 6 months .

At approximately 9:28 PM, while conducting routine patrol inside the store, the writer observed a white middle-aged female, approximately in her 40s, average build, wearing a green jacket, light blue jeans, glasses, and black sneakers with white soles. She had brown hair and was carrying two large black grocery bags.Due to her suspicious behavior, the writer maintained observation.The female initially had two bottles of pop in her hand (one Pepsi valued at approximately $3.00 and another beverage valued at approximately $3.49). She selected a wrap valued at approximately $7.00. She then proceeded to the produce section, where she selected additional merchandise valued at approximately $3.00.Afterward, she moved toward the deli section from the meat side and selected two packs of tortillas valued at approximately $6.00 each. She later returned one pack to the shelf and kept one. She then returned to the produce section and selected one pack of salad valued at approximately $6.00. The suspect then proceeded toward the pharmacy aisle by the natural foods section, and later went to Aisle 7 where she selected one sausage product valued at approximately $6.00. She then moved to Aisle 11 and selected one Ruffles sausage valued at approximately $7.00.The female then went to Aisle 9, where she concealed the merchandise inside her grocery bags.She proceeded past Cashier Counter #8 and exited the store without making any attempt to pay for the concealed merchandise.  The writer followed and approached the female outside the store after she exited. The writer requested that she either return to the store to pay for the merchandise or surrender the unpaid items. The female did not respond and continued walking away. The writer made a second request, at which point the female began yelling and making excuses.The writer issued a verbal PPA (Prohibited Person Act notice) banning her from the property for six months and advised that a police complaint would be filed.  The female left the premises at approximately 9:42 PM and proceeded toward the bus stop located in front of McDonald’s.The total estimated value of the stolen merchandise is approximately $40.00. Police complaint has been filed.

S/O Faraz had a shift today at Kelsey Drive NLC from 1300 to 2100 hours on Wednesday, February 25th, 2026. At 1733, a male approximately 65 years of age, about 5’6” in height, with a grey beard, wearing an army green pant with a hoodie on his head and sunglasses, proceeded to the whisky wall. He put three items in his cart and then went to the cooler area. Later, he placed beer cans in his cart, and when S/O Faraz passed by him, one of the cans fell from his pocket and into the cart. Since he was a regular customer, none of the employees doubted him. However, S/O Faraz felt something suspicious and informed the manager. After checking the cameras, it was found that the individual had concealed two bottles of whisky inside his large jacket: one Dewar’s Caribbean Smooth Scotch Whisky 750 ml valued at $39.79 CAD and one Grant’s Triple Wood Scotch 1140 ml valued at $55.98 CAD. Despite stealing the items, the individual also purchased approximately $100 worth of liquor, provided his Air Miles card information, and paid in cash. The assistant manager later mentioned that this could explain why the whisky inventory had been low over the past few months, as the individual was known as a “good old lad” and had not previously been suspected. The individual exited the store at approximately 1740 hours and the pictures are posted in the NLC WhatsApp group too.

fields.summary: Security Incident Report Date: February 27, 2026  Location: Aberdeen Business Centre, New Glasgow  Reporting Officer: Bhupinder Singh  Incident: Verbal Abuse, Threats, and Protection of Property Act (PPA) Notice  At approximately 1502 hours, while conducting a mobile patrol of the Aberdeen Business Centre, I received a distress call from the staff at Active Life Therapy. They reported a male individual in the lobby who was shouting, swearing, and cursing loudly into his phone, causing the staff and clients to feel unsafe. I arrived on-scene immediately and observed the suspect, later identified as Blain McDonalds, sitting in the lobby with a pet dog. He was described as wearing a brown jacket, blue jeans, and brown shoes. Upon my approach, I identified myself as security and attempted to speak with him. Mr. McDonalds remained on his phone and became hostile, telling me to "fuck off" several times and threatening to call his lawyer, whom he claimed was located upstairs.  During the interaction, Brian, the manager of Crombie Property, arrived and attempted to de-escalate the situation. The suspect directed the same verbal abuse and profanity toward Brian. Consequently, Brian contacted the New Glasgow Police Service for assistance. While waiting for their arrival, I retrieved the Protection of Property Act (PPA) book from the Aberdeen Sobeys office. We intercepted the suspect again in front of Sobeys as he attempted to leave the area. Two officers from the New Glasgow Police Department, including Officer B. Orrell, arrived shortly thereafter and engaged with the suspect.  As I began to document the formal PPA notice, Mr. McDonalds became increasingly agitated, shouting at the police officers. Despite police attempts to keep him on-site for the duration of the paperwork, he fled the scene while continuing his verbal outburst. In his Presence, Manager Brian and the police issued a verbal trespass notice for a period of six months, effective immediately to him . Mr. McDonalds is barred from all Crombie-owned properties until August 27, 2026. A copy of the PPA documentation was provided to Brian, and I advised the staff that if the suspect returns, they are to call the police immediately for his arrest and charging. The scene was cleared and the incident concluded at 1545 hours.

22:58 – While posted at Door 3, Residents 219 RW and 258 ED were in the hallway between Door 2 and Door 3, having a normal conversation. I was positioned nearby observing. Resident 219 RW made a joke toward Resident 258 ED, which 258 ED did not appreciate. Both residents then began verbally abusing each other. Shortly after, Resident 258 ED started walking toward his room. However, Resident 219 RW attempted to follow him and appeared to want to engage in a physical altercation.  I immediately radioed the Team Lead and positioned myself between the residents to prevent further escalation. The Floor Coordinator was present on scene and assisted. Together, we redirected Resident 219 RW by guiding him backward and preventing him from advancing further. The verbal altercation de-escalated shortly after, and both residents returned to their respective rooms. No further incidents to report at this time.

At approximately 15:45 hours, the writer observed a previously banned individual inside the store.  The suspect is described as a short Black male with a piercing on his right eyebrow. He was carrying a black bag and had a chocolate item in his hand at the time of observation.  The writer approached the suspect, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and advised him that he was previously banned from the store and must leave the premises immediately. The suspect ignored the direction and continued walking inside the store. The writer then advised him that police would be contacted if he did not leave.  The suspect proceeded to the self-checkout area, left the chocolate item (valued at $8.54), and exited the store.  Police were informed regarding the trespassing in reference to previous Report #2025-4007961. MOD Chris was notified and advised the writer to contact police immediately should the suspect return to the store in the future.

At approximately 1140 hours, the writer arrived at 5515 Victoria Rd, Halifax, NS, B3H 4J9, apartment 906. The writer knocked on the door multiple times, and a white male opened the door. Further he introduced himself as Charles-Bob Lawrence. The writer introduced himself as a Bailiff and handed over the Form D document.

At approximately 1734 hrs, LPO noticed a male having a bag chain open and the male was putting unknown 5-6 items in his bag. LPO immediately followed the male and noticed the male did not pay for the items at self checkout and exited from self checkout at approximately 1736 hrs. LPO followed the male without loosing sight and approached the male outside the exit. LPO informed the male to give items back however the male continued walking towards his car. LPO verbally banned the male from the store. LPO was able to get the license plate of the car which is Grey Honda civic HNV 082 Nova Scotia number plate. The car went towards Westside wine and beer store which leads to intersection between Radcliffe drive and dunbrack street. The stolen items are estimated to be $50 cad with taxes. LPO came back to the store at approximately 1740 hrs. LPO reported the incident to Sobeys manager Nate, ISS manager Branon. LPO reported the incident to Sobeys non emergency. LPO also reported the incident to Halifax non emergency police and received incident number which is 26-31632. Nothing further to report.   The description of the male includes,  Caucasian male, medium build, Nike red plain hoodie, in his 20s, height approximately 5’7, grey trackpants, light trimmed beard and moustache, black shoes, crème colour cap, black bag.

A white male was observed in the meat department selecting a pack of meat. At that time, he already had a salad pack in his cart. While handling the meat, he immediately concealed it inside his bag.  He then proceeded to move through various aisles, selecting additional items. Some items were concealed in his bag, while others were placed in his cart. He was also observed removing certain items in different aisles.  The male ultimately concealed one additional pack of steak and proceeded to the cashier, where he paid approximately $15 worth of merchandise. However, he had concealed merchandise valued at approximately $15 as well.  I maintained full continuity and approached him outside the store after he exited. When confronted, he stated that he did not do it intentionally and apologized. When asked to pay for the concealed merchandise, he stated that he could not pay because he did not have enough time.  I calmly requested the concealed merchandise and his identification. He complied and provided his ID. The subject was issued a six-month ban from the store. He agreed to the ban and left the premises without further incident. Exit store: 8:20 pm Description: white male, 68 years old, white hoddie with blue pants and black shoes. Blonde hair and he wore the safety vest of RFS

White male with gray hair, wearing black pants and a black jacket with a grey hoodie underneath, entered the store. Potential theft was deterred.

12:55 Merrymeeting. Upon arriving at the store I was alerted by the staff of a suspicious activity in progress. I quickly identified the subject in question and followed her around. At one point interrupting her attempt to conceal a product between the isles. Subject left the store empty-handed.

At approximately 19:40 hours, during routine patrol, the writer entered Unit 3360 Romans Avenue for inspection. Upon entry, the writer observed the unit in a disturbed condition. Clothing items were found removed from a tote box and spread throughout the living room and kitchen area. The kitchen area appeared disorganized, with various household items displaced. A seat cushion from the sofa was found partially placed on top of another seat cushion. An iPhone that was previously known to be kept on the corner table inside the residence was no longer present. Upstairs, one of the bedrooms was observed with multiple items placed on top of the bed. It appeared that some items were missing, and certain belongings had been bagged. The items that were bagged inside Unit 3360 were placed in clear garbage bags. Additionally, a black Adidas bag containing groceries and other household items was found placed next to the main entrance of Unit 3360 Romans Avenue. The writer checked with S/G Pawan regarding the condition of the unit. S/G Pawan advised that as of the previous day, no clothes had been removed from the tote bag. There were no visible signs of forced entry or trespassing. All windows were intact with no damage observed. There were no visible footprints in the snow outside near the windows. All doors were found locked at the time of entry. The writer informed Brennan McGinnis of the situation. The writer then proceeded to patrol Unit 3373 Romans Avenue, where a potential clean-up of the unit had been initiated. Several garbage bags were observed placed on the couch, and additional clean-up activity was noted upstairs. Brennan McGinnis advised the writer via phone to complete an incident report. Brennan McGinnis will contact the property manager and provide further direction once confirmation is received.

At approximately 21:15 hours, the writer observed a suspicious female inside the store.  The suspect is described as a female wearing a black jacket and carrying a pink bag. She selected various grocery items and proceeded to Cash Register #2.  The suspect paid for grocery items totaling approximately $20; however, she concealed additional merchandise, including liquid soap and beef products valued at $31.33, and did not pay for those items.  The writer approached the suspect outside the store, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and advised her that she had not paid for the concealed merchandise. The suspect complied and returned all unpaid items. She stated that she is a student, apologized for her actions, and indicated that she would not repeat the behavior.  A verbal warning was issued, and the suspect was served with a PPA notice banning her from the store for six (6) months.  All merchandise was recovered.  This report is for information purposes only.

While on routine patrol inside the store, writer observed a white male individual approximately 5 ft 5 inches in height wearing a black cap, black jacket, and black pyjamas entering the premises. During this time, he was observed placing approximately 6–7 boxes of protein products and medicines, including vitamins, into his bag. Police were contacted immediately, and a complaint was filed. The assigned police file number is 26-25840. All relevant details have been documented, and CCTV footage will be preserved for investigation. Status: Theft reported. Police notified.

LPO followed both male and female without loosing sight. LPO noticed both took 2 sparkling water bottles and paid for the bottles at checkout 7 and not for the meat. LPO followed them and approached outside at exit. Both male and female refused to steal. LPO informed both to file police report if items were not returned. The female got afraid and went to her car which was red colour Kia Seltos. The female brought 3 packs of lean meat from the black bag in her car and handed to LPO. LPO verbally banned both. The description of the female includes,  Black female, Pink hoodie, Pink trackpants, in her  20s, height approximately 5’2, black scarf covered hair, black puffer jacket, black flip flops, slim build.

A white male was observed at the store entrance engaging in suspicious behavior, repeatedly looking around and appearing to check for staff presence. fields.summary: 28 February 2026 a theft was reported around 6:39 at Harvest Beer and Wine spirits. A black male with his face covered came into the store. The store was a little bit busy. The writer was patrolling throughout the aisles, the black male with face covered garbed two bottles of tequila from one of the shelves. The guy proceeded towards the checkpoint, writer was also coming towards the same direction and the cashier was also focused on the same guy. As soon as the guy reached to the cashier he tried to run away without paying but the cashier was able to retrieve one of the bottles from the guy and the guy ended up running away with one bottle. He was not driving any vehicle, the guy just ran towards the main road. Writer called HRP and provided all the information to them.

While on routine patrol, writer observed a white male individual approximately 5 ft 7 inches in height carrying a parrot-colored bag and wearing a black jacket enter the store. The individual selected one bag of chips and additional grocery items valued at approximately $10–$15. Writer observed the individual placing the items inside his bag while inside the store. At approximately 4:02 PM, the individual attempted to exit the store without paying for the merchandise. Writer approached and attempted to stop him. The individual responded using inappropriate language, stating, “It’s just a bag of chips, don’t be a pain in my ass,” and continued walking out of the store without payment. The individual has been formally banned from the store due to theft and inappropriate conduct. Status: Theft and verbal misconduct. Subject banned from premises.

At approximately 14:20 hours, the writer observed a suspicious male inside the store.  The suspect is described as a Caucasian male, approximately middle-aged, average height and build. He was wearing a black winter jacket over a dark hoodie, an orange beanie, blue jeans, and brown footwear. He was carrying a large black reusable shopping bag.  The suspect selected multiple grocery and meat products and concealed them inside the black bag. The concealed merchandise (as confirmed by receipt and recovery) included: 	•	Green grapes (2.09 lb @ $5.99/lb) – $12.52 	•	Raspberry cordial – $3.79 	•	Cavicchi’s back bacon – $9.29 	•	Armstrong bacon package – $12.99 	•	Cavicchi’s GF local sausage – $10.78 	•	Lean ground beef (multiple packs) – $7.28, $8.55, $9.09, $7.23  Total value: $81.62  The suspect exited the store at approximately 14:27 hours without making any attempt to pay for the merchandise.  The writer approached the suspect outside the store, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and advised him that he had not paid for the items. The suspect initially denied the allegation and became verbally aggressive. After the writer told him police will be called, he handed the bag to the writer. All merchandise was successfully retrieved.  A verbal PPA notice was issued, and the suspect was banned from the store for six (6) months. Total value recovered: $81.62  Nothing further to add. This report is for information purposes only.

POSTING ON BEHALF OF T/L SABRINA JERIN MARCH 1, 2026  T/L SABRINA began her shift at HORIZONS AT 106 on March 1, 2026 at 16:00. At approximately 19:15, S/S CASEY informed T/L SABRINA that a resident had reported someone screaming near the GAZEBO. T/L SABRINA responded immediately and observed DH(248) speaking to himself. His speech was disorganized and incoherent. The resident was able to state his name correctly; however, he failed to make eye contact when prompted. His eyes appeared red, his pupils were noticeably dilated, and his complexion seemed paler than usual.   T/L SABRINA attempted verbal de-escalation and encouraged the resident to return inside the building, as he appeared to be in a mentally unstable state and remaining outside posed potential safety concerns. After approximately 15 minutes of engagement, the resident began to comply and agreed to return indoors. F/C STEPHANIE then engaged with the resident briefly and assisted in escorting him to his room.    MOBILE CRISIS UNIT was contacted and arrived at 20:13 to assess the resident. Following their assessment, they recommended that the resident remain on site and be monitored. MOBILE CRISIS UNIT departed at 20:26. There were no further escalations related to this incident.

When the writer was patrolling the store at 15:16 hours, he observed a white female wearing a black winter jacket and black trousers, with the hood of the jacket up. She was an average-built white female, approximately 5’6” in height, with a tattoo below her right eye and short blonde hair. She was also wearing black sneakers with red soles. She entered the store at 15:16 hours, and due to her suspicious behavior, the writer began observing her. She first went to the deli section, where she selected chicken strips valued at $19. She then proceeded to the meat section and selected four packs of FS SS Rib Steak (each valued at approximately $55). From there, she went to Aisle 3, passing through the pharmacy aisles. At that location, she placed the chicken pack down momentarily. She looked around, and while the writer continued observing her from a distance, she concealed the box of chicken strips inside her pants. Afterward, she carried the meat packs to the last aisle of the pharmacy section and concealed all the meat packs inside her jacket. She then walked past Cashier Counter 5 and exited the store without paying for any items. The writer followed her and approached her outside the store at approximately 15:23 hours. The writer introduced himself and explained the incident. The meat department was also nearby the exit to gave the accompany to the writer as writer informed the meat manager. He requested that she either pay for the items or return them. She denied having any unpaid items. The writer again informed her of the concealed merchandise, and she initially agreed to return only the box of chicken strips. When asked to return the meat packs, she first returned only two. After the writer warned her that the police would be contacted, she returned all the remaining items. At that time, a black middle-aged male who was accompanying her, wearing a blue jacket and brown pants and carrying black floral bags, was present. The female made a threatening statement to her partner, saying, “Did you see his face? We will see you later.” The total value of the recovered merchandise was $243.48. The writer filed a police complaint against them regarding the threat. Verbal PPA has been issued.

Incident Description: At the time of inspection, Room No. 627 was reported to have no power supply. Upon checking the electrical panel, it was observed that the circuit breaker for the room was in the OFF position.  The breaker was switched OFF completely and then turned back ON to reset it. After resetting the breaker, power was successfully restored to the room.  Action Taken:  Inspected electrical breaker panel  Reset breaker (turned OFF and then ON)  Confirmed power restoration in Room 627  Current Status: Power has been restored and is functioning normally. No further issues observed at the time of reporting.

fields.summary: POSTING ON BEHALF OF T/L SABRINA JERIN MARCH 1, 2026  T/L SABRINA began her shift at HORIZONS AT 106 on March 1, 2026 at 16:00. At approximately 21:15, KL(344) entered the lobby in a highly escalated state, yelling and using curse words due to personal distress. F/C ASHLEY was notified, who then advised  to avoid engagement with the resident to prevent further escalation. T/L SABRINA continued keeping distance and monitoring the resident for any signs of harm to herself or others and offered her assistance as needed.    At approximately 21:30, the resident verbally threatened F/C ASHLEY, GEORGIA and T/L SABRINA outside F/C OFFICE, stating she would “rip this place apart.” She then threw the bus tub located outside the kitchen area, resulting in several plates breaking. Shortly after, she caused additional disturbance and property damage by throwing several other things in the lobby.    She was then verbally informed by T/L SABRINA that continued property damage would result in physical intervention for safety reasons, the resident responded directly, stating: “F**K YOU B*TCH! TRY ME! YOU ARE NO BETTER THAN ME!”. However, the resident did stop causing property damage after the verbal advice. She subsequently exited and re-entered the building yelling, “CALL THE COPS! I DON’T FCKING CARE! I DON’T WANT TO BE HERE ANYWAYS!!”     Throughout the incident, T/L SABRINA followed the resident closely to ensure the safety of the her, staff, and other residents. Due to ongoing threats, disturbance and property damage, F/Cs contacted the RNC.    RNC arrived on site at 21:55. OFFICERS J. A. SIMÕES RE, W. W. DIXON, and S. J. HURLEY located the resident outside the property and engaged her. The resident eventually cooperated. Officers advised that grounds for arrest were not established under the circumstances. The resident agreed to comply with facility expectations provided she was granted access to the computer room, and arrangements were made accordingly.    Officers remained on site until approximately 23:00 and departed shortly after. No further escalations were observed. The resident was monitored for the remainder of the shift.

Event: Mischief and verbal warning  Location: Sobeys 670 Sackville Drive, 752 Sackville Dr, Lower Sackville, NS B4E 1R7 Items involved: “Custard cake slice” kitchen item  Value: $4.98 (excluding taxes)  On 02/03/2026, writer Amandeep Singh Jaggi was scheduled to work as Loss Prevention Investigator at Sobeys 670 Sackville Drive from 1230 hrs to 2100 hrs.  At approximately 1300 hrs, while writer was patrolling the store, writer observed a male inside the store. The subject had a cart and a bag kept in it. The subject kept on selecting items from the store.  The subject selected above mentioned item from store and started consuming the same while roaming around. The writer maintained visuals throughout.  The subject consumed the whole pack and dumped the empty box inside the washroom.  Moving ahead, subject shopped around, proceeded checkout and paid for all other items in the cart.  At approximately 1330 hrs, subject passed all points of purchase and exited the store.  The writer stopped subject at exit identifying himself as Loss Prevention Investigator for the store and informed about mischief to property.  The subject admitted his act and felt ashamed followed by asking writer to allow him to pay for the same. The writer agreed, subject paid for item and writer gave subject a verbal warning for repeating the similar act in future which could eventually turn into trespass from property (PPA).  MOD was notified of the same.  End of Report

At approximately 2:58 PM, while entering the store, the writer observed a mobile phone (believed to be an iPhone 11 or 12) in the lobby area near the entrance of Lawtons. The device was black or navy blue in colour. The writer immediately submitted the phone to the manager.  A few minutes later, a female customer standing at the cashier counter stated that she was looking for her mobile phone. The writer confirmed ownership by asking her to describe the phone case and wallpaper. She correctly described the sparkling phone case and provided additional identifying information. After verification, the writer returned the phone to her.  The female was described as a Black, average-built, middle-aged woman wearing black glasses, a pink T-shirt under a black jacket, and black dreadlocks. The writer requested identification; however, she did not have any ID with her. She then successfully completed her purchase using the same mobile device on the first attempt.  The personal information included in this report was verbally provided by the individual.

Incident Number:26-25920 Date of Incident: February 16, 2026 Location: Rockhead beer/wine/spirits at windsor st. Halifax  Reporting Party / Witness: writer / staff member (MATTHEW) team lead Time of Report: Approx. 1655- 1720 hours Narrative / Chronological Log: •  1650 hrs – Two black male customers entered the premises. 	•  Customer #1: Had valid government-issued ID. 	•  Customer #2: Did not have any ID. •  Staff refused service to both because of the establishment’s policy because if one person doesn’t have id they don’t serve the other Person who came together.  •  Customers became angry and started arguing loudly with one staff member . •  Witness (writer) was standing outside with Customer #2 (the one without ID) telling him about the rule and regulations  •  When I saw the argument getting heated inside, I entered inside to try to calm the situation and break up the verbal argument. •  Verbal argument quickly turned physical. Customers began fighting with the staff member. •  writer stepped in to separate the individuals and break up the fight. During this, one other person who doesn’t have id standing outside, suddenly came in jumped in and put a choke hold to the staff member , I was holding one individual at the moment who was the main culprit, and struck several times while trying to stop the brawl.  •  writer at the end intervened and physically removed both customers from the premises. Writer was with them outside because both the customer kept the heat up and telling them to fight outside.  •  Staff had already called police during the incident. 1720 hrs – Police officers arrived on scene. Officers spoke with staff, security, and gathered descriptions of both involved customers. Both customers had already left the area before or during police arrival. No arrests made on scene. Store managers came around 1800 to check and discuss the whole situation with us. Additional •  No serious injuries observed, staff member (MATTHEW) got gum bleeding.  No damage found inside. They took two can of beer worth $12  •security has video surveillance that may show the incident.  End of log.  16feb2026

(16:05 ) - While working at the Highland Square Mall on Westville road in New Glasgow, the Bluenotes Shop staff called me reporting there are 2 people heading toward the Charms Jewelers shop that have just taken an amount of clothing from Bluenotes without paying for them. I asked the staff person if she kept her eye on them as they shopped and is she sure they are carrying stolen merchandise? She said yes. Then I asked her why didn't she call police when they were leaving the shop area. She said she didn't know the protocol in that situation.  I then told her to call police to come and She gave me a description of 2 people. One is wearing a Formula 1 jacket and they are both wearing sweat pants carrying shopping bags. We then ended the call and I walked the eastern hall to look for the suspects.  I was unable to find a person wearing a Formula 1 Jacket but I stopped in front of the Stitches shop because there was a senior aged couple ( a male & female) with multiple shopping bags in the shop acting a bit suspicious The male had a big grey winter jacket on and the woman wore a pink winter jacket. Approx ( 16: 13 ) - Two New Glasgow police officers arrived from the East end doors of the Mall and walked toward  the Stitches shop (Officer Ironside #2027 and Constable Hayes # 2024, Occurrence # 2026278226)  I went to meet them and inform them I could not find a person with a formula 1 jacket any where. They continued to walk to Stitches located in the east hall, with me accompanying them,   as another two backup officers arrived at the front area of Stitches from the Center hall. Constable Hayes entered Stitches because he recognized the man in the grey jacket, calling by his name , and arrested him. Apparently the man was wearing the grey jacket over the formula 1 jacket. At approx. (16:19) - Constable , Hayes then calls the woman in pink out of the Stitches shop and questions her among the other police officers standing out front of the Shop. On February 16, 2026, at approximately 20:04 p.m., two individuals exited the liquor store without making payment for merchandise. One individual, approximately 6 feet tall, was carrying a Walmart bag and was wearing white shoes and a black jacket. This individual had placed approximately two to three bottles of liquor inside the Walmart bag and proceeded toward the exit. The writer stopped the individual at the exit area and followed him for approximately 100 meters outside the store. The individual ignored verbal communication and sat inside a grey Subaru vehicle parked nearby. The writer took photographs of the individual and the vehicle; however, the license plate was not visible. The writer verbally warned the individuals that police would be contacted and issued a verbal PPA. At the same time, a second individual, described as a white male wearing a black jacket and black shoes and carrying a Shoppers Drug Mart bag, exited the store from the beer shop side. The writer also took photographs of the vehicle. Another vehicle was observed positioned behind the grey Subaru and appeared to be following it. Store employees contacted the police immediately after the incident. Police arrived on scene at approximately 20:37 p.m., took photographs of the vehicle and the suspicious individuals, and gathered relevant information. The writer requested that employees review CCTV footage for clearer identification; however, staff advised that they did not have access to the camera system at that time. The merchandise involved included vodka bottles, Blue Lobster, and Alberta vodka, with an estimated total value between $1,200 and $1,500. Police provided file number 26-25982 in relation to this incident.The Cops came at the store at around 21.25 and recovered the items worth cost 476.39 but still looking for the another individual carrying shoppers drug mart bag.The merchandise cost is estimated as they didnt check the cameras .

On March 3, 2026, at approximately 1:30 PM, a male and female attempted to exit the store without paying for concealed merchandise.  Prior to 1:30 PM, I observed the pair in the meat section. The male was wearing a tan-colored hoodie, black shorts, and sneakers. The female was wearing a black jacket and black leggings.  I observed them select multiple meat packages and place them into a personal bag. Additional merchandise was placed into a shopping cart.  The pair proceeded to the self-checkout area where they scanned and paid only for the items inside the cart. The merchandise concealed in the bag was not scanned or paid for.  At approximately 1:30 PM, they attempted to exit the store.  I notified the self-checkout staff member immediately.I told the the staff member I can approach them after they exited the store but The staff member stopped the individuals before they exited the store. I approached, identified myself, and requested the unpaid merchandise. The individuals complied and returned the concealed items without resistance.  No physical confrontation occurred.

One white male noticed in the meat department having cart and Walmart bag in his hand . He was suspected of concealing approx 8 packs of meat in his bag . He then left his cart near to self checkout and went out with the meat stuff in his Walmart grocery bag from selfcheck out. I approached him and tried to took his bag . All the meat packs was on the floor but he aggressively picked all the packs from floor and went out from the store . He gave the stuff to the individual in the car parked Fenwick street . Both went away different directions. I called the cops and gave them description.  Description of suspect- white 6ft tall male wearing red hoodie with brown black and black cap  and red Mazda sedan car .  Suspect left the store approximately 1.55pm

At the beginning of my shift, front desk staff member Usama informed me about a suspicious male from the previous day who had been denied entry and had made gun gestures toward him. At approximately 7:45 PM, I observed a medium-build male, approximately 5'7", appearing Asian, wearing a blue hoodie, black cap, brown safety shoes, and carrying an army-colored shoulder bag entering the elevator and proceeding to the 3rd floor. Based on the description provided earlier, I suspected it could be the same individual. I proceeded to the 3rd floor and located the male sitting on a sofa in front of the elevator. I approached him politely and asked if he was a guest. He confirmed he was not a guest. I informed him that he was not permitted on the property and asked him to leave. He initially complied. While exiting through the front entrance, he asked me to open the door for him, pretending the door was not functioning properly. As I opened the door, he suddenly removed a lighter from his pocket and attempted to burn my beard. Due to his suspicious behavior, I was alert and reacted quickly, avoiding injury. I pushed him away from me, causing him to move outside the main entrance. He attempted to re-enter the building, but I immediately secured and locked the front entrance. I contacted HRM Police immediately as the individual remained outside, attempting to open the entrance door and disturbing hotel guests. He also attempted to open the door of a parked vehicle. Police arrived at approximately 7:55 PM and arrested the individual. They searched his pockets and bag and located a lighter, cigarettes, and a significant amount of cash. Police asked if I was injured; I confirmed I was not harmed as I avoided the attempted burning. Police issued him a PPA ticket and released him. After police departed, the individual waited outside briefly and again attempted to open the front entrance. Upon seeing me, he left the property while verbally abusing. The situation is now clear, and the individual has not been seen since.

At approximately 3:40 PM, the manager contacted security to respond to a suspicious individual near the pharmacy and bakery sections. Upon receiving the call, the writer proceeded to the bakery area to locate the individual but was unable to find him initially. Shortly after, the manager contacted security again and informed that the individual was observed in another deli section. The suspect was described as a male approximately 5 feet 5 inches tall, wearing a black hoodie and black pants. The individual was seen in possession of two packets of meat with an estimated total value of approximately $50. He then moved toward the pharmacy aisles and attempted to conceal additional merchandise. However, he was unsuccessful in stealing any further items. The manager approached the individual, and he handed over all unpaid merchandise back to the manager inside the store. After returning the items, the individual exited the store without further incident. No physical confrontation occurred, and all merchandise was successfully recovered.

At approximately 6:10 PM, while I was on my scheduled break, a male individual previously involved in an incident attended the store. He selected a bottle of wine and proceeded to the checkout area. At the register, he stated that the money transfer had not gone through and said he would return later. He then left the store without completing the purchase.  At approximately 7:00 PM, a store employee informed me of the incident and provided a description of the individual. I showed the employee the individual’s photo from the NLC WhatsApp group, and the employee confirmed that it was the same person.  I informed the employee that this individual is not permitted in the store as per prior management direction. The employee acknowledged this and advised that in the future, the individual will not be allowed to enter the store.  Security camera footage was reviewed, and it was confirmed that no merchandise was taken. No theft occurred during this incident.

Incident Report  Location: Sobeys Pictou Date: March 4, 2026 Time: 1555 hours Store Number: 232 LPO: Nijo Joy  ⸻  On March 4, 2026, at approximately 1555 hours, the writer was conducting regular patrol inside the store when two female individuals were observed near the front area.  The first female was described as wearing a red checkered shirt, grey pants, a black cap, and a slim build. The second female was wearing a black jacket, cream-colored pants, had reddish hair, and a slim build. Both individuals initially appeared to be engaged in normal shopping activities; however, one of the females drew the writer’s attention due to suspicious behavior and repeated glances toward the exit area.  As the individuals were positioned close to the exit, the writer secured an exit position to maintain observation. Shortly thereafter, the two females separated and walked in different directions. The writer continued surveillance on the female who had been acting suspiciously. Meanwhile, the second female proceeded toward the exit where one of the store managers was present.  The manager stopped the individuals near the exit and requested proof of purchase. The writer attended the scene immediately to provide support. Upon inspection, unpaid merchandise was identified and successfully recovered.  All stolen items were recovered in full. No police involvement was required as the merchandise was retrieved, and the situation was resolved without further incident.  Report completed by: Nijo Joy Loss Prevention Officer

On March 4, 2026, at approximately 15:54, a male individual entered the store wearing a yellow jacket. The individual was approximately 5’10” tall with a heavy build. He approached the cashier and asked for the location of computer mouse, then proceeded to the aisle where they were displayed. While the guard was patrolling the area, the individual was observed looking at the mouse products without taking any action. However, once the security guard passed the aisle, the individual removed the spider alarm from a mouse box and placed it in his pocket. Komal, who was the Manager on Duty (MOD) at the time, noticed the action and asked the individual to remove the wire from his pocket. The individual repeatedly denied having anything. When the security guard attempted to retrieve the mouse from his hands, the individual stated that he intended to pay for it. The group proceeded to the cash register area, where the individual requested to speak with a manager. Komal informed him that she was the manager on duty, but he refused to acknowledge this and stated that she was not the manager. The individual then left the store without paying. The mouse product was recovered; however, the spider alarm wire was taken by the individual. Cost of mouse was 150$.

Event: Theft Deterrence  Location: Sobeys 574 Queen St, 1120 Queen St, Halifax, NS B3H 2R9 Item: Gillette cartridge  Time: 1958 hrs Value: $120.82 (including taxes)  On 04/03/2026, writer Amandeep Singh Jaggi was scheduled to work as Loss Prevention Investigator at Sobeys 574 Queen St from 1730 hrs to 2200 hrs. While on duty as a Security Guard at the Highland Square Mall, New Glasgow,  I was informed by a customer in the Dollar Store front area of a male in the men's washroom ( Tall and slim build wearing a hoodie with a front hand pouch pocket). He was pulling out of the pouch pocket a long bottle and drinking it . While on my way to investigate at approx. (16:22) - The Shoe and Watch repair shop owner approaches me in the center floor area of the Mall and says a man is drinking in the men's washroom.  I go to the men's washroom and  enter cautiously and see a man ( Caucasian, about 5'7" wearing a brown jacket over a black hoodie top, dark pants, black sneackers,  and a black ball cap),  standing in front of the urinals by the right corner of the room and leaning against the baby change counter. He is mumbling and talking to himself with his eyes closed and being incoherent to my greetings and questions of how he is doing.  Approx. at (16:23) - I call the New Glasgow police and report there is an intoxicated man in the men's washroom of the Highland Square Mall. The Dispatcher takes my name and at (16:25), tells me that a police unit is on its way there.  (16:35) - Police arrive at the washroom (Sargeant Paris and Constable Fulton #240). Sargeant Paris tries to talk to the man and get him to show some ID. I mention to him that the man possibly has a long bottle of alcohol in his pouch pocket of his hoodie top.   (16:37) - Sargeant Paris inspects the pouch pocket and took out a bottle of liquor and puts it on the sink counter. ( 16:40) - As Sargeant continues to question the man for his ID  he starts to show aggression  and causes a struggle with Sargeant Paris. As a result, the man ended up losing his balance and stumbled to the floor to a laying position while being Sargeant Paris tries to hang on to him to break his fall. He continued to resist the instructions of Sargeant Paris to stop resisting or he will be hand cuffed.  The man continued to squirm and yell out seeming obscenities towards the officers  so he was then man handled by the two officers and lifted up to stand on his feet.   (16:40) - The officers then man handled the man to press him face forward,  up against the wall beside the front of the sink counter where they then applied hand cuffs.  Sargeant Paris then leaves Constable Fulton in the custody of the man with me standing near by while he goes out to the hallway to talk with Dispatch on his radio : to find info on the man in custody  (16:44) - The man begins to resist Constible Fulton and in the struggle the man stumbles sideways and falls on his side on the floor, overturning a garbage can on his way down helping to break his fall.  Constable Fulton also tries to break his fall by trying to hold to him as he falls to the floor . The man then stays laying on the floor and looks uninjured.  (16:50) Sargeant Paris then reenters the washroom asking what just happened. We give him a summary of the event noting that the man did not injured himself on the fall. Sargeant Paris then tells us of some info that he had received from Dispatch concerning the man. That he just was released from the Hospital with some serious medical conditions related to his alcohol abuse. He was also a new resident in the area and has connections with Violas Place personal. They were sending a woman to meet him at the hospital when he is transported there.  The ambulance has also been called and was on its way to pick him up.  Sargeant Paris then asked me me to stay with Constable Fulton while he goes to the Mall's main entrance and wait to receive the paramedics arriving in the ambulance. I said yes. The man on the floor fell asleep while we waited for the paramedics, waking up occasionally and mumbling.  Approx at (17:03) Constable Fulton received a message from Sargeant Paris that the ambulance ETA was 4 minutes...  (17:13) - Two paramedics arrive at the washroom pushing a gurney.  ( 17:15) - the man is woken up by the paramedics and the police tell him they are taking off his cuffs. The officers assist the man as he moves to go lay down on the gurney.  ( 17:16.) The man lays down on the gurney almost unassisted and the paramedics begin to medically check him over. The man is mostly compliant with some complaining and gestures of annoyance but he allows the paramedics to do their work... Approx. ( 17:19) - the paramedics and police leave with the man laying in the gurney and he is wheeled out to the ambulance. Incident report is pending. End of report.

At approximately 15:50 hours, the writer observed a suspicious male inside the store.  The suspect is described as a male wearing a black jacket and pushing a shopping cart with a blue Walmart reusable bag placed on top.  The suspect proceeded through the self-checkout area and paid for grocery items totaling $10.95. However, he failed to scan and pay for one (1) large pack of eggs and one (1) apple, with a combined value of $11.81.  The writer approached the suspect outside the store, identified himself as a Loss Prevention Officer, and advised him that he did not scan the eggs. The writer also asked if he had any additional unpaid items. The suspect complied and returned the apple and eggs.  A verbal warning was issued.  All merchandise was recovered.  Nothing further to add. This report is for information purposes only.

osition: LPO (Loss Prevention Officer) Store: Sobeys Westside Date of Incident: March 6, 2026 Time of Incident: Approximately 7:22 PM Incident Description: While conducting routine patrol inside the store at approximately 7:22 PM, I observed a suspicious female individual wearing all black clothing pushing a cart loaded with merchandise toward the exit. The individual proceeded through the checkout area without attempting to pay for the items and exited into the vestibule area. I approached and stopped the female suspect immediately after she exited the vestibule with the cart. At that moment, a male individual accompanying her approached me. The male suspect was wearing a grey hoodie, black cap, blue jeans, and brown shoes. He quickly took control of the cart and stated that he intended to pay for the merchandise, claiming he needed to retrieve a card from his vehicle. The male suspect then proceeded toward the checkout area and appeared to attempt payment but stated that he had the wrong card. During this time, I observed that the female suspect had already gone to the parking lot and was waiting inside a vehicle. The vehicle was identified as a white Buick Verano sedan with license plate GFB-337. The male suspect returned to the vehicle, entered it, and both suspects immediately fled the parking lot. The total value of the merchandise in the cart was $314.34. All merchandise involved in the incident was successfully recovered . Actions Taken: 	•	The Manager on Duty was immediately informed of the incident. 	•	Police were contacted right away. 	•	Sergeant D. Paris (Badge #271) attended the location shortly afterward. 	•	At the time of the officer's arrival, there was no staff available with access to the CCTV system. The officer advised that he will return tomorrow to collect CCTV footage and complete the report. Police Report Number: 2026299004 Clear images of the suspects will be provided once CCTV footage is accessed. Identification of the suspects will be confirmed once police review the CCTV footage. Reported By: Vishal Mangvana Loss Prevention Officer (LPO) Sobeys Westside

Incident Report  On March 6, 2026, at approximately 8:35 PM,the writer was on routine patrol near the checkout area of the store. While observing the checkout line, the writer noticed a female customer and her friend waiting to pay for their items. The writer observed that the friend paid for two sodas and bananas, while the remaining merchandise was already placed inside a bag and was not presented for payment.  The writer continued observation and noticed that the female attempted to exit the store with the unpaid merchandise As she approached the exit, the writer stopped and confronted her, asking her to return the items. The female stated that she had already paid for the items; however, the writer was certain that the merchandise had not been paid for.  The writer then recovered the bag and requested that she return inside the store. The writer brought the bag to the checkout and asked the cashier to verify whether the items had been paid for. The employee confirmed that the merchandise had not been purchased.  The writer asked the female to provide a receipt for the items, but she stated that she did not understand English,At that time, the writer issued a verbal PPA and instructed her to leave the store  The writer asked the store staff to provide a void receipt for documentation purposes. All merchandise was recovered in sellable condition, with an approximate value of $30.

Date: 03/06/2026  Location: JAG HOTEL   Security Guard: Alisher At approximately 11:20 PM, the bartender informed me that a male individual had become intoxicated and aggressive toward the bartender and staff, and had been arguing with them. The individual then left the bar area and went outside with his friends.  The bartender and the night supervisor advised me that the individual was not permitted to return inside the bar or restaurant, and asked me to monitor the entrance.  I remained at the entrance for several minutes. When the individual returned with a group of friends, they stated that they had someone inside and wanted to go back in. I informed them that I could not allow them inside at that time and advised them that I would check with the supervisor.  I then went to the front desk and brought the supervisor to the entrance. The supervisor spoke with the group and informed them that the individual was not allowed to return to the bar or restaurant, but if he was a hotel guest, he could return to his room.  During the conversation, the individual became aggressive toward the supervisor and attempted to move toward him. At that point, I intervened and attempted to escort the individual out of the building. The individual then became physically aggressive toward me, and a brief physical confrontation occurred.  I was able to push the individual away and escort him out of the building. After he was removed from the premises, the situation ended and no further incidents occurred.

At approximately 1745 hrs, LPO noticed a male without cart near meat department. LPO followed the male and noticed the male was taking chicken thighs packs one by one without looking the price. He took 4 packs of chicken thighs and 5 packs of blueberry sausages. The male went towards natural source area and started packing all items in the bag. LPO noticed the male putting the items in the bag. LPO followed the male without loosing sight as the male left from self checkout at approximately 1748 hrs without paying for the items. LPO approached the male outside the exit, however the male did not respond and ran away Willet street towards McDonald’s side. LPO verbally banned the male from the property. LPO came back to the store, received approximate price of the stolen items from meat department manager Jeremy and made an incident report. The incident has been reported to Sobeys non emergency, manager Krista, manager Branon and Halifax non emergency police - 26-35429 . Nothing further to report.   The description of the male includes,  Caucasian male, clean shaven, medium build, height approximately 5’7, in his 30s, blue cap, grey hoodie, black under armour jacket, black pants, black shoes.
fields.merchandise_description: 4 packs of chicken thighs big pack, 5 packs of blueberry sausages

fields.summary: Incident Report  Location: Sobeys Stellarton Date: March 5, 2025 Time: 2005 hours Store Number: 100 LPO Name: Nijo Joy  Writer was conducting regular patrol inside the store when writer identified a female known for previous theft incidents. Due to her history, writer immediately began observing and following her movements inside the store.  Suspect Description: Female wearing a green cap, glasses (spex), green and white hoodie, blue jeans pants, black shoes, and slim built.  The female selected multiple items throughout the store and filled her cart with various merchandise. While the writer was maintaining observation, the female noticed the writer and became aware of the surveillance. Writer then positioned near the exit area to maintain presence and prevent a potential theft.  The female continued to move around the store for some time, appearing to wait until the writer left the area so she could exit quickly. However, the writer remained near the exit and maintained observation.  After realizing the writer was still present, the female eventually left the store without attempting to exit with merchandise and without making any purchase. No theft occurred.  Writer’s presence prevented an estimated $500 worth of merchandise from being stolen.

Event: Recovery & Trespass from property  Location: Sobeys 574 Queen St, 1120 Queen St, Halifax, NS B3H 2R9 Item: Coffee  Time: 2108 hrs Value: $14.99 (excluding taxes)  On 05/03/2026, writer Amandeep Singh Jaggi was scheduled to work as Loss Prevention Investigator at Sobeys 574 Queen St from 1500 hrs to 2200 hrs.  At approximately 2050 hrs, while writer was patrolling the store, writer observed a male entering the store. The subject selected above mentioned item and cautiously observed his surroundings.  The writer witnessed the subject concealing the subject inside his jacket and moving to other aisle.  The subject selected another item and brought it to checkout, dropped the item citing card issues and proceeded towards exit without trying to pay for concealed item.  At approximately 2108 hrs, writer stopped subject at exit identifying himself as Loss Prevention Investigator for the store and asked for items back.  The subject complied and voluntarily returned the item while apologising for the same. The writer trespassed the subject for 1 month (PPA) verbally while leaving.  MOD was notified of the same.  End of Report

Around at 2155H, a white male entered in the store. He was wearing a red winter cap, black zipper up, dark blue jeans, white and brown shoes. He was around 5’5” tall, around 40 years old. 

Around 2156H the male took the basket and start selecting the merchandise. The male selected two drinks, chips, chocolate and some other merchandise and put all the items in the basket.

Around 2204h, the male left the store without paying and full basket of merchandise in hand. The approximately value of the merchandise was $80.
I approached the suspect outside the store but the male didn’t stop. He left the spot and went towards the Macdonalds.

One of the store employee call the police nad filled the police report.
